skill_nodes.description_text: Abyssal Echoes deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Abyssal Echoes' initial burst shreds enemy armor.
Armor shred applied by Abyssal Decay has increased effect.
Abyssal Echoes no longer has a cooldown, but costs more mana.
Abyssal Echoes costs less mana.
Directly using Abyssal Echoes has a chance to cast a {Void Rift} from each nearby {Devouring Orb}.
Abyssal Echoes can now be cast at the target location.
Abyssal Decay has increased duration.
Abyssal Decay now blinds enemies.
Abyssal Decay causes enemies to have reduced physical resistance, but it is no longer consumed when the enemy is hit.
Abyssal Decay causes enemies to take increased damage over time.

Abyssal Decay now deals damage when the enemy is hit, rather than over time. Each hit deals a portion of the full damage Abyssal Decay would have dealt over its full duration. This damage still counts as damage over time.

Directly using Abyssal Echoes grants you {Nether Coating} for 5 seconds, granting you increased attack speed with your melee and throwing attacks and causing you to deal more damage to blinded enemies.
{Nether Coating} also grants you a chance to inflict {Time Rot} on hit.
Abyssal Echoes' initial burst consumes all stacks of bleed on the enemy, causing their damage to be dealt instantly.
Abyssal Echoes' initial burst slows enemies.
Abyssal Decay causes Enemies that would apply {Slow} to instead grant their target {Haste} for 1 second.
After casting Abyssal Echoes your next {Erasing Strike} and {Void Cleave} deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to slowed enemies.
Abyssal Echoes affects a larger area.
Abyssal Echoes' initial burst has a chance to ignite enemies.
Abyssal Echoes' initial burst now hits and deals spell void damage, but no longer inflicts Abyssal Decay.

Spell void damage from {Potent Corruption}, {Vorpal Reverberation} and attribute scaling is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to void damage.

Abyssal Echoes' initial burst now also heals you and your allies and nodes on this tree that grant more damage also grant more healing.
Abyssal Echoes' damage now scales with attunement rather than vitality, and its healing effectiveness is also increased by attunement.
Abyssal Echoes deals significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but no longer chains.
Abyssal Decay now spreads to several nearby enemies after 1 second.
Abyssal Decay spreads to enemies further away and can spread to more enemies when it spreads.
Abyssal Echoes now creates an {Abyssal Rift} at the target location that casts Abyssal Echoes for you 3 times at its location before expiring.
{Abyssal Rifts} cast additional Abyssal Echoes.

Abyssal Echoes can chain additional times. 

This effect is tripled for echoed casts.
Echoed casts deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Acid Flask deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Acid Flask has a chance to grant {Haste} on use for a short duration.
Acid Flask has a chance to shred enemy armor.
You have a chance to poison enemies while standing in your {Poison Pools}.
{Poison Pools} last longer.
Acid Flask has a chance to shred enemy poison resistance on hit.
Acid Flask has additional chance to poison enemies on hit.
Acid Flask has a chance to apply {Frailty} to enemies hit, causing them to deal less damage.
Acid Flask has increased poison duration.
Acid Flask has increased throwing speed.

You have a chance to drop an Acid Flask at your feet when an enemy hits you. 

This effect has a 5 second cooldown.
Acid Flask costs less mana and deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Acid Flask has a chance to slow enemies on hit. Slow caused by Acid Flask lasts longer.
Acid Flask deals additional throwing fire damage.
Acid Flask has a chance to ignite enemies.
Acid Flask has increased critical strike chance and critical strikes deal more damage.
Acid Flask deals damage in a larger area.
Acid Flask travels faster.
{Poison Pools} have a chance to inflict enemies with {Efficacious Toxin} each second, causing them to take increased damage over time.

When you directly use Acid Flask, you now throw 3 Acid Flasks in a line, but Acid Flask costs more mana.

Enemies can only be hit by one flask per throw.
When you directly use Acid Flask, you now throw Acid Flasks in a fan in front of you instead of a line. Flasks travel faster.

Acid Flask leaves behind a {Poison Pool} for 4 seconds that poison enemies that stand in it and adds a 2 second cooldown per flask thrown.

You can have a maximum of 4 {Poison Pools} active at once.
Acid Flask penetrates poison resistance based on your own uncapped Poison Resistance

Acid Flask's base damage is converted to fire damage and its hits deal significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers). Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to physical damage.

Acid Flask can no longer apply poison or create poison pools.
Converts Armor Shred from all sources that Acid Flask would apply to Fire Resistance Shred.
When Acid Flask is thrown directly it causes 3 smaller explosions around the impact location dealing fire damage on hit, but it costs more mana.
Acid Flask has a chance to blind enemies hit for 4 seconds.

Acid Flask is now thrown as a trap that starts arming and explodes when enemies walk over it.

You can have a maximum of 6 Acid Flask Traps active at once.
Aerial Assault deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has a shorter cooldown.
After the initial {Wing Burst} your Falcon continues releasing {Featherstorm} at the target location for 5 seconds.

{Featherstorm} now has a chance to slow enemies inside its area each second. 

Additionally, you and your allies have additional dodge rating while inside the area.
If there is a boss or rare enemy nearby, Aerial Assault now also creates a {Shadow Falcon} to use {Featherstorm} against it.

Aerial Assault no longer moves you. Additionally, it deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and it no longer has a minimum range.

Aerial Assault loses its {Traversal} tag.
{Featherstorm} lasts longer and deals more direct damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per second it has been active.
{Featherstorm} now has a chance each second to drop a {Black Arrow}.

{Featherstorms'} base physical damage over time is converted to poison. Consequently this damage scales with increases to poison damage, but not increases to physical damage.

A portion of your global poison chance on hit now applies as poison chance per second for {Featherstorm}.

Swaps {Featherstorms'} {Physical} tag for a {Poison} tag.
A portion of Aerial Assault's cooldown is recovered when your Falcon hits an enemy. This effect can occur a limited number of times per 3 seconds.
Using Aerial Assault now also drops {Caltrops} at your location.
{Caltrops} from all sources have a chance to slow on hit and deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
You now drop additional {Caltrops} halfway through Aerial Assault's movement if you traveled at least 6 meters.

{Caltrops'} hits from all sources have a chance to cause enemies to bleed. 

Additionally, when an enemy walks over your {Caltrops} your Falcon's hits gain additional bleed chance scaling with your Dexterity for 4 seconds. This effect can occur a limited number of times per 4 seconds.
Directly using Aerial Assault recovers a portion of your missing mana.
Directly using Aerial Assault recovers a portion of the remaining cooldown of {Falconry} and {Dive Bomb} per 10 maximum mana you have.
Directly using Aerial Assault recovers a portion of your missing health.
Convert the health gain from the {Healing Gust} node to ward at half the effect.

For the next 8 seconds after using Aerial Assault, critical strikes dealt by you or your {Falcon} grant you a stack of {Aerial Prowess} up to a maximum.

When you next use Aerial Assault, it consumes all stacks to restore health per stack and deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per stack.

You have more global dodge rating (multiplicative with other modifiers) while traveling with Aerial Assault.

Additionally, each hit you dodge while traveling grants you a stack of {Aerial Prowess}.
Aerial Assault now has a chance to grant you {Aerial Prowess} stacks on non-critical strikes and when you kill an enemy.
Directly using {Ballista,} {Explosive Trap}, or {Dive Bomb} now also consumes your stacks of {Aerial Prowess}, applying the damage bonus to the ability that consumed them.
The {Wing Burst's} hits apply frailty.
After landing, all your active {Ballistas} prioritize targets around you, deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per Intelligence, and gain increased attack speed.
The {Wing Burst} deals damage in a larger area.
The {Wing Burst} deals more direct damage to enemies on high health.
The damage bonus from {Ambush} always applies against bosses and rare enemies, regardless of their health.
When the {Wing Burst} kills an enemy and you have 3 or fewer active {Shadows}, it creates a {Shadow} at that enemy's location.
A portion of your added, increased, and more throwing damage modifiers now apply to the {Wing Burst}.
The {Wing Burst} now also includes a few {Umbral Blades} that your Falcon throws for you.
The {Umbral Blades'} hits from the {Twilight Strike} node now shred enemy armor.
Anomaly hits enemies in a larger area.
Anomaly lasts longer.
Anomaly is cast instantly, but Anomaly has a longer cooldown.
{Time Wave} deals more hit damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area, and increases to Anomaly's area also affect Time Wave.
An additional {Time Wave} is released when you cast Anomaly.
Anomaly has a shorter cooldown.
{Future Strikes} applied by Anomaly have additional Void Penetration
When enemies return to the present, all damaging ailments currently applied to them apply faster.
When enemies return to the present, the duration of damaging ailments applied to them is reset.
Enemies hit by Anomaly are afflicted with {Time Rot} when they return.
{Time Rot} inflicted by Anomaly lasts longer and has additional Void Penetration
When enemies return to the present, they take increased void damage for a duration.
Anomaly now applies Ignite instead of Time Rot. Modifiers to Time Rot apply to Ignite instead.
Anomaly applies additional stacks of ignite.
{Time Bubble} grants you and your allies increased critical strike chance while in its area.
{Time Bubble} lasts longer.
{Time Bubble} is now cast on yourself and moves with you, but Anomaly costs more mana.
{Time Bubble} grants you and your allies increased attack and cast speed while in its area.
{Time Bubble} grants you and your allies increased cooldown recovery speed while in its area.
{Time Bubble} grants you and your allies global health leech. This is doubled for void damage.
{Time Bubble} grants you and your allies increased health leech while in its area.

When enemies return to the present they are afflicted by {Time Lock,} rendering them unable to move, attack, or cast spells for 2 seconds.

Anomaly costs more mana.

10% of remaining cooldown recovered whenever you {Time Lock} an enemy with Anomaly. This effect can occur a maximum of two times each second.
{Time Lock} lasts longer.
When enemies return to the present, enemies within the area of Anomaly's original cast are also time locked for 2 seconds.
When allies or enemies exit {Time Bubble's} area of effect, effects caused or granted by Time Bubble persist for 2 seconds.
When enemies return to the present a {Time Wave} is released, a spell dealing void damage to nearby enemies.
{Future Strike} is applied to all enemies hit by Anomaly.
Anomaly leaves behind a {Time Bubble} for 5 seconds, which slows enemies and shreds their void resistance.
Anomaly no longer sends enemies foward in time. Effects that would be triggered when enemies return to the present are instead triggered immediately.
Arcane Ascendance grants you increased spell damage while active.
While Arcane Ascendance is active, each hit you take increases your spell damage and cast speed, but drains your mana.
You are granted increased spell damage each second while Arcane Ascendance is active.
You are granted increased cast speed each second while Arcane Ascendance is active.
While Arcane Ascendance is active, spells and attacks have a chance to slow enemies over 5 meters away and you deal more damage to enemies per stack of slow on them.
While Arcane Ascendance is active, you are granted mana whenever you kill an enemy that is over 5 meters away.
You are granted {Haste} for a duration after leaving Arcane Ascendance. The duration is capped at 200% of the time you spent in Arcane Ascendance.
Arcane Ascendance has a shorter cooldown.
You gain ward when you activate Arcane Ascendance.
While Arcane Ascendance is active, your spells and attacks that can freeze enemies have a higher chance to do so.
You knock back all nearby enemies when you enter Arcane Ascendance.
Lightning Blast cast by {Superconductor} triggers more frequently.
Arcane Ascendance drains less mana per second.
All of your spells cost less mana while Arcane Ascendance is active, but you cannot regenerate mana while Arcane Ascendance is active.
You can cast more spells via {Acuity}, but Arcane Ascendance has a longer cooldown.
While Arcane Ascendance is active, whenever you kill a frozen enemy a nearby enemy is frozen for a duration.
While Arcane Ascendance is active, spells and attacks have a chance to chill enemies over 5 meters away.
While Arcane Ascendance is active, spells and attacks have a chance to shock enemies over 5 meters away.
{Mark Of Thunder} deals more damage when it is consumed (multiplicative with other modifiers).

While Arcane Ascendance is active, you automatically cast {Lightning Blast} when you hit an enemy. Lightning Blast uses its skill tree and you lose mana equal to Lightning Blast's cost. This effect has a 2 second cooldown.
Arcane Ascendance now has a 2 second cooldown and no longer drains your mana. You can cast 4 spells before Arcane Ascendance ends automatically. Lasts 12 seconds.
While Arcane Ascendance is active, you {Mark} enemies you hit that are over 5 meters away. When you exit Arcane Ascendance, you consume all marks, dealing lightning damage to affected enemies.
You regain mana while you enter Arcane Ascendance, but the mana is lost when you exit Arcane Ascendance.
Hold ability key to channel to increase the area. When you release, combine all of your minions in the area into a single hulking abomination with states based on which minions are consumed.

For each {Skeleton Warrior} absorbed, your abomination deals more melee damage and has more health (both multiplicative with other modifiers).

For each {Skeleton Rogue} absorbed, your abomination has increased attack speed.

For each {Skeleton Archer} absorbed, your abomination has increased area of effect.

Effects such as this which apply per skeleton absorbed can only consider up to 20 skeletons in total.

Your abomination has additional critical strike chance.

For each {Skeleton Rogue} absorbed, your abomination has increased movement speed.

Effects such as this which apply per skeleton absorbed can only consider up to 20 skeletons in total.
Your abomination has increased critical strike chance, but can no longer regenerate health or absorb {Bone Golems}.
Your abomination's melee hits deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but can no longer absorb {Skeletal Mages}.
Your abomination has more health (multiplicative with other modifiers) and generates more threat, which increases the likelihood of enemies targeting it.
Your abomination reflects a percentage of damage to attackers, and disassembles into a number of {Skeletal Vanguards} on death.
Your abomination deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Your abomination deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but has reduced attack speed.
Your abomination's hits leech a portion of the damage they deal as health.
Your abomination's melee attacks deal damage in a larger area.
Your abomination gains increased attack speed when it kills an enemy. This effect can occur a limited number of times per 5 seconds.
Your abomination has more health (multiplicative with other modifiers) and restores a larger percentage of its maximum health when devouring other minions.
Your abomination gains more max health (multiplicative with other modifiers) for 8 seconds each time it devours a minion, up to a limited number of times per 8 seconds.

{Eternal Arrow} has a reduced cooldown.

For each {Skeleton Archer} absorbed, Eternal Arrow has additional Recurve chance.

Effects such as this which apply per skeleton absorbed can only consider up to 20 skeletons in total.

Your abomination has more health and deals more damage (both multiplicative with other modifiers) for each type of minion absorbed to create it among {Summoned Skeleton}, {Skeletal Mage}, {Wraith}, and {Bone Golem}.

If the abomination was made by absorbing at least 1 of every type of minion mentioned by {Engorgement}, it gains more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), increased attack speed, and increased and movement speed.

{Devouring Souls} has a reduced cooldown.

Devouring Souls deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) for each {Skeletal Mage} absorbed.

Devouring Souls releases an additional soul for each Skeletal Mage absorbed.
Your abomination has increased cooldown recovery speed for its abilities, and deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) with abilities that have cooldowns.

{Stomp} has a reduced cooldown.

For each {Bone Golem} absorbed, Stomp deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has increased area of effect.

If a {Skeleton Warrior} or {Skeleton Rogue} is absorbed, your abomination is granted the ability to {Double Strike} with a 6 second cooldown, dealing significant melee damage.

Double Strike deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per Skeleton Warrior or Rogue consumed.

Effects such as this which apply per skeleton absorbed can only consider up to 20 skeletons in total.
If a {Skeletal Mage} is absorbed, your abomination can cast {Devouring Souls}, a spell that releases multiple necrotic projectiles.
Your abomination has increased cooldown recovery speed for its abilities, but takes damage equal to a percentage of its maximum health each time it uses an ability with a cooldown.

If a {Wraith} is absorbed, its melee attack is replaced with {Soul Reave}, which deals melee necrotic damage.

Soul Reave has 200% damage effectiveness compared to the basic melee attack's 150%, and Soul Reave has 15% more radius compared to the basic melee attack.
If a {Bone Golem} is absorbed, your abomination uses {Stomp}, a melee attack that deals physical damage and has a high chance to stun enemies in a large area.

If a {Skeletal Archer} is absorbed, your abomination casts {Eternal Arrow} every 4 seconds, a bow ability that deals physical damage and has 100% chance to Recurve.

Your abomination gains additional bow damage per point of your Intelligence.
If a {Volatile Zombie} is absorbed, your abomination gains an explosive {Tail Slam} ability.

Your abomination can no longer absorb {Summoned Skeletons}.

Minions within 12 meters of your abomination gain increased attack speed.

Your abomination casts {Aura of Rot}, poisoning itself and nearby enemies every second.

For each {Skeleton Rogue} absorbed, your abomination gains additional chance to inflict poison on hit.

Effects such as this which apply per skeleton absorbed can only consider up to 20 skeletons in total.
Your abomination will not devour {Volatile Zombies}, even if they were absorbed to create it.
When your abomination would devour a minion, it will instead be {Sacrificed}, consuming a percentage of Sacrifice's mana cost. This Sacrifice is cast by you and uses your Sacrifice tree.
Aura of Decay applies poison and other ailments to enemies more frequently.
While Aura of Decay is active, you have a chance when hit to cast {Freezing Blast}. This effect has a 4 second cooldown.
Aura of Decay applies Poison and other ailments to enemies more frequently, but drains mana each second.
Poison and other ailments inflicted by Aura of Decay last longer. This does not include ailments that slow.
Aura of Decay has a chance to shred the armor of nearby enemies every second. A percentage of your chance to shred armor on hit also contributes to this chance per second.
Aura of Decay has a chance to slow nearby enemies every second.
Enemies are inflicted with multiple stacks of poison when they hit you, but you also gain poison stacks when hit.
Allies within range are healed by Aura of Decay each second. Aura of Decay heals more health the less health you currently have.
Minions within Aura of Decay’s area of effect deal more poison damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
When you lose a stack of {Fester} or gain a stack while already at maximum stacks, you fire a {Poison Bolt} at a nearby enemy.
You take less poison damage while Aura of Decay is active (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Enemies within Aura of Decay’s area of effect have less Poison Resistance.
You and Enemies within Aura of Decay’s area of effect have less poison resistance for each point of intelligence you have.
{Poison Nova} can occur an additional number of times per 3 seconds.
Aura of Decay has a chance to inflict nearby enemies with {Plague} each second, and Aura of Decay deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Aura of Decay affects a larger area.
Aura of Decay has a chance to chill nearby enemies every second.
You have additional poison resistance while in Aura of Decay and you have increased health regeneration if you have deactivated Aura of Decay recently (past 4 seconds).

Aura of Decay is converted to cold, inflicting frostbite instead of poison. Your chance to inflict frostbite applies to Aura of Decay at 100% effectiveness. 

All of its effects related to poison and poison resistance are converted to frostbite and cold resistance. Effects from Aura of Decay which granted less poison damage taken instead grant less cold damage over time taken.

Swaps Aura of Decay's {Poison} tag for a {Cold} tag.
Aura of Decay applies poison and other ailments to enemies more frequently, but also applies poison to yourself more frequently.

Aura of Decay is converted to physical, inflicting bleed instead of poison. Your chance to inflict bleed applies to Aura of Decay at 75% effectiveness. 

All of its effects related to poison and poison resistance are converted to bleed and physical resistance. Effects from Aura of Decay which granted less poison damage taken instead grant less physical damage over time taken.

Swaps Aura of Decay's {Poison} tag for a {Physical} tag.
While Aura of Decay is active, when using a traversal skill or {Evade} you leave behind a {Putrid Bomb}. Putrid Bombs explode after a 1 second delay and Poison all enemies they hit.
Minions within Aura of Decay’s area of effect have a chance to poison on hit, but are poisoned every second.

While Aura of Decay is active and you are stationary, you gain two stacks of {Fester} per second, up to a maximum. You lose two stacks per second while moving.

{Fester} grants your active Aura of Decay more damage per stack (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Enemies that die within Aura of Decay’s area of effect have a chance to emit a {Poison Nova}, which poisons all enemies it hits. This effect can occur a limited number of times per 3 seconds.

Aura of Decay consumes mana every 15 seconds to emit a powerful {Defiling Nova}, as long as there is a boss or rare enemy nearby.

This effect occurs with increased frequency based on your uncapped poison resistance.
You gain {Fester} stacks faster while channeling.
{Fester}'s maximum stacks is increased, and the rate at which you lose stacks while moving is reduced.
You have a chance when you would lose a stack of {Fester} to instead gain a stack. Additionally, you lose Fester stacks at an increased rate while moving.
Avalanche costs less mana.
Avalance now persists after you stop channeling for a duration equal to how long you spent channeling, up to a maximum.
Avalanche deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Avalanche hits have an increased chance to stun enemies.

Additionally, the impact from the large boulders have a chance to create your {Upheaval} towards a nearby enemy.

This consumes mana equal to a portion of {Upheaval's} mana cost.

Avalanche costs less mana.

If it's channeled l it will reduce the channel cost instead.
Avalanche hits deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) while you have at least one active totem.
Small boulders have a chance to be replaced by large boulders.

Avalanche drops additional small boulders.

If Avalanche is channeled, small boulders drop more frequently instead.

Improves Avalanche's base critical strike chance.

When a large boulder critically strikes, it has a chance to drop a small boulder at the same location.
Avalanche hits have an increased chance to freeze enemies.
Avalanche's large boulders have a chance to leave behind {Frozen Ground,} which chills all enemies who stand on it.

{Frozen Ground} affects a larger area.

You and your allies standing on your {Frozen Ground} gain a chance to inflict frostbite on hit.

Avalanche hits deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

This effect is doubled against frozen enemies.
Avalanche boulders fall in a larger area, and each boulder has a larger area of impact.
Avalanche boulders have a larger area of impact.
Boulders fall in a larger area. This reduces your chance to hit single targets.
Avalanche is now channeled, creating 1 large boulder and 5 small boulders every second, but has a channeling cost every second.
Small boulders now fall in a smaller area up to a maximum, causing them to hit more precisely at the target location.

The persisting Avalanche is now cast around you and follows your movement.

The large boulders will automatically target enemies within 6 meters.
Avalanche's base cold damage is converted to physical damage, and freeze rate multiplier bonuses from this tree are converted to increased stun chance.
While in range of a {Storm Totem}, Avalanche hits deal additional lightning damage.

Large boulders will cause a {Fissure} to open underneath them, dealing physical damage over 4 seconds to enemies who stand on it.

You can have a maximum of 1 {Fissure} at a time.
Avalanche's base physical damage is converted to cold damage, and increased stun chance provided by this tree is converted to freeze rate multiplier.

Avalanche's every third large boulder creates an {Earthquake} on impact.

This effect consumes mana equal to a portion of {Earthquake's} mana cost.
Boulder hits have a chance to grant you a {Storm Stack}.

Large boulders have a chance to form into a {Coldstone Elemental}, which is a melee minion that deals cold and physical damage and decays over time.

You can summon up to 9 {Coldstone Elementals}, and summoning past this maximum will refresh the decay and health of the most decayed one.
When a {Coldstone Elemental} is hit by a boulder, they regain health and gain {Frenzy} and {Haste} for a short duration.
Summons a ballista that fires fire arrows at nearby enemies.
Ballista costs significantly less mana, but has a shorter duration.
Ballista has a chance to shoot extra projectiles.
Ballista has more attack speed (multiplicative with other modifiers), but deals less damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Ballista has a chance to shred enemy armor.
Ballista has a chance to fire twice in quick succession.
Ballista bolts are lit on fire dealing additional fire damage and have a chance to ignite enemies.
Ballista has increased attack speed.
Ballista's bolts now explode on hit dealing fire damage in an area, but has less attack speed (multiplicative with other modifiers).

The explosion's base fire damage is converted to cold. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not increases to fire damage. The explosion also has a chance to freeze enemies.

Ignite chance from inside this skill tree is converted to chill chance.
Ballista has more health and lasts longer.
Ballista has a chance to slow enemies.
Your ailment modifiers also apply to your Ballista, but at a reduced ratio.
Ballista gains increased attack speed with each hit.
Your Ballistae gain {Frenzy} while you are standing near them.
Your damage modifiers also apply to your Ballista, but at a reduced ratio.
Ballista deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but has less attack speed (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Ballista shields are wider.
Improves Ballista's base critical strike chance.
Your critical strike chance and critical strike multipliers also apply to your Ballista, but at a reduced ratio.
When you construct a second ballista a tripwire is created between it and the existing ballista. Enemies that cross the tripwire cause both ballistae to explode.
Ballista deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) if constructed within 4 seconds of using a potion.
Ballista deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has more attack speed (multiplicative with other modifiers), but has a shorter duration.
Ballista now also explodes when it expires or is destroyed. This explosion deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and hits enemies in a larger area based on your Dexterity.
Every 5 seconds Ballista shoots a {Harpoon Shot}, that sticks into an enemy and pulls them towards the Ballista.

Hitting your Ballista with {Acid Flask} gives it a chance to poison enemies and more poison damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

If {Acid Flask} damage is converted to fire, this is instead ignite chance and more fire damage.
Ballistae which have hit an enemy have a chance to drop a potion when destroyed, based on the time they existed.
Ballista now has shields in front of it.
Ballista takes less time to place and has increased attack speed based on your Dexterity.
Ballista bolts pierces enemies (continue through targets they hit).
Black Hole deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but Black Hole pulls enemies towards it slower.
Black Hole has a chance to chill enemies within its area of effect each second.
Black Hole’s damage over time penetrates enemy fire resistance.
Black Hole has a chance to ignite enemies within its area of effect each second based on your uncapped fire resistance.
Black Hole deals more fire damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Black Hole grants you ward each second.
Black Hole deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Black Hole deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but has reduced cast speed.
The explosions created by the {Collapse} and {Pulsar} nodes have an increased chance to freeze enemies.
Black Hole has increased duration and a chance to blind enemies within its area of effect each second.
Black Hole has a chance to create another Black Hole once it expires.
Black Hole's cooldown is reduced and it costs less mana, but pulls enemies towards it slower and pulls enemies within a lesser area (both multiplicative with other modifiers).
Black Hole pulls enemies toward it faster and from within an increased area.
Black Hole moves faster toward the nearest enemy.
Black Hole’s center hits enemies, dealing spell physical damage.
Black Hole lasts longer.
Black Hole lasts longer, but has a longer cooldown.
A second Black Hole is created 10 meters away in a random direction from the first Black Hole.
Black Hole deals more damage to enemies within 1 meter of its center.
Black Hole deals significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but only 1 Black Hole can be created at a time.

Black Hole’s base cold damage is converted to fire damage. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to cold damage.

Black Hole lasts much longer, but no longer pulls enemies.

Black Hole becomes two stars which orbit each other. One deals fire damage and the other deals cold damage. 

Black Hole deals significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Black Hole will {Collapse} at the end of its duration, an explosion which deals cold hit damage and knocks enemies back.
Black Hole will {Collapse} each second while it exists, but it deals less damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Black Hole moves toward the nearest enemy that is not within its damaging area.
Black Hole casts {Meteor} every second, but consumes Meteor's mana cost every second.
You can store an additional charge of Black Hole.
Bladestorms have an increased duration and lower mana cost.
Directly casting Bladestorm throws up to your maximum number of Bladestorms in a spread in front of you, but Bladestorm has additional mana cost.
Bladestorms have increased movement speed based on your dexterity.
Bladestorms gain more movement speed (multiplicative with other modifiers) for 1 second if you {Shift} through any part of the Bladestorm.
Each {Umbral Blade} thrown into a Bladestorm is destroyed upon landing to grant the Bladestorm more damage and radius (both multiplicative with other modifiers) for 4 seconds, up to 40 stacks.
Each Bladestorm created grants stacks of {Blade Frenzy}. Your next direct cast of Shurikens or Umbral Blades consumes all stacks to gain additional throwing damage and projectiles thrown per stack.
Bladestorms cast {Shurikens} with increased frequency.
Bladestorms have a chance to shred armor on hit.
{Acid Flasks} which explode inside a Bladestorm deal more damage and have more radius (both multiplicative with other modifiers).

Bladestorm has increased area with a two handed melee weapon or bow equipped.

Bladestorm has increased hit rate while dual wielding.
Bladestorm has additional critical strike chance per equipped dagger, chance to inflict bleed on hit per equipped one handed sword, and additional critical strike multiplier with a two handed sword.
Bladestorm has increased area and increased attack speed.
Bladestorm has additional critical strike chance.
Bladestorm deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Bladestorm deals more hit damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has a lower mana cost.
Bladestorm has a chance on hit to inflict {Shadow Daggers}. This chance is doubled if the enemy is inside a {Smoke Bomb}.
{Blade Detonations} create {Caltrops} in the area. This effect can occur a limited number of times per 2 seconds.
You and allies hit by {Blade Detonation} gain Haste for a duration.
You gain mana each time you are hit by one of your own {Blade Detonations}.
Bladestorm has a chance to slow enemies on hit.
Bladestorm slowly seeks out enemies.

Bladestorms have more movement speed (multiplicative with other modifiers), and multiple Bladestorms can now hit the same target simultaneously.

Bladestorm has increased mana cost and duration. Your maximum number of Bladestorms is reduced.
Bladestorms repeatedly cast {Shurikens} at enemies within 10 meters of them, consuming 4 mana or Shurikens's mana cost, whichever is higher. This effect is only active while you are above half mana.
Bladestorms hit by {Shadow Cascade} will instantly hit enemies in their area.
When your Bladestorms collide, they are destroyed and each cause a {Blade Detonation}.

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to poison chance for Bladestorm.

This node has no effect if {Frostblade} is allocated.

Bladestorm's base physical damage is converted to cold. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold, but not increases to physical damage.

Bleed chance and poison chance from all sources is converted to frostbite chance.
Apply a curse to enemies in an area that causes spell physical damage when hit.
Bone Curse deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Bone Curse deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and instantly kills enemies that are below a health threshold (a percentage of their maximum health).

If you have a similar effect from an item or passive node then the highest threshold is used.
Enemies afflicted with Bone Curse have a chance to explode on death, dealing damage to nearby enemies, but Bone Curse costs more mana.
Bone Curse deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but falls off after a few hits.
Bone Curse has a chance to reduce enemy armor.
If you kill an enemy affected by Bone Curse you have a chance to gain {Bone Armor} for 4 seconds, which grants less damage taken (multiplicative with other modifiers).

When {Marrow Thief} triggers you are also healed. 

The healing scales with healing effectiveness.
Bone Curse has a chance to cause enemies to bleed.
When you cast Bone Curse, it now also leaves behind {Cursed Ground} for a short duration. Enemies that walk into the Cursed Ground are afflicted with Bone Curse.
Bone Curse affects a larger area.
Bone Curse has a significantly shorter cooldown, but {Bone Prison} now has gaps.
Your {Bone Prison} takes less damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
{Mark for Death} lasts longer.

Bone Curse deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but now only affects a single target. Bone Curse and Mark for Death have unlimited duration.

This is not compatible with the {Signet of Agony} node.
Bone Curse slows enemies within its area of effect
Bone Curse costs less mana and has increased cast speed.
Bone Curse has a chance to apply chill when the enemy is hit.
Adds a freeze rate to Bone Curse, granting it a chance to freeze enemies.

You may cast Bone Curse on your minions, which grants them more physical damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but also applies Bone Curse's negative effects. 

This effect lasts 4 seconds and does not stack.

Casting Bone Curse on minions causes them to reflect a flat amount damage to attackers. 

This effect lasts 4 seconds and does not stack.
All minion buffs from Bone Curse have 200% increased effect on Bone Golems.

Bone Curse deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but lingers for a shorter duration on enemies if not reapplied. The duration reduction only applies to Bone Curse applied by the aura.
Killing an enemy with Bone Curse has a chance to summon a Skeleton Vanguard, a minion that deals melee physical damage using a spear. You are limited to 3 Skeleton Vanguards by default.
Shortly after casting Bone Curse, it causes a {Bone Eruption} beneath the target area, causing enemies to take spell physical damage, but Bone Curse Costs more mana.

Bone Curse becomes a toggled aura which surrounds you, but it has reduced area and drains your mana over time. You are also inflicted with Bone Curse.

This is not compatible with the {Oppressive Gaze} and {Illusion of Pain} nodes.

Bone Curse falls off after only 1 hit, but it always crits and deals significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers). Bone Curse now has a cooldown.

This is not compatible with the {Signet of Agony} node.
The duration of {Cursed Limbs} and {Cursed Ground} are significantly reduced.
When Bone Curse times out, you cast {Rip Blood} on that enemy, a spell dealing physical damage. This effect can occur a limited number of times per second.

When you directly cast Bone Curse you summon a {Bone Prison} around the target area, trapping enemies inside. Bone Prison lasts a long time, but can be attacked and destroyed.

Bone Curse has a significantly longer cooldown.

Bone Curse applies {Mark for Death} for 2 seconds, but costs more mana.

This effect overwrites {Mark for Death's} normal duration.
Bone Curse's base physical damage is converted to necrotic. Consequently this damage scales with increases to necrotic damage, but not increases to physical damage.

After directly casting Bone Curse, you also cast Bone Curse on hit for the next few seconds, but Bone Curse costs more mana.
This cast on hit effect can't occur more than once per second.
Chaos Bolts has increased cast speed, and its projectiles travel faster.
Chaos Bolts' hits have a chance to inflict enemies with damned.

Chaos Bolts' base necrotic damage is converted to physical. Consequently this damage scales with increases to physical damage, but not increases to necrotic damage.

Damned chance from all sources are converted to bleed chance for Chaos Bolts.

Swaps Chaos Bolts' {Necrotic} tag for a {Physical} tag.
Chaos Bolts costs less mana, but now consumes a portion of your current health and ward on cast.
Chaos Bolts deals more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers) equal to a portion of your missing health.

Directly casting Chaos Bolts extends the duration of your active {Cthonic Fissures} and {Infernal Shades}. 

This effect has a 3 second cooldown.
Chaos Bolts' hits deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to damned, ignited, bleeding and frostbitten.
Chaos Bolts' hits have a chance to ignite enemies.

Chaos Bolts' base fire damage is converted to cold. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not increases to fire damage. It now has a chance to freeze enemies.

Ignite chance from all sources are converted to frostbite chance for Chaos Bolts.

Swaps Chaos Bolts' {Fire} tag for a {Cold} tag.

Chaos Bolts' hits have a chance to chill enemies. 

Additionally, Chaos Bolts' hits have a chance to deal double hit damage to chilled enemies.

Chaos Bolts' hits have a higher chance to freeze enemies per 1% uncapped cold resistance.

Additionally, Chaos Bolts deals more physical damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers) per 4% uncapped cold resistance.
Chaos Bolts explosions have a chance to deal damage in a much large area.
Chaos Bolts' hits now have a chance to fear enemies, but the projectiles target a wider area.
Chaos Bolts releases additional projectiles, but it has a higher mana cost.
Chaos Bolts' projectiles have a chance to consume additional mana causing it to deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Whenever mana is consumed by the {Mana Anarchy} node, your minions gain additional spell damage based on your maximum mana and they also gain increased cast speed.

This buff lasts 13 seconds.

Chaos Bolts no longer shoots multiple projectiles, but now instead shoots one large projectile causing it to deal more damage (multiplicative with other multipliers) in a larger area. 

These effects scale with the amount of projectiles Chaos Bolts would have shot.
Chaos Bolts' hitting one of your {Volatile Zombies} causes it to instantly explode. Zombies exploding this way deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area.
{Volatile Zombies} exploding from the {Volatile Kindling} node deal even more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area and poisons enemies.

Whenever you cast Chaos Bolts you gain a stack of {Destructive Intensity} up to a maximum.

Each stack reduces the target area and improves Chaos Bolts base critical strike chance.

If you go 2 seconds without gaining a stack, all stacks are lost.
Chaos Bolts deals more damage and its hits deal more damage to {Cursed} enemies (both multiplicative with other modifiers).

Chaos Bolts' hits have a chance per Dexterity to cause you to attack with {Harvest} from the location of enemies hit, hitting them and enemies around them.

This effect can occur a maximum of 3 times per second.
Chaos Bolts' hits against {Cursed} enemies regain health and mana.

Chaos Bolts' hits against {Cursed} enemies has a chance to cause you to recast Chaos Bolts from their location towards another nearby enemy, consuming a portion of its mana cost. 

The recast has a minimum range of 5 meters, and will not cast at enemies within that range.

Chaos Bolts' hits have a chance per Intelligence to cast {Rip Blood} at target enemies.

This effect can occur a maximum of 2 times per second.
Chaos Bolts hits refreshes the duration of your {Curses} on them and grants you health per curse.

Chaos Bolts' hits against {Cursed} enemies casts {Bone Curse} at their location.

This effect has a 6 second cooldown.

Chthonic Fissure costs less mana and it refunds some of its mana cost if there is a cursed player, rare enemy or boss within 20 meters when you cast it.

This effect also works if you are Cursed.
{Tormenting} an ignited enemy spreads all of your ignite stacks from it to a number of other enemies around it.
The Fissure releases {Spirits} more frequently.

Chthonic Fissure now creates a secondary Fissure in the opposite direction that casts its own {Spirits}. Enemies can take damage from both Fissures if they stand on both, but Chthonic Fissure costs significantly more mana.

{Spirits} now have a chance to be replaced by a cast of {Chaos Bolts} that consumes a portion of its mana cost and scales with your {Chaos Bolts} tree.

{Chaos Bolts} cast this way have a chance to have no additional projectiles.
When {Tormented} enemies die they have a chance to release the {Spirit} to inflict and {Torment} another enemy within 8 meters.
The {Spirits'} hits shred enemy necrotic resistance.
The {Spirits'} hits shred enemy fire resistance.

When {Spirits} {Torment} an enemy afflicted by an {Infernal Shade} they consume the Shade, causing it to instantly deal its remaining damage and increases all existing Chthonic Fissures' frequency of releasing {Spirits} up to a maximum.
The {Spirits'} hits inflict enemies with {Damned}.
Chthonic Fissure and ailments it inflicts deal more necrotic damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per 1% Added Critical Strike Multiplier.
{Torment} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) based on your uncapped necrotic resistance.

Chthonic Fissure deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to rare enemies and bosses.

This effect is doubled if the enemy or you are cursed.

{Spirits} now target you instead of enemies, and whenever you are {Tormented} or {Torment's} duration on you is refreshed, you gain additional spell damage and Endurance Threshold.

This buff can stack and has the same duration as {Torment}, but persists if {Torment} is cleansed.
The Fissure deals more direct damage for each second it is active (multiplicative with other modifiers) and you leech a portion of the damage it does.

You can now have additional Fissures active at a time, but {Spirits} are released less frequently.

If a Fissure is placed over an existing one, the new Fissure closes after 1 second.
Chthonic Fissure deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but it no longer releases {Spirits}.

Fissures now cast {Volatile Zombies} every 2 seconds, but consumes mana equal to a portion of {Volatile Zombies} mana cost.

{Volatile Zombie} cast this way has a chance to not create multiple {Zombies} if it would otherwise.

Chthonic Fissure deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

This effect is doubled if you are cursed.
The Fissure's initial impact now hits enemies dealing spell fire damage, with added damage applying at 300% effectiveness for this hit.

The Fissure's initial hit now deals additional spell damage with a higher chance to stun per 2% ignite chance.

This effects scales with bleed or poison if the Fissure is converted to physical or poison respectively.
The Fissure's initial hit now Curse enemies with {Acid Skin}, which causes them to take poison damage over time and a chance to be critically struck by any hit.

Your hits against enemies cursed by {Acid Skin} have additional base critical strike chance per 10% of your global increased poison damage.

This bonus critical strike chance is not affected by other critical strike chance modifiers.
The Fissure's initial impact now spreads all of your ignite stacks from the target to nearby enemies up to a maximum.

When directly casting Chthonic Fissure you now travel along the Fissure as it opens, but this adds a cooldown to Chthonic Fissure.

Chthonic Fissure gains the {Traversal} tag.

The Fissure gains a percentage of your global chance to ignite on hit as ignite chance per second. 

Additionally, the Fissure has a base chance to ignite enemies each second.

Chthonic Fissure's base fire damage is converted to physical. Consequently this damage scales with increases to physical damage, but not increases to fire damage.

Ignite chance from all sources are converted to bleed chance per second for the Fissure.

Swaps Chthonic Fissures' {Fire} tag for a {Physical} tag.
{Torment} lasts longer and any ignite, poison and bleed inflicted by Chthonic Fissure also last longer.
{Torment} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to ignited, poisoned or bleeding enemies.

Chthonic Fissure's base fire damage over time is converted to poison. Consequently this damage scales with increases to poison damage, but not increases to fire damage.

Ignite chance from all sources is converted to poison chance per second for the Fissure.

Swaps Chthonic Fissures' {Fire} tag for a {Poison} tag.
Cinder Strike has a chance to ignite enemies and ignites inflicted by the first attack have increased duration.
Cinder Strike's first attack deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area.
Cinder Strike's second and third attacks have increased attack speed.
Blind chance from all sources is converted to {Critical Vulnerability} chance for Cinder Strike.
Cinder Strike deals more critical strike damage. This bonus is doubled for the first attack.
Cinder Strike costs less mana.
{Volatile Flask} deals more explosion damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Cinder Strike has a chance to cause an explosion on kill. This counts as a {Volatile Flask} explosion.
Cinder Strike hits deals additional fire damage for each ignited enemy you have hit recently up to a maximum.
Cinder Strike's first attack has more critical strike chance (multiplicative with other modifiers) per stack of ignite on the target up to a maximum.
Cinder Strike deals increased fire damage over time per point of Dexterity.
Poison chance from all sources is converted to ignite chance for Cinder Strike.
If you use and hit at least one enemy with Cinder Strike you are granted {Oil Coating} granting you increased fire damage. This effect can stack and lasts 4 seconds.

If you have at least 3 stacks of {Oil Coating} you are granted additional fire penetration.

This effect is doubled for ignites inflicted by Cinder Strike.
{Oil Coating} is limited to 5 stacks. If you would gain another stack you instead regain health and mana.
You have additional chance to dodge and additional fire resistance per stack of {Oil Coating}.
{Oil Coating} now grants you increased elemental damage rather than just fire damage.
{Oil Coating} now also grants additional throwing fire damage.
Cinder Strike has a chance to shred enemy fire resistance.
{Burning Daggers} from all sources deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), and Burning Daggers from {Molten Arsenal} consume less mana.
All your {Shadows} now also throw 5 {Burning Daggers} in a cone in front of them when you do. This consumes all active {Shadows}.
Cinder Strike deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers and its hits deal additional fire damage.
Cinder Strike now only has the first attack and it deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but costs significantly more mana.
When you use Cinder Strike and hit with its first attack it has a chance to throw a {Volatile Flask} at a nearby target.
If you use and hit at least one enemy with Cinder Strike it consumes 8 mana granting you a {Burning Dagger} that circles you for a second before launching at the nearest enemy.
You now also throw 5 {Burning Daggers} in a cone in front of you on Cinder Strike's first attack.
A series of fast dash attacks.
Dancing Strikes penetrates enemy physical resistance.
Dancing Strikes inflicts enemies with {Shadow Dagger}.
Dancing Strikes has a chance to cause enemies to bleed.
You deal more global damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers) for each time you have used Dancing Strikes recently.
Dancing Strikes deals more melee damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per poison stacks on the target up to a maximum.
Dancing Strikes has a chance to poison enemies, but deals less physical damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Dancing Strikes hits deal more damage to bleeding and poisoned enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Dancing Strikes grants you additional dodge rating for a short duration on use.
Dancing Strikes grants you additional dodge rating per point of Dexterity for a short duration on use.
Dancing Strikes deals more damage to rare enemies and bosses (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Dancing Strikes' third attack creates a {Fighting Arena} for 4 seconds.

While fighting inside the arena, you and your allies deal more damage and take less damage (both multiplicative with other modifiers). The less damage taken is doubled for you.
You and allies fighting inside the arena have a high chance to cause enemies to bleed.
Dancing Strikes deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) but you travel less far between each attack.
You take less damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers) for a short duration after using Dancing Strikes.
{Lethal Mirage} and {Synchronized Strikes} deal increased damage for each time you have used Dancing Strikes recently.
Dancing Strikes' third attack heals you if it hits at least one enemy.
Dancing Strikes has a chance to grant you {Haste} for 2 seconds on use.
Dancing Strikes' second and fourth attack have a chance to throw {Shurikens} in target direction.
Dancing Strikes deals damage in a larger area.
Dancing Strikes has a chance to shred enemy armor.

When you use Dancing Strikes and hit at least one enemy, you gain a stack of {Rhythm} for a short duration and refresh all existing stacks, granting you global more damage per stack (multiplicative with other modifiers).
You have increased dodge rating per stack of {Rhythm}.
You have a global chance to cause enemies to bleed per stack of {Rhythm}.
Your critical strikes deal more damage per stack of {Rhythm}.
Each stack of {Rhythm} also grants 5% global more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but Dancing Strikes cost more mana.
Dancing Strikes' first attack shreds enemy armor.
Dancing Strikes' second attack creates a {Shadow}, but Dancing Strikes costs more mana.
Dancing Strikes shreds enemy physical resistance.
Your next {Puncture} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) after using Dancing Strikes.
Dancing Strikes' first attack has a chance to also attack with {Shadow Cascade}.
All bleed chance is converted to poison chance with Dancing Strikes. Physical penetration from this tree converted to poison penetration.

Requires a bow.

Summons 7 {Black Arrows} which rain from the sky over 4 seconds, granting bonus damage to your next bow attack when retrieved.
Using a {Black Arrow} grants increased damage over time to your next bow attack.
Picking up a {Black Arrow} grants you a stack of {Crimson Shroud} for a short duration, granting you bleed chance and less damage over time taken. This stacks up to 3 times for a short duration.
Picking up a {Black Arrow} restores health.
Picking up a {Black Arrow} grants you a stack of {Dusk Shroud} for 4 seconds, granting you glancing blow chance and dodge rating. This effect can stack.
Using a {Black Arrow} grants additional physical damage to your next bow attack.
Using a {Black Arrow} grants increased critical strike chance to your next bow attack.
Using a {Black Arrow} grants a chance to shred enemy physical resistance to your next bow attack.
Picking up a {Black Arrow} grants you the Frenzy buff, granting you 20% increased attack and cast speed for a short duration.
Using a {Black Arrow} grants more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to your next bow attack, but consume additional mana.
Using a {Black Arrow} causes your next {Cinder Strike} to instantly kill enemies that are below a health threshold (a percentage of their maximum health).
Picking up a {Black Arrow} restores mana.
Additional {Black Arrows} are summoned, but the arrows are summoned at the same frequency causing {Dark Quiver} to have a longer duration.
Using a {Black Arrow} grants increased elemental damage to your next bow attack.
Using a {Black Arrow} grants a chance to freeze, shock or ignite enemies, depending on the element, to your next bow elemental attack.
Picking up a {Black Arrow} slows nearby enemies for a short duration.
{Black Arrows} are summoned more frequently. This does not cause more arrows to be summoned and also causes {Dark Quiver} to have a shorter duration.
You instantly summon a number of {Black Arrows} on use. The rest are spread out over the normal duration.

Picking up a {Black Arrow} grants active {Ballistae} increased attack speed while they are alive.

This effect stacks up to 4 times.
{Ballistae} that have been buffed by at least 4 {Black Arrows} always critically strike.
Using a {Black Arrow} causes your next {Multishot} to pierce additional enemies (continue through targets they hit).
Using a {Black Arrow} improves your next {Flurry's} base critical strike chance.
Picking up a {Black Arrow} resets the combo of {Cinder Strike}.

Using a {Black Arrow} consumes all active {Shadows} to grant your next bow attack more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per {Shadow} consumed.

Consuming at least 3 {Shadows} grants significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Using a {Black Arrow} causes your next {Detonating Arrow} to deal damage in a significantly larger area.
Picking up a {Black Arrow} knocks nearby enemies back 4 meters.
Using a bow attack grants you a chance to summon a {Black Arrow} at a random nearby location. This effect has a 3 second cooldown.
Using a {Black Arrow} causes your next {Hail of Arrows} to deal increased damage over time equal to twice your critical strike chance.

Picking up a {Black Arrow} grants active {Ballistae} additional bow physical damage while they are alive.

This effect stacks up to 4 times.
Picking up a {Black Arrow} creates a {Shadow} at the arrow's location.
Using a Black Arrow grants your next {Heartseeker} additional {Recurve} chance.

Combo skill. Seals your health at your current amount of health, draining your ward to 0 and preventing you from gaining health or ward for up to 5 seconds. For that duration you gain more damage equal to your percent missing health.

Reactivate to release a wave of death dealing damage based on how long the seal has been active, and percent missing health.
When you kill an enemy while sealed, a percentage of all of your delayed damage is cleared.
The Wave of Death has a chance to shred necrotic resistance.
If {Wandering Spirits} is active when you activate Death Seal it is granted additional duration.
When Death Seal ends, a percentage of all of your delayed damage is cleared.

You release a Wave of Death when you cast {Reap} or {Evade}.

Death Seal must be on your action bar for this effect to work.
For the duration of Death Seal, you gain armor and stun avoidance for each 10 health you were missing when Death Seal was activated.
If you drop below 10% health while you are sealed, Death Seal is released automatically.
While sealed, you take less poison damage per 10% missing health, up to 50% missing health.
Wave of Death hits deal more damage against poisoned targets (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Wave of Death hits deal more damage per stack of poison up to a maximum (multiplicative with other modifiers).
The Wave of Death has increased area of effect and additional critical strike chance.
Wave of Death deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Wave of Death hits now leech a portion of the damage they deal as health.
When you release Death Seal, you are granted {Bone Armor}, which grants less damage taken for the duration that Death Seal was active.
Death Seal's waves of death instantly kill enemies that are below a health threshold.
Minions hit by a Wave of Death deal more damage for 4 seconds (multiplicative with other modifiers).
When you release Death Seal, you are granted {Haste} for 4 seconds.
When you release Death Seal, you recover a percentage of your missing health.

You gain a stack of {Damned} for each second you remain sealed. Waves of Death hits now deal more damage per stack of Damned on you (multiplicative with other modifiers), up to a maximum benefit. 

You are cleansed of all Damned stacks when you release Death Seal.
Death Seal's maximum duration is increased.
When used on a minion, Death Seal also grants it more damage and critical strike chance (both multiplicative with other modifiers).

You release {Hungering Souls} more frequently.

Additionally, whenever you cast Reap or Evade, you gain this effect for a percentage of Death Seal's maximum duration. Death Seal must be on your action bar for this effect to occur.
Death Seal prevents health loss from effects that continously drain a percentage of your current health.
You release a Wave of Death each second while sealed and upon activation, in addition to when you release Death Seal, but your waves of death deal less damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
If your health is above 33% of your maximum when you activate Death Seal, it is reduced to 33% of your maximum health.

Wave of Death's base damage is converted to physical. Consequently this damage scales with increases to physical damage, but not increases to necrotic damage.

All of its effects related to necrotic resistance and necrotic resistance shred are converted to physical resistance and physical resistance shred.
Death Seal now targets only minions.
Minions targeted by Death Seal are rooted in place for its duration. When Death Seal is released, the minion explodes in addition to releasing the wave of death at its location.
You now repeatedly cast {Hungering Souls} while you are sealed. Hungering Souls cast this way has a 70% chance to have only 1 projectile.

The Wave of Death's base damage is converted to cold. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not increases to necrotic damage.

All of its effects related to necrotic resistance and necrotic resistance shred are converted to cold resistance and cold resistance shred.
Summon a decoy.
Decoy's final explosion deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and explodes in a larger area.
All explosions from Decoy deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Detonation costs less mana.
All explosions have a higher chance to freeze enemies.
Decoy can cause additional small explosions.
Decoy lasts longer, but has a longer cooldown.
Decoy reflects a percentage of damage to attackers.
You have additional dodge chance while the decoy exists.
When the decoy expires, for the next 4 seconds you have additional critical strike multiplier equal to your dodge chance while the decoy was active.
The Decoy has increased ignite frequency.
Decoy costs less mana.
{Sonic Wave} grants {Haste} to you and your allies hit for a duration.
{Sonic Wave} inflicts additional stacks of armor shred, and armor shred it applies lasts longer.

{Sonic Wave} inflicts fire resistance shred instead of armour shred.

If you have taken {Frostfire Powder} it instead inflicts cold resistance shred.
Decoy's final explosion deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but Decoy has a shorter duration.
Decoy's final explosion ignites enemies.
Decoy's final explosion deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but costs more mana.

Decoy becomes a combo ability. The first cast creates the decoy, and the second detonates it. The remote detonation costs mana.

Has no effect if Decoy has additional charges.
Decoy has a chance to cause a small explosion when hit, up to a maximum of 2 explosions.

All explosions' base fire damage is converted to cold. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not increases to fire damage. Explosions also gain a base freeze rate.

Ignite chance from all sources is converted to chill chance and effects related to ignite now depend on chill instead.
The Decoy is on fire and ignites nearby enemies each second, but costs more mana.

Small explosions are replaced by Acid Flask detonations. The final explosion is replaced by three Acid Flasks ejected from the decoy.
{On Fire} now inflicts poison instead of ignite.
Decoy can store an additional charge.
The Decoy emits a {Sonic Wave} when it appears, inflicting {Frailty} and armor shred to nearby enemies.
{Sonic Wave} Fears enemies for a duration.
Decoy leaves a {Shadow} in its place after detonating.
Throw additional Decoys near the target location. Decoy location is slightly randomised.

Requires a bow.

Fires an arrow that attatches itself in a target then explodes after 0.6 seconds. Added bow damage is converted to lightning damage.
Detonating Arrow has increased critical strike chance and it has reduced arming time.
Detonating Arrow critical strikes deal more damage and it has a chance to apply {Critical Vulnerability} to enemies, making them easier to critically strike.
The explosion deals damage in a larger area.
Detonating Arrow has a chance to shock enemies, but the explosion hit deals less hit damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

The explosion deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area.

Detonating Arrow has a higher chance based on your uncapped Cold Resistance.
Detonating Arrow causes enemies to have less movement speed (multiplicative with other modifiers) for each arrow attached.
Detonating Arrow has a longer arming time (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Detonating Arrow causes enemies to have less elemental resistance while they have at least 1 arrow attached.
The explosion hit deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per 5% health the enemy is missing.
The explosion deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but in a smaller area (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Detonating Arrow deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per second charged up.
Detonating Arrow has a chance to shock enemies.

Detonating Arrow now explodes on impact in a cone behind the target rather than a circle, but no longer has an arming time.

The explosion base lightning damage is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to lightning damage.

Shock chance from all sources is converted to ignite chance and effects related to shock now depend on ignite instead.

Swaps Detonating Arrow's {Lightning} tag for a {Fire} tag.
Detonating Arrow now has a cooldown, but when you directly use it, it deals more damage in a significantly larger area (both multiplicative with other modifiers), and has an increased chance to stun.

Swaps Detonating Arrow's {Lightning} tag for a {Cold} tag.

Detonating Arrow can now be charged up for up to 2 seconds, causing it to pierce a number of enemies (continue through targets it hits) per 0.5 seconds charged up.

Swaps Detonating Arrow's {Lightning} tag for {Poison} and {Physical} tags.

This has no effect if Detonating Arrow is converted to fire or cold with {Dragon Arrow} or {Frostfang Arrow}.

When you hit an enemy with Detonating Arrow that already has an arrow attached to them, the explosion deals more damage in a larger area (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Devouring Orb costs less mana, but has a shorter duration (multiplicative with other duration modifiers).

Entangling Roots' base physical damage over time is converted to poison and it now has the poison tag. Consequently this damage scales with increases to poison damage, but not increases to physical damage.
Rooted enemies are also blinded.

Entangling Roots now creates seeds in the area. When an enemy draws too close to a seed it bursts, releasing a burst of spores that slow and poison enemies.

If you have taken the {Winternid Thorns} node, the seed bursts inflict {Frostbite} and {Chill} instead of slow and poison.

The spores from {Seeds} now heal you and your allies. 

This effect is tripled for minions.
Seeds now burst when an ally draws close to them which grants you and your allies the {Haste} buff, granting you 30% increased movement speed.
Seeds now burst when you hit them with {Upheaval} and deal damage in a larger area when detonated this way.
Seeds will now grow {Vines} when they burst.
Root Wave immobilizes enemies for a longer duration.
Root Wave cannot be dodged.
Entanging Roots now wraps around your totems and vines for twice the duration as enemies, granting them additional armor and increased attack and cast speed.
Entangling Roots now also wraps around your Spriggans for twice the duration as enemies, granting them all buffs from {Frenzying Thorns} as well as additional projectiles with their {Thorn Volley}.
Entangling Roots leeches a portion of the damage it deals as health to you.
Entangling Roots deal more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Entangling Roots damage accelerates over time, dealing more damage each second (multiplicative with other modifiers).
You now cause Entangling Roots to expand in a circle around you rather than a cone in front, but it now has a cooldown.
Entangling Roots is now cast from the nearest totem to your cursor.

When you are stunned, you now cast Entangling Roots. 

This effect has a 20 seconds cooldown, which doesn't affect Entangling Root's cooldown.
Root Wave hits shred enemy physical resistance.
Root Wave's hits deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and have a higher chance to stun enemiess.

Entangling Roots' base damage is converted to cold. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not increases to physical damage. It also has a chance to freeze enemies.

Physical Resistance Shred from all sources is converted to Cold Resistance Shred chance and effects related to Physical Resistance Shred now depend on Cold Resistance Shred instead.

Root Wave has a chance to inflict {Frostbite}. 

Poison chance from all sources is converted to Frostbite for Root Wave and effects related to poison now depend on Frostbite instead.
Root Wave gains additional freeze rate per character level.

Root Wave now heals other allies it hits. The amount of health gained is increased by healing effectiveness.

This effect is tripled for minions.
Root Wave causes your minions and other allies it hits to deal additional physical damage with spells and attacks for 8 seconds. This effect does not stack.
Root Wave causes your minions and other allies it hits to deal additional damage with spells for 8 seconds. This effect does not stack.
Root Wave causes your Wolves and Bear it hits to have improved base critical strile chance and deal more damage with critical strikes for 8 seconds. This effect does not stack.

Root Wave causes your minions and other allies it hits to have additional chance to poison on hit. This buff lasts 8 seconds and does not stack.

The effect is tripled for Scorpions and Serpents.
Erasing Strike and {Void Rifts} it creates deals damage in a larger area.
Erasing Strike hits have a chance to inflict enemies with {Time Rot}, an ailment that deals void damage over time, increases stun duration by 5%, lasts 3 seconds, and stacks up to 12 times.
Erasing Strike deals more damage over time.
Erasing Strike always creates a {Void Rift} at its impact location.
Erasing Strike hits shred enemy void resistance.
Erasing Strike has increased cooldown recovery speed and deals more melee damage to enemies that are already damaged (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Erasing Strike deals more melee damage to enemies at full health (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Erasing Strike deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and instantly kill enemies that are below a health threshold (a percentage of their maximum health).

Erasing Strike deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers). 

This bonus is doubled while wielding two handed weapon.

Improves Erasing Strike's base critical strike chance.

Additionally, Erasing Strike's hits cannot be dodged.
Erasing Strike critical strikes deal more damage.
You gain the {Haste} buff for 1 second, granting 30% increased movement speed when Erasing Strike kills an enemy or hits a boss or rare enemy.
The kills required to summon {Void Beams} via the {Ravenous Void} node require less kills to trigger, but Erasing Strike costs more mana.

Erasing Strike deals more void damage if you are using a two handed mace (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Erasing Strike has increased cooldown recovery speed if you are using a two handed sword.

Erasing Strike's melee hit and void rifts have a larger area of effect if you are using a two handed axe.
When Erasing Strike kills an enemy you have a chance to cast {Void Beam} at their location, a spell that continuously damages surrounding enemies for 3 seconds.

Erasing Strike critical strikes have a chance to grant you a {Void Essence}, which grants 3% more void damage, 3% more melee damage, and 15% reduced duration of stuns received.

You can have a maximum of 3 void essences at once.
When Erasing Strike kills an enemy or hits a boss or rare enemy you regain health equal to a percentage of the target's maximum health.
If you kill 5 or more enemies simultaneously with Erasing Strike, you create 4 {Void Beams} which travel at random, damaging surrounding enemies for 3 seconds.
When Erasing Strike hits a boss or rare enemy you have a chance to cast {Void Beam} at their location, a spell that continuously damages surrounding enemies for 3 seconds.
All {Void Beams} created by Erasing Strike deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Erasing Strike consumes mana to cause additional echoes when used. Each echo will appear one second after the last. These additional echos cannot be created while you have negative mana.
Erasing Strike no longer has a cooldown, but it deals less damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Erasing Strike hits have a chance to slow and deals more melee damage to slowed enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to {Time Rot} chance for Erasing Strike and effects related to bleed now depend on Time Rot instead. 

Erasing Strike deals more melee damage against targets with 12 stacks of Time Rot (multiplicative with other modifiers).
You can store an additional charge of Erasing Strike.
Erasing Strike has increased cooldown recovery speed, but also has a higher mana cost.
Eterra's Blessing grants elemental and poison resistance.
Your next spell after casting Eterra's Blessing deals increased damage.
After casting Eterra's Blessing, your spells leech a portion of the damage they deal as health for 4 seconds.
When cast on a minion, Eterra's Blessing grants stun immunity for 4 seconds.
When Eterra's Blessing is cast on your {Scorpion} companion, you are both granted a chance to poison enemies with attacks and spells for 4 seconds.
When cast on a minion, Eterra's Blessing also grants increased movement, attack, and cast speed for 4 seconds.
When Eterra's Blessing heals one of your companions it has a chance to refresh its activated ability cooldown, but Eterra's Blessing costs more mana.
When Eterra's Blessing targets a downed companion, it heals them for a portion of their maximum downed duration.
When Eterra's Blessing is cast on one of your companions, you also heal yourself for a portion of its base heal amount.
Eterra's Blessing has a chance to heal all allies nearby its target for 50 health.
Eterra's Blessing costs less mana.
Eterra's Blessing knocks back enemies a given distance from its target.
Eterra's Blessing has increased healing effectiveness.
Eterra's Blessing cleanses all ailments on its target.
When {Efflorescence's} heal over time expires, heal again for half of Eterra's Blessing's value.
When you are out of mana Eterra's Blessing is free, but Eterra's Blessing can no longer provide additional buffs.
When you heal a target that is already at high health, you have a chance to summon a {Healing Totem} at its location which heals nearby allies.
Eterra's Blessing heals for more if you have transformed recently (last 4 seconds).
When Eterra's Blessing is cast on your {Wolf} or {Bear} companions, you are both granted increased physical damage for 4 seconds.
When Eterra's Blessing is cast on your {Sabertooth} or {Raptor} companions, you are both granted a chance to cause enemies to bleed with attacks and spells for 4 seconds.
When you directly cast Eterra's Blessing on a companion it taunts nearby enemies for 4 seconds. However Eterra's Blessing now has a long cooldown.
Eterra's Blessing automatically targets the lowest health ally in range, but Eterra's Blessing costs more mana.
When Eterra's Blessing is cast on your {Spriggan} companion, you are both granted increased spell damage for 4 seconds.
Eterra's Blessing heals half of its value immediately, and half of its value over 4 seconds.

Whenever you directly cast Thorn Shield, you also cast Eterra's Blessing on the same target.

Consumes Eterra's Blessing's mana cost as rage.
Explosive Trap deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and its hits shred enemy fire resistance.

Whenever you hit an enemy with any melee attack, you attach an Explosive Trap to that enemy.

This effect has a 1.5 second cooldown and attached traps explode after 1 second ignoring arming time or when the enemy it's attached to dies. Enemies cannot have more than 1 trap attached.

Attached traps deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

If the enemy that the Explosive Trap is attached to hits you, it now attaches to you instead (but will still only damage enemies). Hitting an enemy then attaches it back to them without changing the delay before exploding.

Additionally, when it explodes, the trap deals 30% more damage for each time it has been passed.

When an Explosive Trap detonates, all other Explosive Traps now also detonate in quick succession and fire {Detonating Arrows} at the original target trap's location. The Detonating Arrows can occur a limited number of times per 2 seconds.

This effect consumes mana equal to a portion of your {Detonating Arrow's} mana cost.
{Detonating Arrows} fired this way deal additional bow damage per 3 Dexterity.

Explosive Trap's base fire damage is converted to lightning. Consequently this damage scales with increases to lightning damage, but not increases to fire damage.

Fire resistance shred chance from all sources is converted to lightning resistance shred.

Swaps Explosive Trap's {Fire} tag for a {Lightning} tag.
Explosive Trap's hits have a chance to shock enemies and inflict them with a {Spark Charge}.

When directly using Explosive Trap, an unconverted Explosive Trap and a trap corresponding to each conversion node you have taken are thrown at the same time. Traps thrown this way are thrown in a small area.

Additionally, Explosive Trap deals less damage in a smaller area (both multiplicative with other modifiers) and cost additional mana.

Explosive Trap's base fire damage is converted to cold. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not increases to fire damage.

Fire resistance shred chance from all sources is converted to cold resistance shred.

Swaps Explosive Trap's {Fire} tag for a {Cold} tag.
