Explosive Trap's hits have a chance to chill enemies.

Additionally, Explosive Trap has additional freeze rate per Intelligence.

When you directly throw Explosive Trap, its hits always freeze enemies, but it now has a cooldown.

Whenever you freeze an enemy with any skill, a portion of Explosive Trap's remaining cooldown is recovered.
Explosive Trap is now triggered from farther away and deals damage in a larger area.

Particularly fast enemies (with a movement speed above 6 meters per second) trigger Explosive Traps instantly, even if they haven't armed.

Additionally, Explosive Trap's hits deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to moving enemies.
Explosive Trap deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area for each second it has existed before detonating up to a maximum.
Explosive Trap deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) if the arming time is not ignored, but it takes longer for it to arm before it can detonate.
When an Explosive Trap detonates, if it hit at least one enemy, it has a chance to grant you a stack of {Dusk Shroud}. This chance increases for each second it spends arming.
Explosive Trap deals additional throwing damage per stack of {Dusk Shroud} you have.
Explosive Trap costs less mana and it has increased throwing attack speed.

Whenever one of your Explosive Traps detonate, your {Falcon} gains physical and elemental penetration for a short duration.

This effect can trigger up to a maximum of 15 times within 3 seconds.
Whenever one of your Explosive Traps detonate, your {Falcon's} skills recover a portion of their remaining cooldowns.
You can now have more active Explosive Traps.
When you directly use Explosive Trap you throw additional traps, but Explosive Trap costs more mana.
Explosive Trap costs less mana, but deals less damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a smaller area.

You now shoot arrows attached with Explosive Traps up into the air that then drop down around the target location.

Explosive Trap now scales with your bow damage instead of throwing damage and your bow attack speed instead of throwing attack speed.

This requires wielding a bow.

Explosive Traps now instantly detonate when the arrows hit the ground via the {Blast Rain} node, ignoring the arming time.

Additionally, Explosive Trap has a higher chance to stun enemies.
Whenever an Explosive Trap detonates, it has a chance to drop a fresh Explosive Trap at its location as long as there is an enemy within 6 meters, but Explosive Trap costs more mana.

Explosive Trap has an additional chance to drop a fresh Explosive Trap via the {Trap Sprinkler} node, but Explosive Trap costs more mana.

Traps dropped this way cannot drop more additional traps.

Whenever an Explosive Trap detonates, it now has a chance to create a {Ballista} for you.

This consumes mana equal to a portion of {Ballista's} mana cost.

Explosive Traps dropped by the {Trap Sprinkler} node are now flung at a nearby enemy within 12 meters instead of being dropped.

Explosive Traps dropped by the {Trap Sprinkler} node can now drop Explosive Traps when there's an enemy within 12 meters even if there is not an enemy within 6 meters.

When an Explosive Trap drops a trap by the {Trap Sprinkler} node, it also flings an {Acid Flask} for you towards the target location.

This consumes mana equal to a portion of Acid Flask's mana cost.

Whenever you directly throw Explosive Trap, you now throw traps, up to your maximum number of traps, in a wide area around the target location.

Explosive Trap deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per maximum traps, but it now has a cooldown and costs more mana per maximum trap.
Whenever the Falcon hits an enemy with {Falcon Strikes} it {Marks} them. Your next hit against a {Marked} target deals more damage and consumes the {Mark}.
Your Falcon's other hits now have a chance to inflict {Falconer's Mark}.

When you consume a {Falconer's Mark} your Falcon gains additional melee damage equal to a portion of your added melee damage, throwing damage, bow damage, or spell damage depending on the type of ability used to consume the mark.

This effect lasts 10 seconds and can stack a limited number of times.

Your Falcon now throws {Acid Flasks} for you. {Acid Flasks} thrown by the Falcon scale with your stats and your {Acid Flask} tree, but can apply {Falcon's Mark} from {Marking Strikes}.

This effect consumes mana equal to a portion of your {Acid Flask's} mana cost and has a 6 second cooldown.
Your Falcon has additional {Acid Flask} charges.
When you directly use {Acid Flask} your Falcon has a chance to gain additional {Acid Flask} charges.
Your Falcon deals more global damage per character level and per point of Dexterity (both multiplicative with other modifiers).

Your Falcon deals more global damage (multiplicative with other modifiers). 

This effect is tripled for {Falcon Strikes}.
Your Falcon now more heavily prioritizes enemies close to you during combat.
Your Falcon now heavily prioritizes enemies close to the target location of your most recent melee, throwing or bow attack.

Your Falcon deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

This effect is tripled against moving targets.
When your Falcon hits an enemy it has a chance to drop a {Black Arrow} at its location.
A portion of your chance to inflict bleed, poison, shred armor, shred resistances, and slow on hit now also apply to your Falcon's hits.

Your Falcon now uses {Feather Knives} at nearby enemies. This effect has a 10 second cooldown.

Your Falcon also throws a single Feather Knife whenever you consume {Falconer's Mark}.
A portion of your global throwing attack speed now applies to the Falcon's {Feather Knives} ability as increased cooldown recovery speed.
A portion of the highest of your global increased melee damage, increased throwing damage, or increased bow damage now apply to all your Falcon's damage.
Your critical strike chance modifiers now also apply to your {Falcon}, but at a reduced ratio.

When you use a melee, throwing, or bow attack and critically strike at least one enemy, your Falcon gains a portion of your global melee, throwing, or bow critical strike multiplier respectively for 10 seconds.

This effect can occur at most twice every two second.
When you directly use a melee, throwing, or bow attack and hit at least one enemy, you reduce a portion of {Falcon Strike's} remaining cooldown.

Your Falcon's {Screech} now also fears enemies it hits.

Additionally, your Falcon moves to you when a hit leaves you at low health, and this effect will interrupt any skill it is currently using.

This effect can occur at most every 10 seconds.
{Falcon Strikes} hits addtional times and your {Falcon} has increased melee attack speed.

{Falcon Strikes} instantly kill enemies that are below a health threshold (a percentage of their maximum health).

If you have a similar effect from an item or passive node then the highest threshold is used.
{Falcon Strikes} now cause your Falcon to swoop down in a line in front of you attacking all enemies in that line rather than hitting enemies individually.

A portion of your global increased area for area skills now applies to the width of {Falcon Strikes}.

Additionally, {Falcon Strikes} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) if you have directly used an area skill in the past 8 seconds.

If {Falcon Strikes} kills an enemy it will hit an additional time.

Additionally, when {Falcon Strikes} hits a boss or rare enemy it has a chance to hit an additional time.

When your Falcon kills an enemy or hits a boss or rare enemy it restores health and mana to you based on your total attributes. This effect can occur from hits a limited number of times per 3 seconds.

When you consume {Falconer's Mark} you gain the {Falcon's Wake} buff for a short duration. 

This buff grants you increased movement speed, attack and cast speed.
Consuming a {Falconer's Mark} instantly recovers {Falcon Strike's} cooldown, but it now also costs additional mana.
Fireball now pierces enemies (continue through targets it hits), but costs more mana.
Fireball hits have additional fire penetration and lightning penetration.
Improves Fireball's base critical strike chance.
Fireball deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and travels faster, causing it to travel farther.
Fireball has additional critical strike chance, but it has less cast speed (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Fireball releases additional projectiles that fire in a cone when cast, but costs more mana.
Fireball releases additional projectiles. These additional projectiles are NOT reduced by the extra projectiles penalty via the {Embers} node.
Fireball hits have a higher chance to ignite enemies.

Fireball hits have a chance to apply {Spreading Flames}, an ailment which deals fire damage over time and spreads to nearby enemies.

Additionally all damage over time ailments applied by Fireball deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifier).
{Spreading Flames} applied by fireball has additional fire penetration
Fireball hits have more critical strike chance against ignited enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Fireball has increased cast speed.
Fireball hits have a chance to reduce enemy fire resistance.
Flame Burst requires fewer hits with Fireball to trigger.
Fireball deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and costs less mana.
Fireballs have a chance to pierce enemies (continue through targets it hits), but {Flamethrower} costs more mana per second to channel.
{Flamethrower} costs less mana per second to channel.
You have additional Armor and Fire Resistance while channeling Fireball's {Flamethrower}.
Fireballs last longer, causing them to travel further.
Fireball releases additional projectiles that fire in a cone when cast, but costs more mana.
Nodes on this tree that add crit chance instead increase stun chance by 20 times the stated value.

Directly cast Fireballs fire in sequence in the same direction and can now hit the same target, but Fireball releases fewer projectiles and has less cast speed for each extra projectile (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Fireballs now seek nearby targets.
Hitting the same enemy with several fireballs triggers a {Flame Burst} around them.
Fireball has increased cast speed and deals more damage for each extra projectile (multiplicative with other modifiers), but no longer fires extra projectiles.
Fireball becomes a channelled ability that rapidly spews fireballs from your hand, but rapidly drains your mana. You do not regenerate mana while channelling.

Half of Fireball's base fire damage is converted to lightning. Consequently this portion of its damage scales with increases to lightning damage, but not increases to fire damage.

Fireball gains a {Lightning} tag.

Fireball also gains additional critical strike multiplier.
A melee attack which grants you a stack of firebrand buff if it hits an enemy. Firebrand buff increases the size of subsequent firebrand attacks and adds melee fire damage.
Firebrand deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Firebrand deals more melee damage per stack of Firebrand (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Using {Shatter Strike} consumes stacks of Firebrand to gain {Frostbite} Chance per stack consumed.
Firebrand has increased melee critical strike chance per stack of Firebrand.
Firebrand has additional melee critical strike multiplier per stack of Firebrand.
Consuming stacks of Firebrand with {Incineration} grants bonus critical strike chance and critical strike multiplier per stack.
Firebrand can have more maximum stacks.
Firebrand grants additional armor and fire resistance per stack of Firebrand.
While you have at least 1 stack of Firebrand, your next cast of {Lightning Blast} will consume all stacks to chain additional times per stack consumed.
Gain stacks of {Static} if Firebrand hits at least one enemy.
You gain ward when Firebrand hits at least one enemy.
You gain ward per second per stack of Firebrand.
Stacks of Firebrand last longer.
You refresh the duration of all stacks of Firebrand when gaining a new stack.
Firebrand attacks faster per stack of Firebrand, up to a maximum.
Firebrand grants global spell critical strike multiplier per stack of Firebrand.
If Firebrand hits at least one enemy, it consumes mana to cast {Flame Wave}, a spell that deals fire damage in a line.
Firebrand has a chance to ignite enemies.

Firebrand has a chance to inflict {Spreading Flames} when hitting an ignited enemy.

Stacks of Firebrand also grant more fire damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) against enemies afflicted by Spreading Flames.
Each stack of Firebrand grants you global Ignite Chance.
Using {Flame Reave} consumes stacks of Firebrand to gain {Ignite} Chance per stack consumed.
You gain an additional stack of Firebrand when attacking, but Firebrand costs more mana.
You gain ward when a stack of Firebrand expires or is consumed.
Firebrand now shoots three lines instead of one in a cone in front of you, but costs more mana.
Your next melee attack, that is not Firebrand, consumes all stacks of Firebrand and deals more damage per stack consumed (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Firebrands's base fire damage and added melee fire damage from stacks is converted to lightning. Consequently this damage scales with increases to lightning damage, but not increases to fire damage.

All ignite chance from this tree is converted to shock chance and effects related to ignite now depend on shock instead.

Swaps Firebrand's {Fire} tag for a {Lightning} tag.
Firebrand can have more maximum stacks and deals more damage per stack, but it has less attack speed (both multiplicative with other modifiers).
Stacks of Firebrand now also grant added spell fire damage.
Directly using Firebrand has a chance to grant a stack of Fire Aura based on your current Firebrand stacks. This effect can trigger a limited number of times per second.
Flame Reave has increased attack speed and costs less mana, but travels more slowly, reducing its range.

Using Flame Reave grants 4 stacks of {Rhythm of Fire}. If you already have 12 stacks, Flame Reave instead consumes all the stacks to gain significantly more width, range, and damage (all multiplicative with other modifiers).

Directly using Surge, Fireball, Mana Strike, or Firebrand also grants stacks of {Rhythm of Fire}.

These skills cannot consume stacks of {Rhythm of Fire} and you cannot exceed the number of stacks required for Flame Reave to consume stacks, which is 12 by default.

Flame Reave hits have a chance to apply {Spreading Flames}.

Flame Reave deals more fire damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Flame Reave hits have a chance to grant you {Frenzy} for 3 seconds.

In addition to Frenzy's base effect it now grants Flame Reave additional melee fire damage.
Flame Reave hits deal additional melee fire damage per equipped sword.
Flame Reave deals more fire damage (multiplicative with other modifiers)

When you directly use Flame Reave and kill an enemy or hit a boss or rare enemy, you cast {Lightning Blast} at a random enemy within 6 meters. This effect can occur a limited number of times per 2 seconds, and consumes a percentage of Lightning Blast's mana cost.
Flame Reave deals more melee damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and it deals added melee damage based on your added spell damage.
Flame Reave deals more melee damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Flame Reave now has a cooldown, but it expands and returns a second time. This can hit the same enemy two additional times.
Flame Reave can store an additional charge and has increased cooldown recovery rate, but it also has additional mana cost.
Each time Flame Reave returns, it pulls enemies to you and grants you {Haste} for a duration.
Flame Reave has additional critical strike chance, but deals damage in a narrower area.
When you attack with Flame Reave and critically strike at least one enemy you gain ward.
Flame Reave hits have a chance to ignite enemies.
Flame Reave hits deal more melee damage against ignited enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Flame Reave melee hits have more critical strike chance against ignited enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Flame Reave travels faster, increasing its range.
Flame Reave's critical strikes deal more damage.
Flame Reave deals damage in a larger area.

When you directly cast Flame Reave and consume {Rhythm of Fire}, you cast {Elemental Nova} with more area and damage (multiplicative with other modifiers). This effect consumes a percentage of Elemental Nova's mana cost.

Half of Flame Reave's base damage is converted to lightning, meaning that this half of the damage scales with increases to lightning damage but not with increases to fire damage.

Fire damage bonuses from this tree now also apply to Lightning.

Ignite chance from this tree now also grants an equal amount of shock chance.

Flame Reave gains a {Lightning} tag.

When you directly use Flame Reave and kill an enemy or hit a boss or rare enemy, you cast {Fireball} at a random enemy within 6 meters. This effect can occur a limited number of times per 2 seconds, and consumes a percentage of Fireball's mana cost.
Flame Reave returns to you. This can hit the same enemy again.
Flame Reave hits enemies in an expanding circle centered on you, but has less range and costs significantly more mana.

You have a chance to cast {Fire Aura} when you directly use Flame Reave. Flame Reave has additional chance to Ignite on hit per active Fire Aura.

These effects are tripled if Flame Reave has a cooldown.

Flame Reave requires fewer stacks of {Rhythm of Fire} to consume all stacks. This does not affect the bonuses from consuming stacks.

Additionally when Flame Reave consumes {Rhythm of Fire} stacks it gains additional critical strike chance.
Flame Rush costs less mana and has a shorter cooldown. These effects are doubled if you have enough uncapped fire resistance.
Flame Rush deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per 40 current mana.

Flame Rush now casts {Glyph of Dominion} at the target location instead of channeling and Flame Rush ends when reaching the Glyph.

This effect consumes mana equal to a portion of {Glyph of Dominion's} mana cost.

Flame Rush now casts {Volcanic Orb} that is attached to you and only detonates when you end Flame Rush, alongside any other active {Volcanic Orbs}.

This effect consumes mana equal to a portion of {Volcanic Orb's} mana cost.

You take less damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) during Flame Rush.

This effect is doubled against damage over time effects.
All buff effects from the Flame Rush tree persist afterwards for a portion of the time equal to the duration you spent in Flame Rush.
You gain ward per second during Flame Rush and you gain a burst of ward when Flame Rush ends.

{Flame Rush's} base fire damage is converted to lightning. Consequently this damage scales with increases to lightning damage, but not increases to fire damage.

{Fire Resistance Shred} chance from all sources is converted to {Lightning Resistance Shred} for Flame Rush and effects related to Fire Shred now depend on Lightning Shred instead.

Swaps {Flame Rush's} {Fire} tag for a {Lightning} tag.

Flame Rush now also casts a {Static Orb} in the opposite direction.

This effect consumes mana equal to a portion of {Static Orb's} mana cost.

{Flame Rush's} base lightning damage is converted to cold. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not increases to lightning damage.

{Lightning Resistance Shred} chance from all sources is converted to {Cold Resistance Shred} for Flame Rush and effects related to Lightning Shred now depend on Cold Shred instead.

Swaps {Flame Rush's} {Lightning} tag for a {Cold} tag.

Flame Rush now inflicts {Brand of Subjugation} to enemies you pass through. The Brand does not stack but deals a large amount of cold damage over time and it deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per your chance to chill.
You are granted the {Frenzy} buff for a short duration when you end Flame Rush.

You cast {Runic Invocation} when you exit Flame Rush, if you have it on your action bar.

This effect consumes mana equal to a percentage of {Runic Invocation's} mana cost.

Casting {Fireball} and then using Flame Rush in the same direction within 2 seconds causes that Flame Rush to deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area and grants you mana on cast.
Whenever you hit an enemy with {Fireball} during Flame Rush it causes a {Runic Burst} around the target.
Flame Rush now applies {Fire Resistance Shred} on enemies it passes through.

Whenever you pass through an ignited enemy you gain ward and consume all your ignite stacks on that enemy. 

Consuming your ignite stacks this way grants you ward per your ignite stack on them, but they don't deal their remaining damage.

Whenever you pass through an ignited enemy with Flame Rush, instead of gaining ward per stack of ignite on the enemy and consuming your ignite stacks without dealing damage, you lose 12 ward per stack of ignite and the ignite stacks instantly deal their remaining damage on the enemy.

If you do not have enough ward for this effect, a percentage of your remaining health is consumed instead, up to a maximum. Ignite stacks consumed this way deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per point of intelligence.
You travel faster and further with Flame Rush.
Flame Rush deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area.
Whenever you pass through a {Frost Wall} during Flame Rush it causes the explosion to deal damage in a much larger area and its chance to critically hit is doubled.
Every 0.4 seconds during Flame Rush you create a {Rune Ember}, up to a maximum. Every 0.25 seconds while not in Flame Rush you fire a {Rune Ember} at a nearby enemy if you have any.
{Rune Embers} deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Casting Flame Rush creates additional {Rune Embers}.
{Rune Embers} now pierce enemies.
You now have a chance to create a {Rune Ember} on kill during Flame Rush and you create {Rune Embers} faster during Flame Rush.
Flame Ward's retaliation hits deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Flame Ward's retaliation hits in a larger area.
Flame Ward grants armor while active.
Flame Ward grants additional block chance, block effectiveness, and ward on block while active.
Flame Ward's retaliation hits have a higher chance to freeze enemies.
Flame Ward costs less mana. Flame Ward and Fire Aura last longer.
{Fire Aura} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers). This also effects Fire Aura from sources other than Flame Ward.
Flame Ward grants ward over time while active.
Flame Ward grants increased ward retention while active.

Flame Ward grants additional fire damage to attacks and spells while active.

If Flame Ward is converted to cold or lightning, then this added damage is also converted.
Flame Ward grants additional ward.
You take less damage from hits while Flame Ward is active (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Flame Ward reduces elemental damage taken while active (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Flame Ward costs less mana.
Flame Ward grants increased mana regeneration while active.
Flame Ward grants additional ward, but has a shorter duration.
Flame Ward grants significantly more ward, but no longer retaliates.
Flame Ward grants additional ward equal to a percentage of your missing health.
Flame Ward grants more ward, but has a longer cooldown.

Flame Ward grants increased fire damage while active

If Flame Ward is converted to cold or lightning, then this damage increase is also converted.
You have {Haste} while Flame Ward is active.
Flame Ward's retaliation is centered on the enemy that hits you, but it hits in a smaller area.

Flame Ward is converted to Cold. This converts the base damage of the retaliation, granting it a freeze rate, and converts any fire damage modifiers from nodes on this tree.

This also globally converts {Fire Aura} to cold and grants it a Freeze Rate.

Flame Ward is converted to Lightning, unless it has been converted to cold. This converts the base damage of the retaliation, and any fire damage modifiers from nodes on this tree.

This also globally converts {Fire Aura} to lightning, unless it has been converted to cold.
While active, Flame Ward grants you {Fire Aura}, a spell that deals fire damage over time to surrounding enemies.

Flame Ward can be cast on allies.

This has no effect if you have taken the {Mental Aegis} node.
Flame Ward is cast when you are stunned, if it is off cooldown and you have positive mana.
Mana now absorbs damage while Flame Ward is active. Each point of mana shields 5 health and ward.
Flame Ward can store an additional charge, allowing you to use it twice before it goes on cooldown.
Enemies around you are knocked back when you use Flame Ward
Flay deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), and has a chance to chill on hit.
Every second melee attack with Flay which hits a boss or rare enemy has a chance to trigger a Blood Eruption.
Flay deals more hit damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), doubled against low life enemies.
Enemies hit by Flay are inflicted with Marked for Death for a duration, and Flay hits instantly kill enemies that are below a health threshold (a percentage of their maximum health).
While using a dagger, Flay has increased attack speed.

When you directly use Flay and hit at least one enemy, you have a chance to gain Haste for 2 seconds. This chance is tripled if Flay is a traversal skill.

Flay's Spirit Step can move you from a further distance.

When you directly use Flay and hit at least one enemy with the melee attack, your active {Aura of Decay} is granted more ailment frequency (multiplicative with other modifiers) and increased radius for 4 seconds.

Hitting a boss or rare enemy with Flay's melee attack transfers your active Aura of Decay to them for up to 6 seconds.

While the aura is attached to an enemy, you can move away from it to avoid the self-inflicted ailments.
Flay has increased area of effect and additional spell damage. These effects are doubled if there is a bleeding enemy within 15 meters.
A percentage of the melee damage on your equipped axes or daggers is also applied as spell damage for the Blood Eruption.

Flay and all skills triggered by its skill tree deal more spell damage per 100 maximum mana. 
Flay has additional mana cost.

Directly using Flay and hitting at least one enemy with the melee attack has a chance per 1 maximum mana to release a {Chaos Bolt} towards an enemy.
This effect consumes 2 mana per Chaos Bolt released.
Flay has additional critical strike chance, and leeches a percentage of damage dealt as health.

Flay has additional critical strike multiplier.
Critically hitting at least one enemy with Flay's melee attack has a chance to release Marrow Shards towards another nearby enemy within 6 meters.
Flay inflicts {Exposed Flesh} on hit, a Curse which reduces cold resistance and armor, and increases the target's chance to be frozen.
Blood Eruption has increased area of effect and deals more hit damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) for each of your curses that were on the enemy.
Blood Eruption consumes mana to release a Wandering Spirit. This effect can trigger a limited number of times per 4 seconds.
Blood Eruption spreads all bleeds on that enemy to a limited amount of other enemies within its range.
Flay has a chance to inflict bleed on hit, and bleeds inflicted by Flay have increased duration.

Flay deals more hit damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to chilled or frozen enemies.

Flay has additional freeze rate per active skeleton or bone golem.
If you directly use Flay and hit at least one enemy, but do not hit a boss or rare enemy, a spectral copy of Flay strikes a random nearby boss or rare enemy.

Directly using Flay and hitting at least one enemy has a chance to trigger a spectral copy of Flay against a nearby enemy if one would not be triggered otherwise.

Spectral copies of Flay have increased area of effect.

Directly using Flay consumes a percentage of your current health and ward. Flay gains additional melee damage per 30 health consumed and additional spell damage per 30 ward consumed by this effect, up to a limited amount of each.

Flay deals more melee damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Directly using Flay and hitting at least one enemy reduces the remaining cooldown of Reaper Form by a percentage. This can occur a limited number of times per use of Reaper Form or Reap.

Flay has a chance to inflict bleed on hit, and Flay deals more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Flay has less attack speed (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Flay is now a traversal skill with a cooldown. The Spirit Step can move you from a much further distance and no longer requires a target.
The Spirit Step is removed, and Flay has increased attack speed.

Flay's melee attack is removed. Flay becomes a spell that directly creates the Blood Eruption, and is now affected by cast speed instead of attack speed.

Any modifiers to Flay's attack speed in its skill tree instead apply to cast speed.

Flay's base damage is converted to cold and it now has a chance to freeze. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not increases to physical damage.

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to frostbite chance.

This node has no effect if {Soul Ripper} is allocated.

Flay's base damage is converted to necrotic. Consequently this damage scales with increases to necrotic damage, but not increases to physical damage.

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to damned chance.

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to poison chance.

This node has no effect if {Soul Ripper} or {Frostblight} are allocated.

When you create a {Blood Eruption}, you gain a stack of {Blood Revelry}, up to 5. Direct uses of Harvest or Rip Blood can consume a stack of Blood Revelry to deal more damage and gain increased area of effect.

Blood Eruptions have a significantly reduced delay before erupting.

Flay deals more melee damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has a lower mana cost.

Flay has less attack speed (multiplicative with other modifiers).
A bow or melee skill that performs 3 rapid attacks.
Flurry has increased attack speed.
You have increased attack speed for each hit you dealt with your previous flurry, up to a maximum.
Flurry deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifers).
Flurry leeches a portion of the damage it deals as health.
Each stack of {Adrenaline Rush} grants a chance to inflict bleed on hit.
Eack stack of {Adrenaline Rush} grants Flurry more hit damage against bosses and rare enemies.
{Adrenaline Rush} lasts longer.
{Adrenaline Rush} can have multiple stacks.
Flurry deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) for each second channelled up to a maximum.
You have additional physical and elemental resistances while channelling Flurry.
If you are using a bow you fire arrows faster, but they are less accurate.
Bleed inflicted by Flurry has additional Physical Penetration.
Poison inflicted by Flurry has additional Poison Penetration.
The {Force Waves} travel further.
Improves Flurry's base critical strike chance.
Flurry critical strikes deal more damage.
Critical strikes restore health. The amount of health gained is increased by Dexterity.
Flurry grants health when hitting at least one enemy.
Flurry grants mana when hitting at least one enemy.
Flurry's third strike Slows enemies hit.
Flurry's first and second strike deal less damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but the third strike deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Damaging ailments inflicted by Flurry deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Flurry is now a channelled ability that costs mana per second to maintain.
If you are using a bow, every 6th arrow is replaced with {Multishot}.

Flurry strikes have a chance to grant you {Adrenaline Rush}, granting Flurry 5% more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and 5% increased attack speed. This lasts for 4 seconds, can stack up to 4 times.
If you are using a melee weapon Flurry now causes {Force Waves} to blast forward with each strike, dealing melee physical damage.
Enemies killed by Flurry have a chance to release a {Kinetic Burst} around the target, dealing physical damage.
Focus grants increased health regeneration while channeled.
Focus grants increased mana regeneration while channeled proportional to the amount of mana you are missing when you start channeling.
Casting Focus while at negative mana instantly grants mana equal to a percentage of your maximum mana, but Focus has a longer cooldown.
The mana gained from the Null Profusion and Null Infusion nodes is also gained as ward, but Focus has a longer cooldown.
Focus grants increased mana regeneration while channeled.
Focus grants a chance to shock nearby enemies each second while channeled and causes you to take less damage from shocked enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Shocks, chills and ignites inflicted by Focus have a longer duration.
Focus grants a chance to ignite nearby enemies each second while channeled and causes you to take less damage from ignited enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Focus heightens your armor while channeling (multiplicative with other modifiers) and grants you additional armor based on your intelligence.
Focus grants less elemental damage taken while channeled (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Focus heals the target each second while channeled.
Focus can increase your current mana to a percentage above your maximum mana. The excess decays at a rate of 10 mana per second after you stop channeling.
Focus grants additional resistance while channeled.
Focus grants additional ward retention while channeled.
Focus grants additional ward per second while channeled.
You gain ward equal to a percentage of the mana you gained once you finish channeling.
{Energy Overflow} is cast more frequently.
The waves of lightning released by the Energy Overflow and Energy Battery nodes deal more damage while you are at full mana (multiplicative with other modifiers).
When you finish channeling, you unleash a wave of lightning with base damage equal to a percentage of the mana you gained while channeling.
The burst of ward and mana after channelling for exactly one second grants additional ward and mana.

Focus no longer has a cooldown, but the burst no longer grants mana.

This node has no effect if you have either the {Null Profusion} node or the {Conveyance} node.
Casting Focus while at negative mana instantly grants mana equal to a percentage of your maximum mana, but Focus has a longer cooldown.
Focus grants a chance to chill nearby enemies each second while channeled and causes you to take less damage from chilled enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Focus has increased healing effectiveness per point of intelligence.
Focus can be cast on allies.
You gain Haste for 1 second per 20 mana you gained while channeling once you finish channeling. Maximum of 10 seconds.
You unleash waves of lightning with base damage equal to a percentage of your maximum mana to nearby enemies each second while channeling.

You teleport to the target location when you start channelling Focus, as long as the location is accessible. This does not count as casting the Teleport skill and is not affected by Teleport's skill tree.

Focus now counts as a {Traversal} skill.
Improves Forge Strike's base critical chance, and critical strikes deal more damage.
Forge Strike has increased cooldown recovery speed and attack speed. The attack speed bonus also applies to your Forged Weapons.
You are granted ward when Forge Strike summons a {Forged Weapon}.
Forge Strike and {Forged Weapons} deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and have an increased chance to stun enemies.
Forge Strike hits reduce enemy armor, and armor shred applied by it last longer with increased effect.
You deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) if you have used Forge Strike recently (last 4 seconds).
You have increased armor if you have used Forge Strike recently (last 4 seconds).
Forge Strike and {Forged Weapons'} hits deal damage in a larger area
Forge Strike deals additional melee fire damage and more fire damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
{Detonating Ground} hits deal more damage.
{Detonating Ground} has a chance to ignite enemies on hit.

{Forged Weapons} deal additional melee fire damage.

{Forged Weapons} gain the {fire} tag.
{Forged Weapons} last longer and you can control more forged weapons at once.
Forge Strike has a higher chance to summon {Forged Weapons}. This added chance is much higher against bosses and rare enemies.
{Forged Weapons} have a much shorter duration, but deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), and explode when they expire, dealing fire and physical damage to nearby enemies.
Bleed inflicted by Forge Strike has additional Physical Penetration and lasts longer.
You have additional global Fire Penetration if you have used Forge Strike recently (last 4 seconds).
{Forged Weapons} no longer move or attack, but generate ward when you are near them.
Improves Forge Strike's base critical chance and its hits inflict bleeding on hit, but your maximum number of {Forged Weapons} is halved.

Converts Forge Strike into a sword that falls from the sky instead. Forge Strike has more attack speed (multiplicative with other modifiers), cannot crit, and critical strike multiplier from the tree is converted to additional Physical Penetration for Bleed.

Converts to a polearm if you also have the {Spear Forge} node.

Converts Forge Strike into a spear that falls from the sky instead. Forge Strike critical strikes deal significantly more damage, but it hits in a smaller area.

Converts to a polearm if you also have the {Put To The Sword} node.
Forge Strike can now be cast at your target instead of in front of you.
Forge Strike hits deal more damage against rare and boss enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Converts Forge Strike into an anvil that falls from the sky instead. Forge Strike has an increased chance to stun enemies, and stuns it inflicts lasts longer, but it has less attack speed (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Additionally, when you directly use Forge Strike it deals more physical damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Forge Strike causes {Detonating Ground}, sending out cracks which erupt after 1.5 seconds, dealing melee fire damage and igniting enemies.
{Forged Weapons} now have a chance to become a {Great Axe} on summon, which deal more damage in a larger area, but attack slower.
Forge Strike has no cooldown, but costs additional mana.
Ailments inflicted by Forge Strike deal increased damage over time equal to your increased stun chance.
Frost Claw has a higher chance to freeze enemies and a higher chance to critically strike.
Frost Claw deals additional spell damage based on your added melee damage.
Frost Claw deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and its explosion deals damage in a larger area. These bonuses are doubled if you have at least 1200 ward.
Directly casting Frost Claw costs less mana and you have a chance to regain mana when you cast it.

Frost Claw's base cold damage is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to cold damage. 

Chill chance from all sources is converted to ignite chance.

Swaps Frost Claw's {Cold} tag for a {Fire} tag.

Frost Claw's base cold damage is converted to lightning, unless it has already been converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to lightning damage, but not increases to cold damage. 

Frostbite chance from all sources is converted to shock chance.

Swaps Frost Claw's {Cold} tag for a {Lightning} tag.
You have increased cast speed with Frost Claw.
Frost Claw deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), and its hits have a chance to inflict {Frostbite}.
Frostbite, chill, slow, ignite, and shock inflicted by Frost Claw last longer.

You have a chance to cast {Elemental Nova} when Frost Claw's projectiles reach the target.

This consumes mana equal to Elemental Nova's mana cost and cannot trigger if you are out of mana.
Frost Claw has a higher chance to freeze based on its critical strike chance.
If you have an active {Ice Barrage} when you hit an enemy with Frost Claw, that Ice Barrage fires faster for the rest of its duration, up to a maximum.

You have a chance to cast {Ice Barrage} in the target direction when you cast Frost Claw.

This consumes mana equal to Ice Barrage's mana cost and cannot trigger if you are out of mana.

When you are hit by an enemy you have a chance to retaliate with Frost Claw.

When you cast Frost Claw by any method you gain Ward.
When you cast Frost Claw you gain ward based on its Freeze Rate Multiplier.

When you use a melee attack and hit at least one enemy you have a chance to cast Frost Claw towards it, or at another nearby enemy if the hit killed the original target.

This effect consumes mana equal to a portion of Frost Claw's mana cost.
Gain a stack of Frozen Sleeper every second up to a maximum. When you cast Frost Claw it consumes all stacks to gain cold penetration per stack and release an {Ice Spiral} for each stack.
When you cast {Ice Spirals} there is a chance that the number of projectiles will be doubled and they'll all deal double damage.
For each active {Ice Spiral}, Glacier gains increased area and additional cold penetration, and Snap Freeze gains increased area and freeze duration.

Directly casting Frost Claw causes it to recast from the target location to a location 4 meters behind it. The second can hit the same enemies as the first cast.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.

The second cast from {On Through The Snow} causes a third cast. The third cast can hit the same enemies as the first and second casts.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.

The second cast from {On Through The Snow} targets a random enemy that is 3 to 9 meters away from the original target location.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.

When the second cast from {On Through The Snow} is released, Frost Claw is also cast back towards you.

Frost Claw's mana cost is increased.

Frost Claw has 2 additional projectiles allowing it to hit enemies in a wider area on the way to the target.

Frost Claw costs additional mana.

Frost Claw no longer creates a burst of damage at the target, but its projectiles are now staggered and they can all hit the same target.

Frost Claw's mana cost is increased, it no longer has the {Area} tag and it has a lowered base freeze rate.
Frost Claw has a lower mana cost and it deals more hit damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to frozen enemies, but its projectiles are slower.
Frost Claw's projectiles are faster and it costs less mana.
Frost Wall has a chance to inflict a stack of Frostbite to enemies inside the wall every 0.25 seconds.

One of Frost Wall's {Pillars} now casts {Lightning Blast} at nearby enemies when you first pass through the wall.

This effect consumes mana equal to {Lightning Blast's} mana cost.

Frost Wall's outer {Pillars} cast {Lightning Blast} at nearby enemies every other second.

This effect consumes mana equal to {Lightning Blast's} mana cost.
After Frost Wall has existed for a short duration, its outer {Pillars} cast {Lightning Blast} more often, up to a maximum, every time an enemy passes through the wall.

After you pass through your Frost Wall your next {Runic Invocation} or {Glyph of Dominion} cast within 5 seconds is empowered. 

An empowered spell cast this way, costs less mana (multiplicative with other modifiers) and it also deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) for each second the wall has existed, before you walk through it.
Frost Wall now cleanses negative ailments on you and your allies when they first pass through the wall.
Frost Wall deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has a higher chance to freeze enemies.

Frost Wall now grants you and your allies ward and mana when they first pass through the wall.

These effects are doubled if the wall is placed at least 2 meters away from you.

Frost Wall now grants the {Haste} and {Frenzy} buffs for a short duration, up to a maximum, when you and your allies when they first pass through the wall.

The maximum duration is equal to Frost Wall's age plus 1 second.
Frost Wall now creates three {Runic Detonations} shortly after you pass through it, dealing spell cold damage to nearby enemies.
The {Runic Detonations} from {Runic Conclusion} intensify, destroying the wall in the process and deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area.
The {Runic Detonations} deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area if you are facing away from the wall when the detonations occur.

You now cast Flame Ward on you or your allies (excluding their minions) that pass through your Frost Wall, if it has existed for at least 0.2 seconds.

Frost Wall now has a cooldown, which it shares with Flame Ward. Using one will put the other on cooldown.
If you haven't cast Frost Wall in the past 15 seconds, it has no mana cost and deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Frost Wall is larger and lasts longer.
You deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to chilled enemies while you have an active Frost Wall.

Frost Wall deals more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers) per second the wall has existed.

This does not affect ailments or subskills.

When you cast Frost Wall while having an existing wall active with more than half its duration remaining, the existing wall is moved to the new target location instead of being replaced and you gain mana equal to half the Frost Wall’s mana cost.
Frost Wall deals additional spell damage equal to a portion of your freeze rate multiplier.

Frost Wall's initial freeze has increased duration, and the wall deals more damage over time to frozen enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).

This does not affect ailments or subskills.
You are granted ward whenever enemies pass through the wall for the first time.

Whenever an enemy passes through the wall for the first time, and after it has existed for 0.2 seconds, you gain ward equal to a small portion of the enemy's maximum health, up to a maximum amount of ward per enemy.

{Frost Wall's} base cold damage is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to cold damage.

For Frost Wall, chill chance from all sources is converted to {Ignite}, and Frostbite chance from all sources is converted to {Fire Resistance Shred}.

Swaps {Frost Wall's} {Cold} tag for a {Fire} tag.

Frost Wall now inflicts Brand of Trespass to enemies inside the wall. This Brand does not stack, but deals a large amount of fire damage over time.

Brand of Trespass deals more damage equal to your ignite chance.

Whenever an enemy passes through your Frost Wall, you have a chance to cast {Fireball} towards them.

This effect consumes mana equal to {Fireball's} mana cost.

{Frost Wall's} base cold damage is converted to lightning. Consequently this damage scales with increases to lightning damage, but not increases to cold damage.

For Frost Wall, chill chance from all sources is converted to {Slow}, and Frostbite chance from all sources is converted to {Shock}.

Swaps Frost Wall's {Cold} tag for a {Lightning} tag.

Frost Wall now casts {Runebolt} in the original target direction the Wall was cast. The element of the bolts match the element of the Wall, regardless of {Runebolt's} tree.

This effect consumes 2 mana per projectile and does not cast if you are at negative mana.
Frost Wall casts {Runebolt} more often.
Fury Leap's critical strikes deal more damage.

You have a chance to reset Fury Leap's cooldown whenever you kill an enemy with any other skill.

Fury Leap's cooldown can only be reset once every 5 seconds.
{Storm Bolts} occur with increased frequency.

You have additional spell damage per 2 Attunement while leaping, and kills with {Storm Bolt} have a chance to reset Fury Leap's cooldown.

Fury Leap's cooldown can only be reset once every 5 seconds.
When you land, you and your minions gain additional melee damage and spell damage for 3 seconds. This buff can stack.
When you land, you and your minions gain {Frenzy} for 3 seconds, which increases attack and cast speed. Frenzy cannot stack.
Fury Leap has an increased chance to stun enemies and stuns it inflicts last longer.
Fury Leap pulls enemies when you land.
Fury Leap has a chance to cast {Summon Vines} where you land.
Fury Leap's deals damage in a larger area and costs less mana.
Fury Leap hits deal more damage against enemies at full health (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Fury Leap deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and you leech a portion of the damage it deals as health.
While you are below half health, Fury Leap always critically strikes.

Fury Leap's cooldown is reset when you kill an enemy with a short leap.

Fury Leap's cooldown can only be reset once every 5 seconds.
Your Wolf, Sabertooth, and Raptor companions leap with you.
Fury Leap has a chance to cast {Summon Vines} where you leap from.
Fury Leap creates {Storm Bolts} while you are leaping.
Fury Leap deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has a shorter cooldown.
When you land with Fury Leap you cast Upheaval. This consumes Upheaval's mana cost and requires positive mana.
You are immune to damage while leaping.
Fury Leap deals more damage based on how far you leap.
When you land, you and your minions are healed.
When you land you cleanse all ailments on you and your minions.
Whenever you expend a {Storm Stack} you have a chance to cast {Storm Bolt} at an additional enemy.
Gathering Storm deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and you expend {Storm Stacks} more frequently, but {Storm Bolts} target enemies within a smaller area.
Whenever you expend a {Storm Stack} you have a chance to cast {Maelstrom}.
skill_nodes.display_name: In the Eye of the Storm

skill_nodes.description_text: Whenever you expend a {Storm Stack} you have a chance to gain a stack instead of losing one.
Whenever you expend a {Storm Stack} you have a chance to gain {Haste}.

Whenever you use a companion ability you expend {Storm Stacks} over the next 0.3 seconds.

Storm Bolts triggered this way deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Whenever you hit a boss or rare enemy with a {Storm Bolt} you have a chance to cast it again at the same enemy after 0.25 seconds.
While wielding a staff the melee attack of Gathering Storm is replaced by a direct ranged cast of {Storm Bolt}, but Gathering Storm costs additional mana.
{Storm Bolts} target enemies from farther away.
If you have from 1 to 15 {Storm Stacks}, your minions' lightning skill hits have a chance to grant you a {Storm Stack}.
Gathering Storm penetrates enemy lightning resistance.
{Storm Bolts} now consume a portion of your current mana to deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per 10 current mana, up to a maximum.
Whenever you expend a {Storm Stack} while dual wielding you now cast {Storm Bolts} at two enemies instead of one.
Gathering Storm has increased attack speed.
Whenever you directly use Gathering Storm you gain mana per active {Storm Stack} up to a maximum.
You have increased global stun chance and additional freeze rate multiplier per active {Storm Stack} up to a maximum.
Gathering Storm's hits have a chance to consume mana to grant you additional {Storm Stacks}.
Gathering Storm's melee attack deals damage in a larger area and you have a chance to gain an additional {Storm Stack} if you hit at least 3 enemies with the same melee attack.

Whenever you use Gathering Storm's and hit at least one enemy you gain a stack of {Wings of Lightning}.

Each time one of your {Storm Crows} attack they consume a stack of Wings of Lightning causing their basic attack to chain 2 additional times.

{Storm Bolts'} hits have chance to shock enemies.

Gathering Storm deals more melee damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to shocked enemies.

Gathering Storm deals additional spell damage per 3 melee damage on your equipped axe or mace.

The added spell damage granted by this effect is significantly reduced when applied to a Storm Totem through its {Unmatched Storms}.

Gathering Storm's and {Storm Bolt's} base lightning damage is converted to physical. Consequently this damage scales with increases to physical damage, but not increases to lightning damage.

Shock chance on hit from all sources is converted to armor shred chance for Gathering Storm.

Swaps Gathering Storm's {Lightning} tag for a {Physical} tag.
Gathering Storm deals additional melee damage per Strength while wielding a 2 handed weapon.

Gathering Storm's and {Storm Bolt's} base lightning damage is converted to cold. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not increases to lightning damage.

Shock chance on hit from all sources is converted to frostbite chance for Gathering Storm.

Swaps Gathering Storm's {Lightning} tag for a {Cold} tag.
Gathering Storm has a higher chance to freeze enemies per 2 Attunement and it also has additional cold penetration for frostbite per 2 Attunement.
Gathering Storm has improved base critical strike chance per stack of {Maelstrom} on you from any source.

Ghostflames's base necrotic and fire damage is converted to physical. Consequently this damage scales with increases to physical damage, but not increases to necrotic or fire damage.

Damned and ignite chance from all sources are converted to bleed chance per second.

Ghostflame now drains a portion of your health each second.

Swaps Ghostflames's {Necrotic} and {Fire} tags for a {Physical} tag.

You leech a portion of the physical damage you deal as health while channeling Ghostflame.

A portion of your global bleed chance on hit now applies as bleed chance per second for Ghostflame.

Damaging an enemy with Ghostflame causes you to cast {Marrow Shards} towards them.

This effect can occur a maximum number of times each second.

Increases the frequency of {Marrow Shards} per second from the {Feast of Bones} node.

Improves the {Marrow Shards'} base critical strike chance cast this way.
Ghostflame has increased ignite and damned duration per point of Intelligence.

A portion of your global ignite chance on hit now applies as ignite chance per second for Ghostflame.

A portion of your global damned chance on hit now applies as damned chance per second for Ghostflame.

After channelling Ghostflame for a short duration, the skull releases a terrible screech, cursing all nearby enemies with {Decrepify}.

Ghost Flame gains the {Curse} tag, but its direct damage still does not scale with increased curse damage.
The screech from the {Decrepit Mortals} node has a shorter delay before being released and a larger area.
The screech from the {Decrepit Mortals} node now also fears enemies and Ghostflame deals more direct damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to feared enemies.
Ghostflame deals more damage, but has a higher channel cost (both multiplicative with other modifiers).
You take less damage from ignited, damned or bleeding enemies while channeling Ghostflame, but it costs more mana to channel.
Ghostflame deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area.

You leech a portion of the damage you deal as health against {Cursed} enemies while channeling Ghostflame.

Additionally, Ghostflame has a chance to inflict Frailty each second.

After channeling for 1.1 seconds Ghostflame detaches itself, and persists for short duration without you needing to channel.

This effect ends early if you start channeling Ghostflame again.
The duration from the {Memorial of Flames} node is longer, but Ghostflame now has an upfront mana cost.
The detached Ghostflame now slowly seeks out nearby enemies.
After channeling for 1 second, Ghostflame consumes a portion of your current ward, and it deals damage in a larger area per ward consumed.

For each 200 ward consumed by the {Silver Oblation} node you now cast a {Stygian Beam} at a nearby enemy dealing spell necrotic damage.

Enemies cannot be hit by more than 1 beam.

All your {Wraiths}, {Spectres}, and {Revenants} gain a stack of {Spirit Kindling} every 0.5 seconds while they are inside Ghostflame's area of effect.

Each stack of {Spirit Kindling} lasts 5 seconds, drains health per second, and grants increased movement speed, melee attack speed, and ignite chance equal to a portion of your ignite chance.
Ghostflame now deals damage in a full circle around you, but it has a higher channel cost.
You now move towards the target location while channeling Ghostflame with increased movement speed, but it has a higher channel cost.
Ghostflame has a lower channel cost, but you move slower while channeling Ghostflame.
You move faster while channeling Ghostflame.
Ghostflame deals more direct damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per second you spend channeling Ghostflame.
Ghostflame has a lower channel cost and you gain a portion of your current mana as ward each second while channeling Ghostflame.
You gain additional dodge rating per intelligence while channeling Ghostflame, as well as more dodge rating (multiplicative with other modifiers) while channeling Ghostflame.
All of your dodge rating is converted to armor while channeling Ghostflame.
Each of Glacier’s explosions has a chance to chill enemies.
Glacier’s smallest explosion deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Glacier’s middle-sized explosion deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Glacier’s largest explosion deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Glacier’s smallest explosion has a chance to chill enemies.
Glacier’s middle-sized explosion deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has a chance to chill enemies.
Glacier’s largest explosion deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has a chance to chill enemies.
Glacier’s smallest explosion has a chance to create an {Ice Vortex}, which lasts for 4 seconds and deals spell cold damage over time.
Glacier’s middle-sized explosion has a greater chance to create an {Ice Vortex}, and the {Rime} buff has increased duration.
Glacier’s largest explosion has a greater chance to create an {Ice Vortex}, and the {Rime} buff has increased effect.
When you cast Glacier you gain the {Rime} buff which increases your damage over time and freeze rate multiplier.
Glacier’s middle-sized explosion deals significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but Glacier costs more mana.
Glacier has an increased chance to freeze and stun enemies.
Critical hits with Glacier grant mana equal to a percentage of your maximum mana, up to a limited number of times per second.
Glacier kills grant mana equal to a percentage of your maximum mana.
Greatly improves Glacier’s base critical strike chance, but reduces its critical strike multiplier.
Glacier has a higher chance to freeze enemies, but deals less damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Glacier costs less mana and deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
You gain ward on critical strikes with Glacier.
Each of Glacier’s explosions has a chance to create an {Ice Vortex}, which lasts for 4 seconds and deals spell cold damage over time.
Glacier costs less mana, but the largest explosion is disabled.
Glacier’s explosions occur in reverse order.
All of Glacier’s explosions occur at a target location, but Glacier's critical hits no longer deal extra damage (compared to non-critical hits).
{Ice Vortices} created by Glacier have a chance to instead become a {Greater Ice Vortex} that deals significantly more damage and has a larger radius.
Glacier deals more hit damage to rare and boss enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Chill chance granted by this tree is doubled. Glacier hits deal more damage to chilled enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).

When you directly cast Glacier you are teleported to the target location, as long as the location is accessible. This does not count as casting the Teleport skill and is not affected by Teleport's skill tree.

Glacier also gains a cooldown and now counts as a {Traversal} skill.
The {Glyph} explosion deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per stack of slow on the target.
Glyph of Dominion deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and costs less mana.

You can now place down up to two Glyphs of Dominion, but Glyph of Dominion costs more mana. When the first {Glyph} explodes the second one does too. The second explosion deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) the further it is from the first one up to a maximum.
Glyph of Dominion expands slower and lasts a longer (both multiplicative with other modifiers). This increases the time before it reaches its full size to detonate.
Glyph of Dominion expands faster and lasts a shorter duration (both multiplicative with other modifiers). This decreases the time before it reaches its full size to detonate.

Glyph of Dominion expands faster and lasts a shorter duration (both multiplicative with other modifiers) per amount of missing mana. This decreases the time before it reaches its full size to detonate.
After Glyph of Dominion its full size it now has a short delay before it detonates.

Glyph of Dominion now has a cooldown, which is refreshed whenever you directly cast {Runic Invocation}. It also deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per rune from {Runic Invocation}, without consuming them.

Glyph of Dominion now consumes the {Runes} from {Runic Invocation} on cast to gain additional effects per {Rune} you have. 

{Rah Rune}: Glyph deals more damage in a larger area per {Rune} of this type.
{Gon Rune}: Glyph detonates additional times during the delay per {Rune} of this type.
{Heo Rune}: Glyph inflicts stacks of Frostbite per second per {Rune} of this type.
Glyph of Dominion has additional charges and all charges are refreshed whenever you directly cast {Runic Invocation}.
You cast {Elemental Nova} in the middle of the {Glyph} if you stand inside it when the delay from {Doom Scribe} starts and you gain stacks of {Fire Aura}.
Glyph of Dominion's damage over time part deals more damage for each second it is active (multiplicative with other modifiers).
While standing on your {Glyph} you and your allies deal more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers) per stack of Armor Shred on the enemy, up to a maximum of 14 stacks.
Glyph of Dominion deals more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers).
{Disintegrate} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and grants you ward per second if you start channelling it while standing on your {Glyph}.
Glyph of Dominion deals damage in a larger area.
Glyph of Dominion is now cast at your current location and it grants you and your allies ward per second per your uncapped resistance while standing on your {Glyph}.
You and your allies gain additional resistances while standing on your {Glyph}.
After casting Glyph of Dominion you gain {Static Charges} at an accelerating rate while standing still, until your {Glyph} detonates.

Whenever you or your allies enters your Glyph of Dominion it cleanses ailments and grants the {Haste} buff for a short duration.

Leaving and re-entering the same {Glyph} does not cause this effect to trigger again.
You and your allies gain additional Shock chance while standing on your {Glyph}.
Normal and Magic enemies have a chance to be stunned every half second while on your {Glyph}.
Your {Lightning Blast} now chains while you are standing on your {Glyph}, but consumes mana when cast this way while you are at positive mana.
You and your allies gain increased area to spells and attacks while standing on your {Glyph}.

{Glyph of Dominion's} base lightning damage is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to lightning damage.

Shock chance from all sources is converted to ignite for Glyph of Dominion and effects related to shock now depend on ignite instead.

Swaps {Glyph of Dominion's} {Lightning} tag for a {Fire} tag.

Ignite chance is halved for minions and other allies.
Glyph of Dominion ignites enemies on the {Glyph} every half second.
The ignite frequency from {Burning Symbol} is increased by your chance to ignite.
Hail of Arrows deals damage in a larger area
You have a chance to gain a stack of {Dusk Shroud} each second for 2 seconds while inside the area, granting you glancing blow chance and dodge rating. This effect can stack.
Hail of Arrows now also grants a stack of {Dusk Shroud} to allies each second for 2 seconds while inside the area.
Improves {Barrage of Arrows'} base critical strike chance.
{Barrage of Arrows} immobilizes enemies for a short duration. Rare enemies and bosses are slowed instead.
Hail of Arrows has a chance to apply {Frailty} to enemies each second.
Hail of Arrows has a chance to shred enemy armor each second.
Hail of Arrows has a chance to inflict enemies with {Crit Vulnerability} each second.
Hail of Arrows deals more bow damage to ignited enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Hail of Arrows deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but in a smaller area (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Hail of Arrows leeches a portion of the bow damage it deals as health.
Hail of Arrows deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Hail of Arrows deals more bow damage to stunned enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers). This does not affect ailment damage.
Hail of Arrows costs less mana, but has a shorter duration (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Hail of Arrows costs less mana and has a shorter cooldown, but has a longer delay before the arrows fall.
Hail of Arrows has a chance to cause enemies to bleed each second.
Hail of Arrows deals more bow damage per stack of bleed on the enemy, up to a maximum. This does not affect ailment damage.
Hail of Arrows has a chance to slow enemies each second.
Hail of Arrows has increased chance to freeze enemies each second and has increased chill duration.
Hail of Arrows lasts longer.
Hail of Arrows deals more bow damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) over its duration scaling up to a maximum. This does not affect damaging ailments.

Hail of Arrows lasts significantly longer (multiplicative with other modifiers), but you can only have one Hail of Arrows active at a time. 

Using Hail of Arrows again causes the old instance to stop.
Hail of Arrows has a chance to poison enemies each second.
Hail of Arrows now also shoots a {Barrage of Arrows} on use dealing bow physical damage, but costs more mana. This does not scale with damage over time.

Hail of Arrows' base physical damage is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to physical damage.

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to ignite chance and effects related to bleed now depend on ignite instead.

Swaps Hail of Arrows' {Physical} tag for a {Fire} tag.

This has no effect if Hail of Arrows is converted to cold with {Crystal Arrows} or to poison with {Acid Rain}.
You can store additional charges of Hail of Arrows, but it has a shorter duration (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Hail of Arrows now fall in a forward advancing rectangle area rather than a circle.

Hail of Arrows' base physical damage is converted to cold. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not increases to physical damage. It also has a chance to freeze enemies each second.

All slow chance from this tree is converted to chill chance and effects related to slow now depend on chill instead.

Swaps Hail of Arrows' {Physical} tag for a {Cold} tag.

This has no effect if Hail of Arrows is converted to poison with {Acid Rain}.

Hail of Arrows is now channeled and can be retargeted while you are channelling. It costs less initial mana, but rapidly drains your mana.

Channelled Hail of Arrows' delay is halved, and increases to its delay are halved.

Hail of Arrows' base physical damage is converted to poison. Consequently this damage scales with increases to poison damage, but not increases to physical damage.

Swaps Hail of Arrows' {Physical} tag for a {Poison} tag.

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to poison chance.
Hammer Throw has increased attack speed.
Hammer Throw deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but hammers travels slower.

Hammer Throw can no longer spiral, chain, or orbit.

If your hammers would spiral, they instead deal significantly more hit damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

If your hammers would chain, they instead deal more critical damage for each time they would chain.

If your hammers would orbit you, they instead have additional base critical strike chance.

Hammer Throw hits deal increased damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
When you are out of mana Hammer Throw is free.
Hammer Throw hits have a chance to reduce enemy armor.
{Disintegrating Aura} deals more damage, and Hammer Throw costs less mana.
Hammer Throw deals more damage, and has a higher chance to stun enemies.
Hammer Throw deals more damage, but Hammer Throw hits no longer pierce (travel through enemies hit). Its hits have a higher chance to stun enemies, and the stuns they inflict last longer.
Hammers chain to additional enemies.
Hammer Throw deals more hit damage against stunned enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers) and the stuns they inflict last longer
Hammer Throw has a chance to grant you health on hit.
Hammer Throw rips a {Dark Orb} from enemies on hit, which travels back to you and grants 6 health and mana. Hammer Throw has a 5 second cooldown.
Hammers travel faster.
Hammer Throw hits have a chance to deal double damage, but hammers no longer return to you.
Hammer Throw deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and costs less mana.
Hammer Throw hits have a higher chance to deal double damage.
Using Hammer Throw grants you a stack of {Zeal} for 4 seconds, which increases your damage and critical strike chance by 10% per stack.
Each stack of {Zeal} also grants you added global ignite chance.

Your hammers gain a {Disintegrating Aura}, causing enemies nearby them to take void damage over time, but Hammer Throw costs additional mana and has less attack speed (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Hammers chain to nearby enemies instead of returning to you. Hammer Throw can no longer throw extra projectiles, but it gains more damage and more attack speed (both multiplicative with other modifiers) for each extra projectile you would have thrown.

Hammer Throw launches additional hammers, but Hammer Throw costs more mana.

Hammers from the same throw cannot hit the same enemy, unless they spiral.
Hammers are now thrown in a nova, with double the amount of additional hammers unless your hammers spiral. Hammer Throw hits deal additional throwing physical damage.
Hammers now travel in a spiral around you, but halves your amount of additional hammers and Hammer Throw costs more mana.
Your spiralling hammers now orbit you, but Hammer Throw costs more mana.
Harvest hits have a chance to reduce enemy necrotic resistance, and Harvest deals more damage over time.

Harvest grants you health each time it hits a cursed enemy.

Health gained scales with your intelligence stat.
Harvest has increased area of effect, and is increased further if using a two handed weapon.
Improves Harvest's base critical strike chance, and increases Harvest's attack speed.
You have increased critical strike chance and critical strike multiplier if you have hit a {Cursed} enemy with Harvest recently (past 4 seconds).
Critical hits with Harvest have a chance to grant Haste for 2 seconds.

Harvest grants you ward when it hits a {Cursed} enemy. This effect can occur a limited number of times per 5 seconds.
Harvest also deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
When you directly use Harvest, you restore mana for each enemy killed or each boss or rare enemy hit.

When Harvest kills an enemy or hits at least one boss or rare enemy, you have a chance to gain a stack of {Stolen Spirit}, up to 5.

At 5 stacks, your next Harvest hit against a boss or rare enemy consumes all stacks to release a Wandering Spirit per stack.
Harvest hits have a chance to poison enemies.
Harvest hits deal more hit damage per unique ailment on the target (multiplicative with other modifiers), up to a maximum benefit.
Harvest has a chance to cause enemies to bleed.

When you hit an enemy with Harvest both you and the enemy are inflicted with bleed. Bleed stacks on enemies inflicted by Harvest have additional Physical Penetration.

The self-inflicted bleed does not scale with your stats.
Harvest has additional critical strike chance and critical multiplier, but a percentage of your current health is also consumed on use.
Minions have increased critical strike chance if you have landed a critical strike with Harvest recently (past 4 seconds).
Harvest hits instantly kill enemies that are below a health threshold (a percentage of their maximum health), and Harvest deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

When Harvest kills an enemy or hits a boss or rare enemy, you have a chance to summon a {Volatile Zombie}, consuming a percentage of its mana cost. This effect can occur a limited number of times per 3 seconds.
Harvest hits leave a trace on enemies, causing enemies to take increased damage and be prioritized by your minions. Traces last for 5 seconds.
{Blood Specters} summoned by Harvest have a significantly increased critical strike chance.
{Blood Specters} summoned by Harvest leech a signficant portion of the damage they deal as health.
{Blood Specters} summoned by Harvest deal additional melee necrotic damage.
When Harvest hits a target afflicted by Spirit Plague, it applies Withering and has a chance to spread Spirit Plague to an additional target.
Harvest hits deal more damage against {Cursed} enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).
When you directly use Harvest and hit at least one enemy, gain a percentage of your missing health as ward.
Harvest has a chance to summon a {Blood Specter} when it kills an enemy. You cannot summon more than one Blood Specter every 2 seconds.

Harvest's base necrotic damage is converted to physical. Consequently this damage scales with increases to physical damage, but not increases to necrotic damage.

Harvest now gains bleed chance per point of intelligence. Harvest's added damage per dexterity scaling is now added physical melee damage.

Swaps Harvest's {Necrotic} tag for a {Physical} tag.

Harvest's base necrotic damage is converted to cold. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not increases to necrotic damage.

Harvest now gains frostbite chance per point of intelligence. Harvest gains added cold melee damage per dexterity instead of added necrotic melee damage. Bleed chance from all sources is converted to frostbite chance.

Swaps Harvest's {Necrotic} tag for a {Cold} tag.
Harvest has increased attack speed, and is increased further if dual wielding.
Healing Hands now hits enemies within its area of effect, dealing spell fire damage. Added spell damage applies to it at 200% effectiveness.
Healing hands deals additional spell damage based on your Increased Healing Effectiveness and on Increased Healing Effectiveness from this tree.

Healing Hands {Immobilizes} void enemies for 1 second on hit and deals more damage to them (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Healing Hands grants allies 10% less void damage taken (multiplicative with other modifiers) for 10 seconds when cast on them. This buff does not stack.

Healing Hands fears Undead Enemies for 2 to 3 seconds on hit and deals more damage to them (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Healing Hands grants allies 10% less necrotic damage taken (multiplicative with other modifiers) for 10 seconds when cast on them. This buff does not stack.
Healing hands deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) based on the combined levels of your specialised Buff skills.
Healing hands has a chance to {Electrify} on hit.

Healing Hands deals more lightning damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers) and when you hit an electrified enemy with Healing Hands it spreads all stacks of your stacks of electrify from that enemy to a number of other enemies within 5 metres.

Healing Hands is converted into a melee attack that hits in a wide arc in front of you. It now scales with melee damage instead of spell damage and effects on this tree that granted spell damage now grant melee damage instead.

Healing Hands still heals you, as well as allies within its area of affect.
Healing Hands has increased healing effectiveness. This bonus is doubled if there is a dead player within 30 meters.
Healing Hands has an increased area of effect and a lower mana cost.
Healing Hands has a lower mana cost.

You cast {Divine Bolt} at nearby enemies when you directly cast healing hands and heal yourself or an ally.

Divine Bolt is affected by specific passives from the Paladin tree and if you take this node then Divine Bolt from all sources scales with nodes on this tree.

Healing Hands also grants ward to allies based on your Attunement.

If the ally has an effect that causes them to gain ward per second based on their missing health, then it will not heal them, but will provide a percentage of its healing as ward.

If Healing Hands would not affect any allied players at the chosen target location, it is cast centered on you instead.

Healing Hands has increased healing effectiveness.
Healing Hands also grants ward to allies based on your Increased Healing Effectiveness and on Increased Healing Effectiveness from this tree.
Healing Hands becomes a channeled ability, casting 4 times per second regardless of cast speed, and draining your mana.

Healing Hands also grants ward per second for three seconds after being cast on an ally.

The ward per second is equal to a proportion of the ward granted up front by the other nodes on this tree and by items that cause you to grant ward with Healing Hands.

This effect cannot stack multiple times, casting again on the same ally just refreshes the duration.
Healing Hands restores additional health up front.
Healing Hands restores additional health over time. This lasts for three seconds just like Healing Hands' inherent healing over time effect.

When you directly use a melee attack and hit at least one enemy, you have a chance to cast Healing Hands around the target. This consumes mana based on Healing Hands's mana cost.

Healing Hands restores less up front health when cast indirectly.
When you directly cast Smite, you have a chance to cast Healing Hands around the target location. This consumes 70% of its mana cost.

When you directly use Healing Hands you dash to the target location first. However, Healing Hands now has a cooldown and counts a {Traversal} skill.

This does not affect indirect casts of Healing Hands.
