A portion of Healing Hands' remaining cooldown is recovered if you use it to heal an ally other than yourself. This effect can occur once per direct cast of Healing Hands.
When you cast Healing Hands, there is a chance it spawns a {Health Globe} at a random point inside the area of effect. When an ally walks over the globe, it is consumed, healing them for 70 health.
You are immune to damage while moving with Healing Hands.
Healing Hands takes longer to cast, but deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has increased healing effectiveness and a larger area of effect.

If you heal an allied player other than yourself that was below half prior to healing, Healing Hands restores them to full health and grants them ward equal to 80% of their max health.

This has no effect on you, nor on minions, nor players that have an effect that causes them to gain ward per second based on their missing health.

While channelling Healing Hands you also call down a beam from the sky that deals fire damage over time to enemies around the target area and heals allies within its area of effect.

Healing Hands costs additional mana per second while channelling.
You leech a portion of Heartseeker's damage and it has an additional chance to {Recurve}.
Heartseeker has additional critical strike chance and its critical strikes deal more damage.
Heartseeker deals more bow damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) after each recurve, up to a maximum.
Heartseeker's hits have a chance to apply frailty and shred enemy armor, but it no longer seeks new targets when the first target dies.
When Heartseeker's target dies, you shoot {Puncture} at a nearby enemy for each {Recurve} it would have triggered after the target died.
Heartseeker has a chance on {Recurve} to grant you a {Dusk Shroud}, up to a maximum every 2 seconds.
When you directly use Heartseeker and hit at least one enemy, it has a chance to consume one of your {Shadows} to use Heartseeker at the same target.

After a Heartseeker arrow {Recurves} at least 3 times, it has a chance to leave a {Black Arrow} between you and the target enemy if the enemy is at least 6 meters away.

This effect can occur a limited number of times every 3 seconds.
Heartseeker's hits cannot be dodged and it deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Heartseeker's base physical damage is converted to cold and it now has a base freeze rate. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not increases to physical damage.

Bleed chance on hit from all sources is converted to frostbite chance for Heartseeker.

Swaps Heartseeker's {Physical} tag for a {Cold} tag.
Heartseeker's hits have a higher chance to freeze and have a chance to chill enemies.
Heartseeker penetrate additional cold resistance scaling with your cold overcapped resistance.

Heartseeker's base physical damage is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to physical damage. 

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to ignite chance.

Swaps Heartseeker's {Physical} tag for a {Fire} tag.
Heartseeker has a chance to grant you a {Burning Dagger} on {Recurve} consuming mana.
Consecutive {Recurves} each grant a stack of {Dragonfang} for 10 seconds. Higher stacks can only be achieved by a higher amount of consecutive {Recurves}, up to a maximum of 20.

Directly using Heartseeker and hitting at least one enemy grants you frenzy for a short duration. 

This effect has a 10 second cooldown.
When directly used, Heartseeker arrows have a chance to grant mana and health on hit.
Heartseeker has increased attack speed and costs less mana.
Heartseeker's hits have a chance to poison enemies and poison it inflicts last longer.
Heartseeker's hits have a chance to cause enemies to bleed and bleed it inflicts penetrate enemy physical resistance.
Heartseeker deals more ailment damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) after each recurve, up to a maximum.
Heartseeker has a chance to grant you {Crimson Shroud} on {Recurve}.
Heartseeker has an additional chance to {Recurve}.
Heartseeker has an additional chance to {Recurve}, and it deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but it now has a cooldown.
Heartseeker has additional charges.
Your Heartseeker restores a charge whenever you pick up a {Black Arrow}.
Heartseeker now has a minimum chance to {Recurve}.
Heartseeker has an additional chance to {Recurve} scaling with Dexterity.
Whenever Heartseeker {Recurves}, it extends the duration of your most recent {Hail of Arrows} by a small amount, up to 10 times per use of {Hail of Arrows}.
Heartseeker now twists around in a semi-cirle to {Recurve} and its hits have a chance to slow enemies.
Armor shred chance from all sources is converted to poison chance.
If you are the one to get the final hit to unleash {Flame Burst} on an enemy, it deals significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Holy Aura grants you and your allies increased attack and cast speed.
Holy Aura grants you and your allies increased critical strike chance.
Holy Aura causes you and your allies' critical strikes to deal more damage.
Holy Aura grants you and your allies increased fire and lightning damage.
Holy Aura grants you and your allies a chance to ignite and electrify enemies.
Holy Aura causes you and your allies to penetrate enemy fire and lightning resistance.
Holy Aura grants you and your allies increased physical damage.
Holy Aura grants you and your allies increased throwing attack damage and increased stun chance with throwing attacks.
Holy Aura grants you and your allies additional elemental resistance and endurance threshold.
Holy Aura grants you and your allies additional dodge chance.
Holy Aura grants you and your allies increased healing effectiveness.
Holy Aura grants you and your allies increasing throwing attack speed and a chance to gain {Haste} for 1 second on hit.
Holy Aura grants you and your allies increased health regeneration.
Holy Aura regenerates ward for you and nearby allies.
Holy Aura grants you and your allies increased ward retention.
Holy Aura grants you and your allies additional block effectiveness and health when blocking attacks.
Holy Aura grants you and your allies ward when blocking attacks.
When an enemy dies in Holy Aura's area of effect, there is a chance it spawns a {Health Globe}, healing for 70 health.

Your melee hits apply 10 stacks of {Fiery Inquisition} and the melee hits of other allies affected by the aura apply 1 stack. If an enemy reaches 60 stacks, the stacks are consumed to release a {Flame Burst}, dealing fire damage to surrounding enemies.

While Holy Aura is active twice as many stacks are applied.

The {Flame Burst} scales with the stats of the player or minion that triggers it.
Holy Aura's active duration lasts longer, but Holy Aura's cooldown lasts longer and its passive is disabled during its cooldown.
Activating Holy Aura grants you a chance to slow surrounding enemies each second.
Holy Aura grants you and your allies a burst of ward when activated, but Holy Aura has a longer cooldown.

Holy Aura grants you and your allies additional poison resistance.

When Shields heal allies, they also grant them a non stacking increased block effectiveness buff for 4 seconds.
While using {Warpath}, your Shields spin faster and take less damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Shields have increased healing effectiveness.
Ring of Shields costs less mana, but Shields have reduced health.
You are healed any time one of your Shields dies.
Shields shatter into {Shrapnel} when they die, dealing physical damage to surrounding enemies.
{Shrapnel} created by your dying Shields has a signficantly increased chance to stun enemies.
You have additional chance to block and a higher block effectiveness if you have cast Ring of Shields recently (last 4 seconds).
You are invulnerable for 1 second after casting Ring of Shields, but you cannot move or use abilities.
Shields have a chance to slow enemies that hit them.
Shields have increased health and additional physical resistance.
Ring of Shields lasts longer.

Shields take less fire damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), and have a chance to ignite enemies that hit them. 

If you have the {Collateral Damage} node the base physical damage of the {Shrapnel} it creates is converted to fire damage.
Shields deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and shields rotate faster.
You deal additional fire damage with spells and attacks if you have cast Ring of Shields recently (last 4 seconds).
Shields rotate around you at a greater distance.
Ring of Shields summons additional Shields, but costs more mana.
You deal increased melee fire damage if you have cast Ring of Shields recently (last 4 seconds).
While using {Shield Rush}, your Shields form a wall in front of you, increasing the area you hit.
Shields push enemies away from you every 4 seconds.
Consuming a potion also heals your Shields.
Shields reflect a percentage of damage taken to attackers.
Shields now emanate {Forge Flames}, dealing fire damage over time to surrounding enemies.
Ring of Shields is cast at your target location instead, but Shields no longer follow you. Shields have more health (multiplicative with other modifiers) and last much longer.
Rips blood out of a target enemy dealing physical damage to it. A {Blood Orb} is drawn back to you which restores 10 health when it reaches you.
{Blood Splatter} deals damage in a larger area.
Rip Blood deals more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers).
{Blood Splatter} grants your minions {Blood Infusion} when it hits them. The {Blood Infusion} buff grants 15% more damage for 4 seconds (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Blood Splatter has increased area for each minion you currently control, up to a maximum benefit.
{Blood Infusion} grants increased attack and cast speed.
{Blood Infusion} causes your minions to leech a portion of the physical damage they deal as health.
Rip Blood has increased cast speed.
Rip Blood costs less mana and has increased cast speed.
When you are out of mana Rip Blood is free.
{Blood Orbs} grant additional health and the {Blood Orb} returns faster.
{Blood Orbs} restore more health based on your Intelligence stat.
{Blood Orbs} now also grant you mana when you directly cast Rip Blood. The amount of mana is tripled for orbs from bosses and rare enemies.
Rip Blood has additional critical strike chance..
When you directly use Rip Blood and hit a boss or rare enemy or hit at least 3 enemies, you cast Marrow Shards in the same direction.
When you directly cast Rip Blood and hit at least one enemy you gain additional spell damage for 6 seconds. The amount of spell damage gained is doubled for Rip Blood. This effect can stack.
Rip Blood deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Rip Blood deals more damage for each minion you currently control (multiplicative with other modifiers), up to a maximum benefit.

{Blood Orbs} now grant ward instead of health, equal to half of the health they would have restored.

Being at or close to full health does not affect the amount of the ward the {Blood Orbs} grant.

Rip Blood hits have a chance to create a {Blood Splatter} at the target, dealing damage to other enemies around it that were not hit by Rip Blood. This effect can occur a limited number of times per cast of Rip Blood.
When you directly cast Rip Blood and hit a boss or rare enemy it attaches a {Blood Tether}, dealing physical spell damage over time.
Rip Blood can be cast on your minions, but no longer can be cast on enemies. {Blood Orbs} restore significantly more health.

When you directly cast Rip Blood and it kills at least one enemy, it will recast from that position towards another enemy, consuming Rip Blood's mana cost. These recasts can also trigger this effect.

Rip Blood no longer creates {Blood Orbs}.

Rip Blood and {Blood Splatter's} base physical damage are converted to necrotic damage. Consequently this damage scales with increases to necrotic damage, but not increases to physical damage.

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to Damned chance.
Rip Blood has increased cast speed leaves a stench on the target enemy that causes your minions to prioritise attacking it.

When a blood orb grants health, you gain a stack of {Coagulated Blood}. Upon reaching at least 6 stacks, your next cast of Rip Blood consumes 6 stacks to form a {Coagulated Blood Orb} that seeks out a nearby enemy, dealing spell physical damage in a small area.

Additionally, Blood Tethers such as from {Blood Bond} grant 3 stacks each second.
The {Coagulated Blood Orb} deals more damage in a larger area (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Rip Blood hits have a chance to cause enemies to bleed..
Rive deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has an increased chance to stun enemies.
When Rive kills an enemy or hits a boss or rare enemy, you have a chance to summon a {Forged Weapon}.

Rive's base physical damage is converted to void. Consequently this damage scales with increases to void damage, but not increases to physical damage or other damage types.

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to chance to inflict {Time Rot}.

Swaps Rive's {Physical} tag for a {Void} tag. 

Additionally, your chance to {Echo} with Rive is doubled.
Rive's third strike deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and restores mana if it hits at least one target.
Rive deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers). This effect is tripled for {Echoes}.
Rive's first strike has a chance to ignite enemies.
Rive's second strike has a chance to ignite enemies.

When you trigger {Flame Drinker's Blade's} effect you also gain an additional buff that causes you to leech a percentage of the damage you deal as health. Unlike the buff from Flame Drinker's Blade, this buff cannot stack.
Rive hits deal more damage to ignited targets (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Rive's first and second strike have increased attack speed.
Improves Rive's base critical strike chance. This bonus is doubled for Rive's third strike.
Rive's first strike grants you increased critical strike chance on hit for 4 seconds. Stacks up to 4 times.
Rive's third strike has double crit chance and its critical strikes deal more damage, but strikes one and two can no longer critically strike.
Rive's first and second strike have a chance to reduce enemy armor.
Changes Rive's sequence of strikes to {1, 2, 1, 2, 3.}
Rive's third strike deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and hits in a larger area, and has increased chance to stun enemies.
Rive's second strike deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area.
Rive's third strike deals damage in a larger area.
Rive's third strike leeches damage dealt as health.
Rive's third strike deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Rive's hits have a chance to cause enemies to bleed. 

Additionally, bleeds inflicted by Rive's third strike last longer.

Rive's third strike {lacerates} enemies on it.

Lacerated enemies take melee damage over time for 3 seconds, with base damage equal to 300% of your added melee damage. Laceration does not stack.

Rive deals additional melee physical damage per point of Dexterity if you are using a Sword.

Rive deals additional melee void damage per point of Vitality if you are using an Axe.

Rive deals additional melee fire damage per point of Attunement if you are using a Mace.
Rive's third strike consumes all stacks of ignite you applied to enemies it hits, granting you a buff that grants physical penetration per stack consumed, up to a maximum.
Rive's first strike consumes mana to release a wave of energy that passes through enemies.
Rive skips its third strike.

If you have positive mana, Rive's second strike pulls enemies.

Additionally, Rive's hits slow enemies.
Rive's third strike instantly kills enemies below a health threshold (a percentage of their maximum health).
If you are using a two handed weapon, stats on that weapon that directly add damage, increase damage or add a chance to apply an ailment on hit, have double effect for Rive.
Whenever you would summon a {Forged Weapon} from any source, you instead gain a stack of Scrap Metal. Each stack grants more armour and more damage with Rive (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Runebolt has increased cast speed and it has a chance to grant you the {Haste} buff for a short duration.
Runebolt deals more cold damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

The {Lightning Bolt's} base lightning damage is converted to cold. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not increases to lightning damage.

Shock chance from all sources is converted to {Chill} for Runebolt and effects related to shock now depend on chill instead.

Swaps the {Lightning Bolt's} {Lightning} tag for a {Cold} tag.

The {Fire Bolt's} base fire damage is converted to cold. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not increases to fire damage.

Ignite chance from all sources is converted to {Chill} for Runebolt and effects related to ignite now depend on chill instead.

Swaps the {Fire Bolt's} {Fire} tag for a {Cold} tag.
Runebolt deals more lightning damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

The {Cold Bolt's} base cold damage is converted to lightning. Consequently this damage scales with increases to lightning damage, but not increases to cold damage.

Chill chance from all sources is converted to {Shock} for Runebolt and effects related to chill now depend on shock instead.

Swaps the {Cold Bolt's} {Cold} tag for a {Lightning} tag.

The {Fire Bolt's} base fire damage is converted to lightning. Consequently this damage scales with increases to lightning damage, but not increases to fire damage.

Ignite chance from all sources is converted to {Shock} for Runebolt and effects related to ignite now depend on shock instead.

Swaps the {Fire Bolt's} {Fire} tag for a {Lightning} tag.
Runebolt deals more fire damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

The {Lightning Bolt's} base lightning damage is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to lightning damage.

Shock chance from all sources is converted to ignite for Runebolt and effects related to shock now depend on ignite instead.

Swaps the {Lightning Bolt's} {Lightning} tag for a {Fire} tag.

The {Cold Bolt's} base cold damage is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to cold damage.

{Frostbite} chance from all sources is converted to ignite for Runebolt and effects related to {Frostbite} now depend on ignite instead.

Swaps the {Cold Bolt's} {Cold} tag for a {Fire} tag.

Runebolt now randomly selects the element with each cast rather than by combo order. 

Directly casting Rune Bolt now has a chance to double cast if the previous cast was a different type. The chance is based on the amount of {Glyph of Chaos} you have in your crafting inventory.
Runebolt has a chance to shoot additional projectiles in a volley in front of you.
Runebolt's chance to shoot extra projectiles from the {Arcane Overcharge} node and the amount of projectiles are doubled while standing in a {Glyph of Dominion}.
Runebolt now shoots in a cone and the projectiles seek out enemies.
Directly casting Runebolt and hitting at least one enemy grants you health if it was a {Fire Bolt}, ward if it was a {Lightning Bolt} and mana if it was a {Cold Bolt}.

If your last three direct casts were all Runebolt, then any further cast of it becomes tri-elemental (fire, lightning and cold). Tri-elemental Runebolt deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and applies all the effects of the {Attuned Recovery} node.

Once you directly cast another skill, Runebolt loses all of these effects.
When Runebolt passes through a {Frost Wall} it is intensified dealing more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and gains increased projectile speed.
Runebolt hits have a chance to shred enemy armor.
Runebolt deals additional physical damage per point of strength of you or your nearby ally with the highest amount of strength.

Directly casting Runebolt now creates a {Runestone} in front of you if you do not currently have one nearby instead of casting a Runebolt. If you have a nearby {Runestone}, directly using Runebolt now casts two bolts from the {Runestone} towards the target.  

After several seconds {Runestone} consumes mana to explode.
Every second while channeling a skill, your nearest {Runestone} has a chance to cast Runebolt towards your target.
Runebolt now explodes when it reaches its target location or when hitting enemies or obstacles, dealing damage of the corresponding element in an area.
The explosion from {Devastator} deals damage in a larger area per uncapped resistance of the corresponding element.
Directly casting Runebolt and hitting at least one enemy has a chance to grant you mana.
Casting Runebolt and hitting at least one enemy grants you a stack of {Runeweave} up to a maximum.
Each stack of {Runeweave} also grants you more elemental damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per stack of armor shred on the target up to a maximum.
Each stack of {Runeweave} also grants your {Runic Invocation} additional critical strike chance.

Runebolt gains additional effects for each unique {Rune} you have. Multiple {Runes} of the same type does not stack the effect.

{Rah Rune}: Runebolt has a chance to ignite enemies.
{Gon Rune}: Runebolt has a chance to shock enemies.
{Heo Rune}: Runebolt has a chance to inflict {Frostbite} on enemies.
Runebolt's combo is now cast in a reversed order.
Casting Runebolt now randomizes the runes of your {Runic Invocation}.
Runebolt is now cast in a sequence with the same order of your most recent {Runic Invocation}

If you have 3 {Runes} from {Runic Invocation} when you create a {Runestone}, the {Runes} are consumed to instead create an {Imbued Runestone} that fires three bolts. An {Imbued Runestone's} explosion has 100% more area of effect and damage.

This effect has a 20 second cooldown. When you cast {Runic Invocation} the remaining cooldown is reduced by 20% per {Rune} in the {Invocation}.
Runic Invocation deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per {Rune} consumed.

When you directly cast Runic Invocation you leap backwards a short distance, hovering in the air for a moment. In this state you take less damage and directly casting Runic Invocation deals more damage (both multiplicative with other modifiers). 

The effect of {Enigma} no longer works when taking this node.

Runic Invocation gains the {Movement} tag.

When you directly cast Runic Invocation now leap forwards instead of backwards. 

The effect of {Enigma} no longer works when taking this node.

Runic Invocation gains the {Movement} tag.

During the leap you also cast the Invocation corresponding to your first two {Runes}, consuming its mana cost.

Your Invocation must have at least two runes for this effect to occur.

During the leap you also cast the Invocation corresponding to your first {Rune}, consuming its mana cost.

Your Invocation must have at least one rune for this effect to occur.
Runic Invocation grants ward per {Rune} consumed and on direct casts you refund a portion of its mana cost.
Runic Invocation has increased cast speed and when directly casting an {Invocation} you gain ward equal to a portion of your current mana.
When you directly cast Runic Invocation, the next non-channelled elemental spell you cast penetrates enemy elemental resistance and deals damage in a larger area.
When you consume {Runes} with a skill other than Runic Invocation you have a chance to also cast the corresponding {Invocation} for those runes.
{Invocations} that deal damage in an area now deal damage in a larger area.
{Invocations} that include at least one {Rah Rune} have a chance to ignite enemies and deal additional spell fire damage.

Runes now provide you with passive effects while you have them.

{Rah Rune}: Grants increased mana regeneration per {Rune} of this type.
{Gon Rune}: Grants ward per second per {Rune} of this type.
{Heo Rune}: Grants a higher chance to freeze enemies per {Rune} of this type.

Every second you go without casting a Runic Invocation you gain a stack of {Runic Energy} up to a maximum. {Invocations} consume all {Runic Energy} to deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per {Runic Energy}.

When you directly cast Runic Invocation with at least 12 {Runic Energy} you also proc a random equipped non-channeling skill of the same element as each of the {Runes} consumed.

This effect consumes these skills' mana costs and puts them on cooldown (excluding movement skills and skills that are currently on cooldown).

These skills are cast at 0.24 second intervals after the {Invocation}.
Runic Invocation now has a chance to be repeated per stack of {Runic Energy}.
While you have 3 runes and your combination of runes changes without casting an invocation, you gain {Runic Energy}.
Your maximum {Runic Energy} is tripled, but bonuses per {Runic Energy} are halved. When you cast a skill you gain {Runic Energy} per mana spent.
Each unique {Invocation} you directly cast causes {Runic Energy} to accumulate faster until you repeat an {Invocation}.
{Invocations} that include at least one {Heo Rune} have a chance to chill enemies and have a higher chance to freeze them.
Directly casting Runic Invocation now refunds its mana cost and it deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but it now has a cooldown when cast this way.

Whenever you gain a {Rune}, instead of it depending on the type of skill cast, it always results in your sequence of {Runes} mirroring the first three skills on your action bar from left to right.

This effect ignores skills that don't have an elemental tag.
Runic Invocation has a shorter cooldown.
While you have 3 {Runes} and your combination of {Runes} changes without casting an {Invocation}, Runic Invocation's remaining cooldown is reduced.

Whenever you directly cast an {Invocation} you blink to the target location first, but Runic Invocation now has a cooldown. 

{Invocations} cast this way automatically targets a random nearby enemy.

Runic Invocation gains the {Traversal} tag.

After casting Runic Invocation you gain additional spell damage and cast speed for a short duration. 

The buff duration scales with amount of {Runes} consumed.
{Invocations} that include at least one {Gon Rune} have a chance to shock enemies and have a higher chance to critically hit enemies.
Runic Invocation deals more damage per type of {Rune} consumed.
When you cast an {Invocation} using a sequence of three different {Runes} you gain ward per point of intelligence.

Whenever you cast Runic Invocation, you also cast up to one of each type of {Elemental Nova} that you can cast directly. Each unique {Rune} you have when casting Runic Invocation represents different {Elemental Nova}. 

These {Novas} are cast with a 0.3 second interval.

This effect consumes mana equal to {Elemental Nova's} mana cost.

You gain {Runes} faster while channelling.

You gain a burst of ward based on your maximum mana when your number of runes increases from channelling.
Runic Invocation penetrates elemental resistances per point of Intelligence.
Sacrifice deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but Sacrifice costs more mana.
Sacrifice deals significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) if the sacrificed minion has more current health than you.
Sacrifice deals significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) if the minion you sacrifice is your only active minion.
Sacrifice deals additional fire damage.

Sacrifice costs less mana and if you cast Sacrifice while you control at least one minion you gain a buff that grants you more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers). This buff does not stack.
Sacrifice deals more hit damage against bleeding enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Sacrifice deals more damage for each minion you currently control (multiplicative with other modifiers), up to a maximum.
{Blood Spectres} have a chance to cause enemies to bleed. Blood Spectres consume less mana when summoned.
{Blood Spectres} summoned by Sacrifice have more maximum health and deal more damage (both multiplicative with other modifiers).

Sacrificing a minion always creates a {Blood Spectre}.

Blood Spectres summoned by Sacrifice have significantly more maximum health and damage (both multiplicative with other modifiers), and are larger, but consume more mana when summoned.
{Blood Spectres} leech a portion of the damage they deal as health. Sacrifice has a greater chance to summon a Blood Spectre.
{Bone Nova} has increased area of effect.
{Bone Nova} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
{Bone Nova} has a chance to slow enemies hit.
{Bone Nova} hits have a chance to cause enemies to bleed.
You deal increased damage over time if you have cast Sacrifice recently (past 4 seconds). You cannot have multiple instances of this buff at once.
Sacrifice deals damage in a larger area and has an increased chance to stun enemies.
Sacrifice deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area if you have three or more minions active when you cast Sacrifice.

When you sacrifice a minion you have a chance to assemble a {Blood Spectre} from the remains. 15 mana is consumed when you summon a Blood Spectre. 

You cannot summon a Blood Spectre by sacrificing a Blood Spectre or when your mana is negative and below -100.
Sacrificing a minion creates a {Bone Nova} around it, but Sacrifice costs more mana.
Sacrifice is recast onto other minions within its area of effect, which can happen multiple times. Sacrifice costs more mana.
Sacrifice is recast onto other minions within its area of effect up to one additional time. It also has additional Bleed Chance.

If you cast Sacrifice while you control at least one minion all your active {Infernal Shades} have their remaining duration reduced to 2 seconds, but deal significantly more damage for their remaining duration.
Serpent Strike hits have a chance to instantly kill enemies that are below a threshold (a percentage of their maximum health).
Serpent Strike hits have a higher chance to freeze enemies.

Serpent Venom deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) based on your Vitality, and if you have 50 Vitality you regain mana when you inflict an enemy with Serpent Venom or refresh its duration.
Serpent Strike hits have a higher chance to poison on hit and they deal more melee damage per stack of poison on the target, up to a limit.

When you lunge forwards with Serpent Strike, if you have a {Scorpion} it casts its companion ability, {Venom Nova}, twice, once centred on itself, and once centred on you.

This effect has a 12 second cooldown, but ignores Venom Nova's regular cooldown and does not put it on cooldown.

You gain a stack of {Constrictor} every 0.5 seconds, up to a maximum of 4. When you use Serpent Strike all stacks are consumed causing the attack to deal more damage per stack (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Each stack of {Constrictor} also grants Serpent Strike additional poison chance and increased poison duration when consumed
You regain mana when you consume a stack of {Constrictor}.

You gain {Slither} for 4 seconds when you use Serpent Strike and hit at least one enemy.

Each stack grants you more dodge rating (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Serpent Strike hits have a chance to cause enemies to bleed.
Serpent Strike has increased attack speed.
Serpent Strike hits have a chance to shred enemy armor and posion resistance.
You regain health when Serpent Strikes kills or critically strikes an enemy.
Serpent Strike's critical strikes deal more damage.
{Primal Serpents} have increased melee attack speed and are granted health when they hit enemies with a melee attack.
{Primal Serpents} have more health (multiplicative with other modifiers) and additional armour

Instead of summoning a new serpent when the {Primal Serpent} node's effect triggers, you heal your current serpent to full health and it deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) for 10 seconds. This damage buff can stack.
When Serpent Strike kills an enemy or hits a rare or boss enemy, you have a chance to summon a {Primal Serpent}.
Serpent Strike hits have a chance to inflict {Serpent Venom}, an ailment that deals poison damage over time and prevents enemies from landing critical strikes. It lasts 3 seconds and cannot stack.
When you attempt to use Serpent Strike against a target that is slightly out of range you will lunge forwards before attacking.

Serpent Strike now has a chance to freeze and its base physical damage is converted to cold damage. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not increases to physical damage.

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to Frostbite chance.
Frosbite inflicted by Serpent Strike lasts longer and has additional cold penetration
You have additional dodge rating per cold resistance, for each stack of the {Slither} buff.
Each active stack of {Maelstrom} grants Serpent Strike additional chance to inflict bleed on hit and more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Armour Shred inflicted by Serpent Strike has an increased effect based on your Dexterity.
Serpent Venom deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) based on the number of poison and frostbite stacks on them, up to a maximum benefit from each ailment.

Serpent Venom deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) based on your melee critical strike chance.

If your melee crit chance is above 50% then Serpent Venom deals its damage twice as quickly. This does not increase the total damage it deals over its full duration, but rather halves its duration and doubles its damage per second.
Ailments applied by Serpent Strike and {Primal Serpents} have additional poison penetration.

Requires dual wielding.

A circular attack that's also used by your  {Shadows}.
Shadow Cascade deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per active {Shadow}.
You have more dodge rating while directly using Shadow Cascade (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Shadow Cascade has a chance to apply {Frailty} to enemies hit, causing them to deal less damage.

If you hit at least 4 enemies with Shadow Cascade, you deal increased physical damage for a short duration.

This effect can be triggered by daggers from {Dagger Dance} as well.
Shadow Cascade slows enemies for a short duration.
Shadow Cascade has a chance to cause enemies to bleed.
Shadow Cascade deals damage in a larger area.
Shadow Cascade deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per 1% of increased melee attack speed you have.
Shadow Cascade has more melee critical strike chance (multiplicative with other modifiers) against bleeding and poisoned enemies.
Improves Shadow Cascade's base critical strike chance.
Shadow Cascade's critical strikes deal more damage.
You gain mana on use with Shadow Cascade per active {Shadow}.
Shadow Cascade has a chance to shred enemy armor.
Shadow Cascade deals more melee damage to enemies on low health (multiplicative with other modifiers).
When you directly use Shadow Cascade it deals more damage, but it has less attack speed (both multiplicative with other modifiers).
Daggers from {Dagger Dance} last longer causing them to travel further.
Daggers from {Dagger Dance} have a chance to cause enemies to bleed.
{Dagger Dance} throws more daggers which deal significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but Shadow Cascade no longer includes a melee attack.
Daggers from {Dagger Dance} inflict enemies with {Shadow Dagger}. This chance is much lower when {Dagger Dance} is used by a {Shadow}.
Bleed chance from all sources is converted to poison when using Shadow Cascade.
Shadow Cascade has a chance to create a {Shadow} on kill.
A portion of Shadow Cascade's mana cost is refunded if used when you have at least one {Shadow}
Shadow Cascade deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) if you have at least a certain number of {Shadows}.
Shadow Cascade now also throws piercing daggers at nearby targets, dealing throwing physical damage.
Shadow Cascade is now channeled and throws daggers 3 times a second. It rapidly drains your mana.
Shadow Cascade deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per sword or dagger you are wielding.

When you directly use Shadow Cascade while targeting a {Shadow} you swap places with the Shadow.

This effect consumes mana based on how many times you have swapped places with a Shadow in the past 5 seconds.
Shadow Rend deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Shadow Rend deals more damage, but has less attack speed (both multiplicative with other modifiers).
Shadows consumed by {Shadow Massacre} have a chance to manifest a new shadow afterwards, based on their distance from you. Maximum effect is reached for shadows that are at least 6 meters away.
Each shadow consumed by {Shadow Massacre} or {Hidden Strength} restores health based on your dexterity.
You have a chance to gain a stack of {Dusk Shroud} when you directly use Shadow Rend and hit at least one enemy with the shadow's attack.
The manifested shadow chooses a random enemy to dash toward, ignoring your targeted direction. It can target enemies that are further away.
For every {Dusk Shroud} on you up to 20, the shadow's attack deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and grants ward if it hits at least one enemy.
Shadow Rend has additional chance to inflict bleed on hit, doubled for the shadow's attack.
Shadow Rend has additional critical strike chance.
Shadow Rend has additional chance to shock enemies on hit, and it has additional critical strike multiplier per shock on the enemy.
Shadow Rend has additional critical strike multiplier, and additional chance to blind enemies on hit.
The shadow's attack has a chance to inflict Shadow Daggers on hit.
Traversal skills consume all stacks of {Ghost Blades} over the course of their duration.
Shadow Rend has additional physical penetration per unique negative ailment on the enemy, up to 5.
While you are inside your {Smoke Bomb}, Shadow Rend has increased attack speed and a lower mana cost.
Shadow Rend has increased attack speed and a lower mana cost.

Shadow Rend has increased melee area for the shadow's attack.

The shadow's attack has a chance to shred armor on hit, doubled with a bow.

Directly using Shadow Rend consumes existing shadows to make them imitate Multishot or Shadow Cascade, depending on your equipped weapon type. Each Shadow Cascade or Multishot imitated consumes mana.

This node has no effect if {Hidden Strength} is allocated.
Shadow Rend no longer moves you. The manifested shadow dashes forward 5 meters in the targeted direction before attacking, and will choose a target within range upon arriving.

Directly using Shadow Rend consumes all shadows. It restores mana if you consume at least 1 shadow.

For each shadow consumed after the first, it gains more damage and more melee area (both multiplicative with other modifiers).

Shadow Rend is now a traversal skill. You dash up to 7 meters forward after manifesting the shadow.

Shadow Rend deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has a cooldown.
Shadow Rend's base physical damage is converted to lightning damage. Consequently this damage scales with increases to lightning damage, but not increases to physical damage.

Directly using Shadow Rend and hitting at least one enemy with the shadow's attack grants stacks of {Ghost Blades}, up to 9. Stacks of Ghost Blades are consumed 3 times per second while moving to throw a Ghost Blade at a nearby enemy.

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to poison chance for Shadow Rend.

Shadow Rend deals more hit damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per poison on the enemy, up to 30.
Shadow Rend deals more bow damage to damaged enemies. When used with a bow, Shadow Rend moves you forwards instead of backwards.
Shadow Rend has an additional charge.

Enemies you inflict with Spirit Plague are also inflicted with Frailty.
Spirit Plague is cast in a larger area.
Spirit Plague deals significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but no longer spreads.
Enemies you inflict with Spirit Plague are also inflicted with poison for the same duration.
Spirit Plague has a chance to summon a {Poison Wisp} when it kills an enemy, a minion which lasts for 15 seconds and poisons nearby enemies.
Spirit Plague costs less mana and lasts longer.
Spirit Plague has increased cast speed and lasts longer.
Enemies afflicted by Spirit Plague are also feared.
You gain increased global damage over time when you afflict an enemy with Spirit Plague. This effect lasts 4 seconds and can stack up to 13 times.
{Plague} inflicted by Spirit Plague has additional Poison Penetration.
You gain increased poison damage when you afflict an enemy with Spirit Plague. This effect lasts 4 seconds and can stack up to 13 times.
Spirit Plague deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Spirit Plague deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but spreads in a smaller area.
Spirit Plague spreads to additional targets, but costs more mana.
Enemies you inflict with Spirit Plague are also inflicted with bleeding for the same duration.
Bleed inflicted by Spirit Plague has additional Bleed Penetration, but you are now also inflicted with bleeding.
You gain mana when Spirit Plague kills an enemy.
You have a chance to apply bleed when you hit an enemy afflicted by Spirit Plague.
Spirit Plague is now cast in an area around the target, but costs more mana.
Spirit Plague spreads after a duration. (This does not affect the maximum number of spreads.)
Spirit Plague is now a combo skill. Directly recasting Spirit Plague within 2 seconds will cause up to 3 inflicted enemies to spread Spirit Plague to other enemies around them.
Enemies you inflict with Spirit Plague are also inflicted by {Plague} for the same duration. {Plague} is an ailment that deals poison damage over time, spreads between enemies, and does not stack.
Spirit Plague has additional spell necrotic damage for each point of Intelligence
{Vines} deal more damage and have more health (both multiplicative with other modifiers).
You now have a chance to summon a new Vine next to a nearby enemy when a {Vine} dies. Vines have increased attack and cast speed.

After using Summon Vine, Human Form is now replaced with {Infestation}, which converts your 2 nearest {Vines} into {Hives} and transforms you to {Swarmblade Form}.

This requires you to have Swarmblade Form unlocked.

Your {Vines} have a chance to grant you {Rage} on hit. Melee hits have a higher chance to grant Rage than spell hits.

If you have the {Great Vine} node the rage gained is 200% higher.

You can now only summon 1 {Vine} at a time up to a maximum of 6, but they have significantly more health, size, threat generation and their melee attacks deal significantly more damage in a larger area (all multiplicative with other modifiers).

Indirect casts only create a vine on every third cast.

You now have a chance to also cast Thorn Shield on the nearest {Vine} when you cast Thorn Shield. Other allies with Thorn Shield deal global increased physical damage and have a chance to cause enemies to bleed.
{Thorn Burst} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area.
{Thorn Burst} and Spirit Thorn hits have a chance to cause enemies to bleed.
You now also have a chance to cast {Maelstrom} when you directly cast Thorn Shield.
You have additional armor and increased armor while in Spriggan Form.
Thorn Shield lasts longer.
Thorn Shield grants additional armor and increased armor.
Thorn Shield has a chance to recast itself on the same target when it expires.
Thorn Shield reflects additional flat damage to attackers.

After directly casting Thorn Shield, Human Form is now replaced with {Bark Skin}, which adds 4 seconds to the duration of every Thorn Shield on you and transforms you to {Werebear Form}.

This requires you to have Werebear Form unlocked.
You gain ward equal to your armor when you enter Spriggan Form.

You summon an additional {Spriggan} when you enter Spriggan Form which doesn't count towards your Spriggan limit, but does count towards your companion limit.

You will not summon an additional Spriggan if you are already at your maximum companion limit.
Your {Healing Totems} have more health and duration (both multiplicative with other modifiers) and you have increased summoning speed while in Spriggan Form.
Healing Totems directly cast from this tree now also shoot thorns. {Thorn Totem's} skill tree, mana cost, cooldown and totem limit also apply to Healing Totems.
You now always summon 3 Healing Totems and you can have additional maximum Healing Totems.
Healing Totems have increased healing effectiveness.
Healing Totems are converted to Fury Totems and now restore {Rage} instead of healing, but can only affect you.
You now have a chance to cast Spirit Thorns from each of your Healing Totems when you directly cast Spirit Thorns.
Spirit Thorns and Thorn Shield deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
You gain {Rage} and ward when you directly cast Spirit Thorns and hit at least 1 enemy.
Spirit Thorns has a chance to pierce enemies (continue through targets they hit) and they penetrate enemy physical and cold resistance.
You now summon a {Vale Spirit} every 5 direct casts with Spirit Thorns, up to a maximum.
Your Healing Totems have a chance to summon a {Vale Spirit} when they die.

When you directly cast Spirit Thorns while at maximum Vale Spirits, you consume them to cast {Entangling Roots} at the target area and you regain {Rage}, which deals more damage when cast this way (multiplicative with other modifiers).

This consumes Entangling Roots' mana cost.
You have a chance to cast {Maelstrom} on you when you directly cast Spirit Thorns.
Your hits have a chance to poison enemies while in Spriggan Form and your Vines have a chance to poison enemies.

Spirit Thorns, Summon Vines and Thorn Shield's base physical damage is converted to cold. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not increases to physical damage. They also have a chance to freeze enemies.

Poison chance from all sources, for all three skills, is converted to {Frostbite} and effects related to poison now depend on Frostbite instead.

Your {Vines} now cast thorns from range, instead of using a melee attack. This is affected by nodes on this tree that affects Spirit Thorns, but they only fire 1 projectile and {Great Vine} fires 3 projectiles.
Thorn Shield can now only target you and can no longer attach to other allies.
You gain charges faster while moving.
Enemies that hit you are shocked while you have more than 80 charges.
You have a higher maximum amount of charges.
You gain {Haste} for 1 second per 50 charges when you discharge.
Discharge has a higher range the more charges you have.
Discharge’s critical strikes deal more damage if you have over 100 charges when you discharge.
Discharge deals additional spell lightning damage (doubled if you have at least 80 charges).
Improves Discharge’s base critical strike chance.
Discharge hits an additional enemy for every 50 charges you have when you discharge.
When you directly cast Static, Discharge deals additional lightning damage per charge, but Discharge costs more mana per charge.
You gain more charges when you are hit.
Discharge grants ward proportional to your charges, but has a longer cooldown.
Discharge hits more enemies.
{Lightning Blast} critical hits have a chance to grant a charge.
Lightning Blasts from {Overload} have additional critical strike chance and a chance to chain additional times.
Discharge shocks enemies on hit. Each enemy is inflicted with 1 stack of shock for each 50 charges expended, up to a maximum.
After discharging, your next spell and melee attack deal additional lightning damage.
Discharge now has a chance to stun enemies, with a higher chance based the number of charges you have.
Nearby enemies are knocked back when you discharge.
Your next melee attack grants charges on hit. This effect has a 1 second cooldown.
You gain charges while moving much faster, but charges decay at your movement charge rate if you stop moving for more than 1 second.
Discharge has a significantly higher range, but can only hit enemies within a 90 degree arc.
You gain charges for each enemy you critically strike if you have discharged in the last 3 seconds.
Static's cooldown resets when you reach your maximum number of charges.
While over 80 charges, you expend 10 charges to cast {Lightning Blast} at an enemy within 8 meters every second. This effect consumes Lightning Blast's mana cost.
{Lightning Blasts} from {Overload} consume less of Lightning Blast's mana cost, but expends additional charges.
Static Orb hits has a chance to shock enemies.
Static Orb deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per 25 maximum mana, but Static Orb costs additional mana equal to a portion of your maximum mana, up to a maximum.
The explosion now leaves behind {Charged Ground} dealing lightning damage over time in an area. Creating another Charged Ground halves the remaining duration of all existing ones.
{Charged Ground} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per additonal critical strike multiplier.
You and allies have additonal shock chance and increased shock effectiveness while inside {Charged Ground's} area.
Static Orb costs less mana, but it no longer pulls enemies.

Static Orb costs significantly less mana, but it now has a chance to not explode.

If you {Scatter Blast} then you only have a chance to create small orbs, and if you have {Orbital Fulmination} all static orbs orbit you for a reduced duration.

If Static Orb does not explode it returns to you and deals damage on the way back, but it costs more mana.

This node has no effect if you have {Orbital Fulmination}.

Static Orb's base lightning damage is converted to cold and it gains a base freeze rate, but it no longer pulls enemies. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not increases to lightning damage.

Shock chance from all sources is converted to {Frostbite} and effects related to shock now depend on Frostbite instead.
The orb and the explosion have a higher chance to freeze enemies.
The orb and the explosion have a higher chance to freeze enemies, but the explosion no longer creates Tendrils.

The orb now orbits you for up to 3 seconds before exploding, but you are limited to a few orbs and casting more will detonate older ones.

Static Orb gains damage and explosion area with time rather than travel distance, but scales up to the same maximum as before.
Static Orb deals more spell damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to shocked enemies.
Static Orb generates {Static Charges} on hit.
The explosion has a higher chance to stun, increased stun duration and you gain ward on stun with the explosion.
Static Orb's critical strikes deal more damage.
The explosion creates additional {Tendrils} to hit enemies outside the explosion area.

Static Orb grants you {Lightning Aegis} for a short duration on cast, which grants 50% increased lightning damage and 25% less damage taken. Lightning Aegis causes an explosion of lightning damage when you lose 25% of your health or after 10 seconds.
Static Orb deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area, but automatically explodes after 4 meters.
The explosion's damage ramps up more with distance, but it travels slower.
Static Orb deals more damage and now travel faster and in an arcing trajectory rather than a straight line, but it no longer pulls or hits enemies until it explodes.
After using Static Orb the next {Lightning Blasts} cast from any source chain 2 additonal times.

For 4 seconds after using Static Orb directly casting {Lightning Blast} and hitting at least one enemy will return 25% of Static Orb's mana cost. 

This effect can only occur a limited number of times.
Static Orb travels faster and deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Static Orb explodes on hit rather than piercing enemies. The orb deals more hit damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per 1 mana cost.
You have a chance to cast an additional Static Orb towards the target when it hits a rare enemy or boss. This effect has a short cooldown.
Casting Static Orb sends out smaller orbs in a cone in front of you. These small orbs can hit the same target as the large orb, but cannot explode and do not pull.
Your bear deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has a chance to cause enemies to bleed.
{Thorn Burst} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has a chance to inflict bleed on hit.
Your bear also releases a {Thorn Burst} when hit, up to a limited number of times per 4 seconds.
{Thorn Burst} has a chance to shred armor on hit.
Your bear's {Swipe} has a shorter cooldown.
Your bear deals more damage with Swipe (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has increased area with Swipe.
Your bear has more health and takes less damage at low health (both multiplicative with other modifiers).
Your bear has additional critical strike chance.
Your bear takes less damage from critical strikes and its own critical strikes deal more damage.
Your bear has additional armor based on your strength.
Your bear's basic attack releases a {Thorn Burst}, a spell that deals physical damage around the bear.

Your bear has more health regeneration (multiplicative with other modifiers).

While you have an active bear companion, you have more health regeneration (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Your bear can use {Swipe} with a 2 second cooldown. Your Bear's Swipe scales with its own stats, but is affected by your Swipe Tree.

Your bear deals additional melee physical damage per 3 points of your Strength.
Your bear can use {Earthquake}, with a 6 second cooldown. When cast this way, Earthquake uses your bear's stats but your Earthquake tree. Earthquakes cast by your bear deal less damage.
{Thorn Burst} has a chance to poison enemies on hit. Additionally, your bear deals more poison damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Your bear has increased movement speed and deals more melee damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per stack of bleed on the target (up to 30).
Your bear's abilities have increased area of effect.
Your bear gains an additional charge for Earthquake. When you directly cast {Warcry} or enter {Werebear Form}, your bear restores a charge for Earthquake.

The base physical damage of your bear's melee attack is converted to lightning, and it has a {Lightning} tag instead of {Physical}.

Your bear shocks enemies on hit.

Your bear gains the ability to teleport towards enemies, on an 8 second cooldown.
Your bear has increased attack speed.
Your bear has increased movement speed and cannot be stunned.
Your bear has a chance to cast a {Thorn Burst} from each enemy hit by its {Earthquake}. This chance is tripled against bosses and rare enemies.
Your bear's {Roar} has increased area and healing effectiveness.
Your bear's {Roar} also grants itself and your minions less damage taken for 6 seconds.
Your bear's {Roar} inflicts Frailty on enemies.
Your bear's {Roar} also grants you a percentage of damage taken redirected to your highest health minion for 6 seconds.
Your Golem has increased attack speed, cast speed, and movement speed.
Your Golem has a chance to ignite enemies, additional fire resistance, and deals more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Your Golem deals additional fire damage with spells and melee attacks.
Your Golem deals more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers), and {Pyre Golems}’ {Infernal Aura} deals damage in a larger area.
Your Golem causes all nearby minions to move faster, including themselves. This bonus is doubled for the Golems themselves.
Your Golem has a chance to grant you a stack of {Putrid Coating} when it is hit. Each stack lasts for 8 seconds and grants you a chance to poison enemies.
Your Golem deals additional melee cold damage and it has a chance to chill attackers when hit.
Your Golem deals more damage and has more health and armor (each multiplicative with other modifiers).
Your Golem deals more damage with melee attacks (multiplicative with other modifers), and has an increased chance to stun with melee attacks.
Your Golem is larger (multiplicative with other modifiers), has increased area of effect, and generates more threat, which increases the likelihood of enemies targeting it.

Your Golems have increased size and deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) if you have no other living golems when you summon them.

Your Golems have significantly higher armor for the first 15 seconds of their life when summoned near the corpse of another Golem.
Your Golem grants additional armor to nearby minions, including itself.
Your Golem's hits restore health to you and itself. This effect restores additional health per 2 Vitality.
Your Golem deals more spell damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), and has an increased chance to stun.
Your Golem has a higher chance to retaliate with {Bone Shatter} when hit and its Bone Shatter deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Your Golem has additional health regeneration based on your total amount of health regeneration.

Your {Skeletons} which die within 18 meters of your Bone Golem grant it more armor and melee damage (both multiplicative with other modifiers) for 10 seconds. This effect can occur a limited number of times per 10 seconds.
If you only control one Golem, it has a much higher chance to retaliate with {Bone Shatter} when damaged by hits from allies.
You summon an additional Golem, but each Golem has reduced maximum health, is smaller, and deals less damage (all multiplicative with other modifiers).
Your Golems deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers). If Bone Golems have been replaced by another type of Golem, you now have an equal chance to summon a Bone Golem.

You now summon a {Pyre Golem} instead of a Bone Golem.

The base physical damage of Pyre Golems' melee attacks is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to minion fire damage, but not increases to minion physical damage.

Pyre Golems also cast {Infernal Aura}, a spell that deals a burst of fire damage on cast and fire damage over time afterwards. Infernal Aura also ignites nearby enemies every second.

You now summon a {Deathchill Golem} instead of a Bone Golem. 

The base physical damage of Deathchill Golems' melee attacks is converted to cold. Consequently this damage scales with increases to minion cold damage, but not increases to minion physical damage. Death Chill Golems' melee attacks gain a Freeze Rate equal to twice their base damage.

Deathchill Golem also casts Freezing Grasp, a spell that deals cold damage and has high freeze rate. Bosses cannot be frozen by this.

You now summon a {Blood Golem} instead of a Bone Golem. 

Blood Golems can also cast {Sanguine Orb}, a spell that deals physical damage to nearby enemies over 2 seconds.
Your Golem's {Bone Shatter} has a chance to repeat from enemies hit by it. This effect can occur a limited number of times per second.
Your Golem's {Freezing Grasp} spell now has additional spell damage and base freeze rate per point of Vitality.

Your {Skeletons} and {Skeletal Mages} which die within 18 meters of your Bone Golem grant it more fire damage over time to ignited enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers) for 10 seconds. This effect can occur a limited number of times per 10 seconds.
Frenzy Totem has more health (multiplicative with other modifiers) and lasts longer.
Frenzy Totem affects allies or enemies in a larger area.
While in range of your Frenzy Totem, you have a chance to cast {Eterra's Blessing} on a random ally each second.
Frenzy Totem stores a portion of the damage it receives, releasing it as damage dealt to enemies after taking a percent of its maximum health in damage.
Frenzy Totem also stores a percentage of the damage taken by you.
Frenzy Totem has a chance to cast {Ice Thorns} when hit by enemies.

Frenzy Totem is no longer summoned at your cursor; instead it is always placed 1 meter in the direction you're facing.

Frenzy Totem costs less mana and grants additional melee damage.
Frenzy Totem's tether has a higher range.
You have additional critical strike multiplier while tethered.
Allies affected by Frenzy Totem are granted increased health regeneration.
When you summon Frenzy Totem, you and your companions are granted {Haste} for a duration (doubled for companions).
Companions affected by Frenzy Totem have higher physical and elemental resistance.
While affected by Frenzy Totem, you revive downed companions faster.
Allies affected by Frenzy Totem are granted a chance to poison enemies.
Frenzy Totem's Frenzy buff is more effective.
When you summon Frenzy Totem you gain a temporary buff that grants you more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and increased melee attack speed, but also increases the damage you take.
When you summon Frenzy Totem, your companions are granted additional melee physical damage for 4 seconds.
While Frenzy Totem is active, your Wolves' howls also grant increased critical strike chance to you and your allies.
While Frenzy Totem is active, your Scorpion's {Venom Nova} hits in a larger area.

Frenzy Totem is now {Sloth Totem,} which will now slow and reduce the armor of enemies instead of granting Frenzy.

Increased effect of frenzy is converted to increased effect of armor shred.
You are tethered to the totem while you remain within 5 meters of it, and while tethered you deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Frenzy Totem's buffs are more effective, but Frenzy Totem now affects only companions.
Whenever you revive a downed companion, Frenzy Totem casts {Healing Nova.}
Summoning Frenzy Totem refreshes companion ability cooldowns.
While Frenzy Totem is active, your Sabertooth's {Flurry Swipes} casts an additional time when used.
Your raptor has additional elemental resistance and have additional dodge chance.
{Screech}'s cooldown is reduced, and it causes enemies it hits to {Bleed}.
Your raptor automatically reduces the cooldown of your other companions' activatable abilities by 3 seconds. This effect has an 8 second cooldown.
Your raptor deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has increased area of effect for its abilities.
{Fire Breath}'s cooldown is reduced, and it deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Your raptor deals more melee damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
When a bleeding enemy dies within 12 meters, your raptor has a chance per stack of bleed on that enemy to gain Haste for 3 seconds.
Your raptor deals more melee damage per bleed on the enemy (multiplicative with other modifiers), up to a maximum.
Your raptor's melee attacks have a chance to cause enemies to bleed.
Your raptor deals more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Your raptor's attacks have a chance to inflict blind, poison, and slow on hit.
Your raptor deals more damage for each percent of its missing health (multiplicative with other modifiers), up to 50% missing health.
Your raptor gains increased effect from {Rampage}.
Your raptor has additional critical strike chance.
{Rampage}'s duration is increased.
{Rampage} costs less mana.
While your raptor is {Rampaging}, its hits instantly kill enemies below a health threshold (a percentage of their maximum health).
Your raptor gains the ability to {Screech} at enemies, dealing damage and shredding their physical resistance. Screech has an 8 second cooldown.

Your raptor becomes a {Dragon Raptor}. The base physical damage of your raptor's melee attacks are converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not with increases to physical damage.

Your raptor gains the {Fire Breath} ability, dealing spell fire damage over time with an 8 second cooldown.

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to ignite chance for your raptor, and all effects in the tree related to bleed now depend on ignite.
Your raptor has increased attack and cast speed. This effect is doubled if it has killed an enemy in the past 4 seconds.
When your raptor hits an enemy, bleeds on that enemy have a 25% chance to double in speed.
Your raptor has increased melee attack speed, but loses 5% of its current health with each attack while above 50% of maximum health.
Your raptor uses {Rampage} if it encounters a boss or rare enemy, if it is able to. This sets Rampage to 50% of its normal cooldown.
{Rampage} also grants your raptor less damage taken for the duration.
Your raptor has increased cooldown recovery speed for its abilities.
{Rampage} also resets the cooldown of {Fire Breath}.
Your raptor has additional critical strike multiplier, and its attacks have a chance to shred armor on hit.
Your sabertooth has more health (multiplicative with other modifiers) and recovers a percentage of its maximum health when it leaps.
Your sabertooth has additional cold resistance and it has a chance to chill attackers that hit it. It also has more health (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Your sabertooth has increased attack speed. Additionally, it deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) with {Flurry Swipes}.
Your sabertooth has more dodge rating (multiplicative with other modifiers), and it gains additional dodge rating for each point of Dexterity you have.
Your sabertooth deals more melee damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and its melee attacks have increased area.
Your sabertooth has additional critical strike chance.
Your sabertooth has additional critical strike multiplier.
Your sabertooth has increased attack speed.
While your sabertooth is on low health it deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and leeches a portion of the melee damage it deals as health.
Your sabertooth deals more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Your sabertooth deals additional cold damage with spells and melee attacks.
Your sabertooth has additional Freeze Rate Multiplier and it has more Freeze Rate per stack of Chill on the target (multiplicative with other modifiers)
Your sabertooth deals additional cold melee damage based on your dexterity.
All physical damage dealt to your sabertooth is converted to cold damage. Consequently it is reduced by cold resistance, but not by physical resistance.
When your sabertooth directly uses a melee attack and hits at least one target it has a chance to use {Flurry Swipes}.
Your sabertooth gains Frenzy for 2 seconds after using {Flurry Swipes}.

Your sabertooth becomes an {Ice Tiger}, converting 100% of its base melee physical damage to cold. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not with increases to physical damage. The ice tiger's melee attacks have a freeze rate equal to twice their base damage.

All bleed chance is converted to frostbite chance for your ice tiger.

The ice tiger's melee attacks' {Physical} tags are swapped for {Cold} tags.
Your sabertooth deals more melee damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per stack of chill on the target.
Your sabertooth deals more melee damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has an increased chance to stun enemies, but your Sabertooth can no longer leap.

Your sabertooth's attacks which hit at least one enemy have a chance to trigger {Upheaval}. Upheaval cast in this way uses your sabertooth's stats, but your Upheaval tree.

This chance is halved if Upheaval creates multiple fault lines.
When your sabertooth leaps, it uses {Upheaval} when it lands
When your sabertooth leaps you gain {Haste} for a duration.
Your sabertooth's hits now have a chance to summon an {Ancestral Sabertooth} that attacks nearby enemies for a short duration. This effect can trigger a limited number of times per 5 seconds.
Your sabertooth's hits have a higher chance to summon {Ancestral Sabertooths} and they deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Your sabertooth's {Flurry Swipes} inflict Bleed on hit and if you or your Sabertoth are below half health it always crits.
Your ancestral sabertooths have increased duration per point of your attunement. Additionally, your sabertooth gains attunement scaling equivalent to its strength scaling.
Your scorpion has more health (multiplicative with other modifiers), additional physical and poison resistance, and increased size.
Your scorpion's attacks grant health on hit, and have a higher chance to poison on hit.
Your Scorpion deals more hit damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per stack of Poison on the target (up to 30).
Your Scorpion's melee attacks have increased area of effect, and your Scorpion has increased attack speed.
Your Scorpion has a chance to cause enemies to bleed, and it deals more damage over time (multiplicative with other modifiers)
{Baby Scorpions} deal more damage (damage is multiplicative with other modifiers) and their attacks have a chance to poison enemies.
Activating your Scorpion's ability summons additional {Baby Scorpions.}
{Baby Scorpions} have more dodge rating (multiplicative with other modifiers).
{Baby Scorpions} have increased attack and movement speed.
Your Scorpion has additional elemental resistance, and its abilities have increased area of effect.
Your Scorpion deals more cold damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has a higher chance to freeze enemies.
Your scorpion has a chance to cast {Maelstrom} whenever you do. Maelstroms cast by your scorpion in this way use your Scorpion's stats, but your Maelstrom tree.
Your Scorpion has a chance to {Shock} on hit and deals more lightning damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per stack of shock on the target.
Your Scorpion deals more poison damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Your scorpion's {Venom Nova} deals damage in a larger area, and deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
{Venom Nova} leaves behind a {Poison Pool} for 4 seconds, which applies a stack of poison each second to enemies standing in the pool.
Your Scorpion has a chance when it is hit to create a {Poison Pool}. This effect has a 10 second cooldown.
{Poison Pools} grant you and your minions increased attack speed and cast speed and a chance to poison enemies.
Your Scorpion deals additional melee damage, and its Poison Chance from all sources is converted to Armour Shred Chance.
Your Scorpion spawns a number of {Baby Scorpions} every 5 seconds. Baby Scorpions have an unlimited duration but can be killed.

Your scorpion takes less cold damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

The base physical damage of your scorpion's melee attacks is converted to cold. Consequently this damage scales with increases to minion cold damage, but not with increases to minion physical damage. These melee attacks now have a {Cold} tag instead of a {Physical} tag.

Your scorpion's melee attacks also gain a chance to freeze, with a freeze rate equal to twice their base damage.
Your scorpion has a chance to inflict {Frostbite} on hit and its Bleed Chance from all sources is converted to Frostbite Chance.

Your Scorpion's activated ability is now {Shock Nova}, a spell that deals lightning damage and applies 3 stacks of {Shock} on hit.

The base physical damage of your scorpion's melee attacks is converted to lightning. Consequently this damage scales with increases to lightning damage, but not increases to physical damage. These melee attacks now have a {Lightning} tag instead of a {Physical} tag.

All sources of poison chance are converted to shock chance for your scorpion, and effects related to poison in this tree instead depend on shock.

Your scorpion has a chance on melee hit to cause a large {Avalanche} boulder to land on the target. The Avalanche boulder uses your scorpion's stats, but your Avalanche tree.

This effect can occur a limited number of times per 2 seconds.

Your {Storm Bolts} will strike your scorpion to grant it a stack of {Charged}, up to 20.

Your scorpion's {Shock Nova} consumes all stacks of Charged to deal more damage per stack.
Your scorpion has additional critical strike chance, and whenever it kills an enemy it restores a percentage of its maximum health.
Skeletal Mages deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Skeletal Mages can cast {Profane Oblation} on your melee Wraiths and Blood Spectres, sacrificing them to deal necrotic damage to surrounding enemies.
Skeletal Mages can cast {Profane Oblation} on your Skeleton Warriors, Skeleton Rogues, and Skeleton Vanguards, sacrificing them to deal necrotic damage to surrounding enemies.
Removes regular Skeletal Mages and Pyromancers from the pool of Skeletal Mages that can be summoned, and your Skeletal Mages deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

You and your {Locusts} have a higher chance to dodge and additional armor while in Swarmblade Form.

You gain {Rage} when you dodge.

A random active companion uses its active ability after you directly use Swarm Strike.

Your {Locust Swarm} deals more damage per {Locust} consumed (multiplicative with other modifiers) and while the Locust Swarm is active you have additional chance to poison on hit per {Locust} consumed.

You and your {Locusts} have improved base critical strike chance while in Swarmblade Form.
You regain {Rage} when you or your {Locusts} critically hit.

You regain health and {Rage} when you or your {Locusts} kill an enemy or when you hit a rare enemy or boss.

While you have {Aspect of the Panther} you have additional critical strike multiplier per Stack. This bonus is doubled if you are dual wielding.
Swipe hits have a chance to cause enemies to bleed.

When you attack with Swipe and hit at least one enemy you gain health if you are transformed, or mana if you are not transformed.

Swipe hits instantly kill enemies that are below a health threshold (a percentage of their maximum health). 

If you have a similar effect from an item or passive node then the highest threshold is used.

Swipe deals more Lightning Damage per stack of Shock on the target and more Cold Damage per stack of Chill on the target.
Swipe deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area.
When you use Swipe and hit at least one enemy your nearest companion uses its companion ability and is restored to full health.
Swipe hits deal more damage against slowed enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers)
Swipe deals significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and slows enemies, but has a cooldown.

Swipe deals more lightning damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Swipe gains a {Lightning} tag.
You have a chance to summon a {Claw Totem} when Swipe kills an enemy or hits a rare or boss enemy.
When you attack with Swipe and hit at least one enemy you have a chance to call forth a {Spirit Wolf} from each of your summoned wolves.

When you use Swipe and hit at least one enemy, all your totems gain +1 Melee Damage and +1 Spell Damage. This buff stacks and lasts until the totems expire.
