Vengeance' base physical damage is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to physical damage or other damage types.

Chance to shred physical resistance from all sources is converted to chance to shred fire resistance.

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to ignite.

Swaps Vengeance' {Physical} tag for a {Fire} tag.

{Iron Blades} and {Riposte} gains different benefits depending on your equipped weapons.

Mace: Riposte deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Axe: Riposte instantly kill enemies that are below a health threshold (a percentage of their maximum health).
Sword: Improves {Iron Blades'} base critical strike chance.
Spear: {Iron Blades} have increased bleed duration.

Vengeance' base physical damage is converted to void. Consequently this damage scales with increases to void damage, but not increases to physical damage or other damage types.

Chance to shred physical resistance from all sources is converted to chance to shred void resistance.

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to chance to inflict {Time Rot} for Vengeance.

Swaps Vengeance' {Physical} tag for a {Void} tag.
Vengeance hits have a chance to cause enemies to bleed.

Vengeance hits have a chance to inflict {Time Rot}.

Additionally, Vengeance has additional chance to Echo if it can Echo.

Vengeance deals more void damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and additional melee void damage.

Additionally, Echoed Vengeance cannot be dodged.
Vengeance critical strikes deal more damage.

Vengeance has a chance to grant you {Frenzy} for 2 seconds when hitting ignited enemies.

Additionally, Vengeance deals more fire hit damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per ignite on the enemy, up to a maximum.

Void Cleave hits ignite enemies and half of its base damage is converted to fire, meaning that this half of the damage scales with increases to fire damage but not with increases to void damage.

Ignite inflicted by Void Cleave gains additional fire penetration based on your increased void damage.

Void Cleave gains the {fire} tag.

All of Void Cleave's base void damage is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to void damage.

Additionally your next non-channeled fire attack or spell after Void Cleave deals significantly more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

Void resistance shred chance from all sources is converted to fire resistance shred and effects related to void resistance shred now depend on fire resistance shred instead.

Void Cleave loses the {void} tag.
When you use Void Cleave and hit at least one enemy, your minions gain a stack of {Molten Infusion} for 4 seconds, granting them additional melee fire damage and a chance to ignite enemies.
Minions only gain a stack of {Molten Infusion} when you directly hit them with Void Cleave, but {Molten Infusion} has significantly increased effect.
You can travel farther with Void Cleave and it deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
After channelling {Warpath} for at least 2 seconds your next Void Cleave within 1 second deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Void Cleave deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in an increased area.
Improves Void Cleave's base critical strike chance and it cannot be dodged.
Void Cleave deals more melee damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to enemies on high health.
Void Cleave hits apply {Critical Vulnerability} and Void Cleave has increased critical strike chance.
Void Cleave critical strikes deal more damage and you gain mana with Void Cleave critical strikes.
If Void Cleave hits at least one enemy your next {Erasing Strike} is always a critical strike.

Void Cleave deals additional melee void damage. 

This effect is tripled for echoed Void Cleaves.
Void Cleave deals significantly more melee void damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) to enemies on low health.
Void Cleave hits slow enemies, have increased stun chance and increased stun duration.
Void Cleave has a shorter cooldown.
Void Cleave hits shred enemy void resistance.

You cast {Abyssal Echoes} after using Void Cleave.

Abyssal Echoes cast this way uses its mana cost.
Void Cleave hits inflict enemies with {Time Rot}.
When you use Void Cleave you gain {Ravaging Aura} for a short duration scaling with the amount of enemies hit up to 10.
{Ravaging Aura} deals additional spell void damage and has an increased area of effect.
{Ravaging Aura} leeches a portion of the damage it deals as health.
If Void Cleave hits at least one enemy your next throwing attack inflicts enemies with {Time Rot}.
Void Cleave deals more melee damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per stack of {Time Rot} on the target.
Void Cleave hits shred enemy armor and {Armor Shred} applied by Void Cleave has increased effect and duration.
If Void Cleave hits at least one enemy your next throwing attack deals additional void damage.
You can store an additional charge of Void Cleave, but Void Cleave costs more mana.
Void Cleave deals more melee damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per stack of bleed up to a maximum.
Your increases to physical damage also apply to fire damage for Void Cleave.
You can travel much further with Void Cleave (multiplicative with other modifiers), but it has a longer cooldown and now counts as a {Traversal} skill.
Volatile Reversal has a shorter cooldown.
Volatile Reversal's forwards jump has a significantly shorter maximum distance, but its cooldown is reduced.
Volatile Reversal has additional critical strike chance.
When you critically strike at least one enemy with Volatile Reversal you regain mana.
You gain the {Haste} buff for a short duration, when you jump backwards in time.
While you are able to jump backwards in time, a percentage of your armor mitigation also applies to damage over time.
You gain the {Frenzy} buff, while you are able to jump backwards in time.
You can use Volatile Reversal's second combo ability for a longer period of time and it deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

When you jump forwards in time, your existing {Devouring Orbs} become older.

This causes them to expire sooner.
When you jump backwards in time, {Devouring Orb} recovers some of its cooldown scaling with the amount of {Devouring Orbs} that expired after jumping forwards in time.
When you jump forwards in time, all your {Devouring Orbs} are pulled towards you and trigger a {Void Rift}, but Volatile Reversal costs additional mana.

When you jump backwards in time, your existing {Devouring Orbs} become younger.

This causes them to expire later, and each orb can only benefit from this once.
Volatile Reversal's second combo ability is removed, but the melee attack deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and Volatile Reversal deals damage in a larger area.
Volatile Reversal deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Volatile Reversal's second combo ability now jumps you forward in time to your target location, instead of backwards.
Volatile Reversal deals damage in a larger area.

Volatile Reversal's base void damage is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to void damage.

Time Rot chance from this tree is converted to ignite chance.

Swaps Volatile Reversal's {Void} tag for a {Fire} tag.

When you jump forwards in time, your arrival location is slightly randomized, but the melee attack deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and Volatile Reversal deals damage in a larger area.
Volatile Reversal's hits have a chance to slow enemies and it deals more hit damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per stack of slow on the enemy.
When you travel at least 4 meters by jumping through time, your next direct melee attack or spell is guaranteed to {Echo}, if it is able to.
When you jump backwards in time, Volatile Reversal deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) per meter travelled, up to a maximum.
Volatile Reversal's melee hits have a chance to grant you a {Void Essence}.
When you directly use Volatile Reversal you now also cast a {Void Bolt} at up to 3 enemies near your starting location, but Volatile Reversal costs additional mana.
Volatile Reversal's {Void Bolts} have a chance to inflict time rot.
Enemies hit by Volatile Reversal's {Void Bolts} take increased damage over time for several seconds. This effect does not stack.

Your {Void Bolts} return to you.

All returning Void Bolts can hit the same targets again. (This means a single cast of the Void Bolt spell can hit a single enemy up to 4 times)
When you directly use Volatile Reversal you now also cast a {Void Bolt} at up to 3 enemies near your arrival location, but Volatile Reversal costs additional mana.

The {Void Bolts} from the {Terminal Void Bolt} node are now replaced with a single cast of {Abyssal Echoes}.

This consumes mana equal to a portion of {Abyssal Echoes'} mana cost.
{Shrapnel}, {Explosive Orb} and {Fire Glyph} have a chance to chill enemies and a chance to inflict frostbite on hit.
{Shrapnels} hits deal more damage against chilled enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).
{Shrapnel} travels faster, causing it to also travel further. Shrapnel is created more frequently.

Volcanic Orb core and {Shrapnel} deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but Volcanic Orb's duration is reduced. 

If you have taken the {Explosive Ground} node, Volcanic Orb leaves Explosive Ground more frequently.
You have increased cast Speed with Volcanic Orb, and Volcanic Orb travels faster, causing it to also travel further.
{Shrapnel} has an increased chance to stun enemies and a chance to ignite on hit.
{Shrapnel} deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
{Shrapnel} pierces enemies (continues through enemies it hits).
Volcanic Orb leaves {Explosive Ground} more frequently, but costs more mana.
Volcanic Orb costs less mana, but moves slower and has a reduced duration.
Explosions from {Explosive Orb} and {Glyph of Fire} deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Explosions from {Explosive Orb} and {Glyph of Fire} have a chance to ignite enemies.
Volcanic Orb's core deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Volcanic Orb's core, {Shrapnel} and {Explosive Orb} hits deal more damage, but Volcanic Orb costs more mana and has less cast speed (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Volcanic Orb's core and {Shrapnel} deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but Shrapnel is created less frequently.
Frozen Orb has an increased chance to freeze enemies.
Volcanic Orb moves slower for each second it exists.
Volcanic Orb's core deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), and it accelerates, moving faster for each second it exists.
Volcanic Orb has a shorter cooldown.
Volcanic Orb costs less mana.
Volcanic Orb fires additional {Shrapnel} from its final explosion.

Volcanic Orb is converted into Frozen Orb. Base fire damage is converted to cold for Frozen Orb and the skill effects it creates. Consequently this damage scales with increases to cold damage, but not with increases to fire damage.

Ignite chance from all sources is converted to frostbite chance for Volcanic Orb.
Volcanic Orb leaves {Explosive Ground} as it travels, which explode after 3 seconds, dealing fire damage to enemies within its area of effect. Volcanic Orb costs significantly more mana.
Volcanic Orb creates a large explosion at the end of its duration, dealing fire damage.
Casting Volcanic Orb creates a {Fire Glyph} at your feet which explodes after 1.3 seconds, creating a {Fire Nova}.
Volcanic Orb's core hits deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but Volcanic Orb no longer creates {Shrapnel} when it ends.
Wandering Spirits reveals Spirits more frequently.
Spirits deal more damage when they pass through enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers), but move slower.
Spirits are revealed in a larger radius around you.
Spirits are revealed in a smaller radius around the player and deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Spirits last longer when they are revealed.
Wandering Spirits shred the necrotic resistance of enemies near them.
Spirits can Fear enemies in a larger radius. If spirits inflict {Necrotic Resistance Shred} or {Damned} those ailments are also inflicted in a larger radius.
Spirits deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers), but the fear they apply to enemies via the {Terrifying Presence} node has a shorter duration.
Fear applied to enemies via the {Terrifying Presence} node lasts longer, but affects a smaller area.
Spirits deal more damage to feared or possessed enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Wandering Spirits has increased cast speed and costs less mana.
Wandering Spirits continues to reveal Spirits for a longer duration, but has a longer cooldown.

Each Spirit is larger and deals damage in a larger area.

If you have {Terrifying Presence}, the area for inflicting fear is also increased.
You gain Ward whenever a Spirit expires.
You have a chance to gain mana whenever a Spirit expires.
Spirits move faster and deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Spirits deal more damage to poisoned enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Spirits cast {Spectral Putrescence} at enemies from further away.
Spirits can cast {Spectral Putrescence} more frequently.
The base necrotic damage dealt by Wandering Spirits is converted to poison damage. Consequently this damage scales with increases to poison damage, but not increases to necrotic damage.
Enemies near Spirits are {Feared} for 0.5 to 0.9 seconds, causing them to flee from the spirit that feared them.
Spirits have a chance to cast {Spectral Putrescence} at nearby enemies each second. Spectral Putrescence is a projectile with 100% chance to poison on hit.
Spirits are now revealed around a target location, rather than around you. Spirits deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Spirits deal more damage to cursed enemies and damned enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Wandering Spirits is now a channelled skill, has a much shorter cooldown, and creates Spirits much more frequently. You can regenerate mana while channelling this skill.
Wandering Spirits has a shorter cooldown
Wandering Spirits inflict enemies around them with {Damned}.
Unleash a mighty roar that stuns and knocks back nearby enemies.
Warcry affects a larger area.
You and allies affected by Warcry gain increased crit chance for 4 seconds.
You and allies affected by Warcry deal increased cold damage for 4 seconds.
You and your cold minions affected by Warcry gain increased crit chance and additional chill chance for 4 seconds.
Warcry inflicts stacks of {Frostbite} to affected enemies.
Enemies frozen by Warcry take increased damage from hits.
Warcry applies one stack of {Frailty} to affected enemies for a short duration.

Warcry now also restores your health. 

The amount of healing is increased by healing effectiveness and is doubled if used while on low health.
Warcry now cleanses negative ailments on you.

Warcry now also restores ally health for the same amount as from {Breath of Eterra}. 

The amount of healing is increased by healing effectiveness and is doubled if used while on low health.
If Warcry hits at least one enemy, you gain {Haste} for 1 second.
Warcry costs less mana and {Haste} gained from {Hastily Advance} lasts longer.
You gain {Frenzy} for the same duration as you have Haste from {Hastily Advance}.
Fear inflicted by Warcry lasts longer and each stack of the {Berserk} buff from the Berserker node now also causes you to deal more damage to Feared enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers).
{Berserk} has increased duration and additional maximum stacks.
{Berserk} now also grants you increased attack speed per stack of Berserk up to a maximum.
Warcry knocks enemies further back.
Warcry now hits enemies and deals spell physical damage.
Warcry causes enemies to bleed.
Warcry now has a cooldown of 5 seconds which cannot be reduced by stunning or freezing enemies, but Warcry no longer has a guaranteed stun, freeze, or fear.

While Warcry is on cooldown, your critical strikes have a chance to use Warcry for you. 

This effect can only happen once per direct Warcry use, and it will reset the cooldown.

Warcry now freezes enemies instead of stunning them, but it no longer knocks enemies back.

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to {Frostbite} chance and effects related to bleed now depend on Frostbite instead.

You now cast {Maelstrom} when you use Warcry. 

Maelstrom cast this way costs less mana.
You are invulnerable for 1 second after using Warcry.

You gain the {Berserk} buff for 1.5 seconds when you use Warcry, granting you additional melee damage.

While you have Berserk, you gain additional stacks of Berserk on melee hit up to a maximum, and melee hits refresh all stacks.
{Berserk} now also affects your Raptor and Bear.
Warcry now fears enemies for 2 seconds instead of stunning them.
You now place down a totem for 3 seconds that repeatedly uses Warcry instead of you, but Warcry costs more mana.
Warcry now pulls enemies instead of knocking them back.
Warcry now also taunts affected enemies for a short duration.

Warpath deals additional melee void damage. 

This effect is doubled while wielding a 2 handed weapon.

Warpath's base physical damage is converted to void. Consequently this damage scales with increases to void damage, but not increases to physical damage.

Warpath has a chance to inflict {Time Rot}. Bleed and ignite chance from all sources are converted to Time Rot chance for Warpath.

Swaps Warpath's {Physical} tag for a {Void} tag.
Warpath hits deal more damage against enemies per stack of {Time Rot} on them (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Warpath deals damage in a larger area.
While spinning, you have increased movement speed.

Warpath deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).

This effect is doubled while using a two handed weapon or dual wielding.
Warpath costs less mana per second.
Warpath hits shred enemy armor.
Warpath costs significantly less mana per second, but you have reduced elemental resistance while spinning.
While spinning, you have additional block chance and block effectiveness.

While spinning, you have additional melee leech while wielding an axe, additional base crit chance while wielding a sword, additional physical penetration while wielding a mace and additional fire penetration while wielding a sceptre.
Warpath deals damage in a larger area while wielding a two handed weapon.
While spinning, you gain mana every three seconds while wielding a two handed weapon.

Warpath's base physical damage is converted to fire. Consequently this damage scales with increases to fire damage, but not increases to physical damage.

Bleed chance from all sources is converted to ignite for Warpath.

Swaps Warpath's {Physical} tag for a {Fire} tag.
While spinning, you leave behind a burning trail that ignites enemies each second. If ignite has been converted to {Time Rot} with {Apocalypse Whirl} this trail inflicts Time Rot instead.
While spinning, you have additional spell damage.
While spinning, you and your allies are healed each second within Warpath's area of effect. The amount of health gained is increased by healing effectiveness.

While spinning, you have a chance to cast {Smite} each second on a nearby enemy.

This consumes Smite's mana cost.
Warpath hits deal more melee fire damage to ignited enemies (multiplicative with other modifiers) and ignite it inflicts has additional Fire Penetration

For each second you continuously spin, Warpath deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and has increased area, but costs additional mana per second. These effects will stop scaling after a duration.

After spinning for at least 2 seconds you do a {Warslash} when you stop spinning. This deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) in a larger area for each second you spent spinning, up to a maximum duration.
Warpath deals more melee damage to enemies on low health (multiplicative with other modifiers).
While spinning, you summon {Forged Weapons} every 5 seconds as if you had hit an enemy with {Forge Strike}.
While spinning, you summon {Forged Weapons} more frequently.

While spinning, you deal increased void damage and you have a chance each second to create an {Echo} at a recent location that uses Warpath on the spot for 2 seconds. This chance is equal to twice your chance for skills to echo.

You can only create a Warpath echo if you have been spinning for at least 2 seconds and have travelled at least 4 metres in the past 2 seconds.

While spinning, you cast {Abyssal Echoes} every 5 seconds.

This consumes Abyssal Echoes' mana cost.
You have additional endurance and endurance threshold while in Werebear Form.
You regain additional {Rage} if you hit at least one enemy with {Swipe}.

For 6 seconds after you enter Werebear Form, you deal more Lightning Damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and 100% of Swipe's Base Damage is converted to lightning. Consequently this damage scales with lightning damage instead of physical damage.
You gain additional spell damage per point of Strength while in Werebear Form.
Maul and Rampage deal more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers).
Rampage has increased movement speed, a higher chance to stun enemies and stuns inflicted by Rampage last longer.
Rampage deals more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and you have global increased damage while Rampaging.
When you leave Werebear Form you cleanse all negative ailments and gain {Haste} for 3 seconds.
Rampage and Maul hits shred enemy armor.
Maul and Rampage have improve base critical strike chance and you have global increased critical strike chance while in Werebear Form.
You cast {Storm Bolts} from the sky a number of times each second while Rampaging.
You regain health and {Rage} on kill or when you hit a rare or boss enemy.

Your hits have a chance to cause enemies to bleed while in Werebear Form.

This chance is doubled for Maul.
Maul and Roar affects a larger area.
Maul has a shorter cooldown.
When you use Maul and hit at least one enemy, your minions deal increased damage for 8 seconds. This effect can stack.
You regain {Rage} and health with Roar per enemy hit, up to a maximum number of enemies.
Maul deals more melee damage to stunned enemies and you regain {Rage} when you hit a stunned enemy with Maul.
Your {Rage} decays slower while in Werebear Form.

You have a chance to cast {Maelstrom} when you use Swipe or Maul and hit at least one enemy.

This consumes Maelstrom's mana cost as Rage.

After using Rampage, Human Form is now replaced with {Arboreal Protection}, which casts {Thorn Shield} on you and up to 5 nearby allies and transforms you to {Spriggan Form}.

This requires you to have Spriggan Form unlocked.

After using Rampage, you now {Roar}.

This consumes Warcry's mana cost as Rage and puts it on cooldown.

Maul and Rampage's base physical damage is converted to lightning. Consequently this damage scales with increases to lightning damage, but not increases to physical damage.

Armor Shred and bleed chance from all sources is converted to shock and effects related to armor shred or bleed now depend on shock instead for Maul and Rampage.

Your {Fury Leap} tree now applies to Maul. 

This consumes Fury Leap's mana cost as Rage.

After using Maul, you now use {Upheaval} towards your target.

This consumes Upheaval's mana cost.

After directly using Roar, Human Form is now replaced with {Swarm Call}, which summons a {Locust} for each nearby stunned enemy up to 6 and transforms you to {Swarmblade Form}.

This requires you to have Swarmblade Form unlocked.

After using Maul, you now cast {Entangling Roots}.

This consumes Entangling Roots' mana cost as Rage.

Rampage has no cooldown.

While using Rampage you move more slowly, but deal more lightning damage. Both of these effects are multiplicative with other modifiers.

skills.description_text: A nova that echoes from up to 8 enemies it reaches and applies abyssal decay. Enemies with abyssal decay take void damage over time for 5 seconds, if they take a hit all the damage from abyssal decay is dealt to them immediately.
Throw a flask of acid that explodes dealing physical damage on impact, poisoning enemies and reducing their armor.
Combo skill that first sends enemies forward in time by 5 seconds. Reactivate the skill to bring those enemies back to your timeline early.
Brings enemies that were sent forward back to your time.

Hold ability key to channel to absorb your Wraiths, Summoned Skeletons, Skeletal Mages, Golems, and Volatile Zombies in an area. When you release, summon a single hulking abomination with added health and more damage per minion absorbed.

The abomination remembers the minions absorbed to create it and will rapidly devour those minions to restore 10% of its maximum health.

Toggles Aura of Decay, which poisons you every second, and leaves a trail of decay. Enemies near you or in the trail are poisoned four times a second, and your chance to poison on hit applies to each of these instances at 100% effectiveness.

While Aura of Decay is active you take 30% less poison damage. Additionally, you take 50% less damage from your own Aura of Decay.
Summon the might of winter and pummel your foes in ice and snow, unleashing a large boulder at the target followed by 10 smaller boulders around the area over 2 seconds.
Summons a ballista that fires bolts at nearby enemies.
Dash to your Banner. Cannot be used without a target.
The thrown blade spins in midair, hitting nearby enemies 3 times per second for 4 seconds.
Apply a curse to enemies in an area for 8 seconds that causes physical damage when hit. The damage is tripled if you inflicted the hit yourself.
A barrage of chaotic projectiles which land in an area around the target. The explosions deal necrotic and fire damage in a small area.

Opens an infernal fissure in the ground, dealing fire damage over time to enemies on top of it, as well as releasing {Spirits} from the fissure that seek nearby enemies. These spirits inflict enemies with {Torment}, a {Curse} that slows and deals necrotic damage over time.

Max 1 active fissure.
Melee or bow combo with three attacks. The first attack also creates a fiery explosion, damaging nearby enemies.

Requires dual wielding.

A series of fast dash attacks. Dancing Strikes' movement does not scale with attack speed, but it gains increased cooldown recovery speed and more damage equal to 50% of your total increased melee attack speed.

Requires dual wielding.

A series of fast dash attacks. Dancing Strikes' movement does not scale with attack speed, but it gains increased cooldown recovery speed and more damage equal to 50% of your total increased melee attack speed.

Requires dual wielding.

A series of fast dash attacks. Dancing Strikes' movement does not scale with attack speed, but it gains increased cooldown recovery speed and more damage equal to 50% of your total increased melee attack speed.

Requires a bow.

Summons 7 Black Arrows that rain from the sky over 4 seconds, granting 100% increased damage to your next bow attack when retrieved.

Toggle to seal your health, preventing it from going above its current value for 3 seconds. While sealed you take less damage equal to half your percent missing health, and 90% of hit damage taken is dealt over 4 seconds. 

Reactivate to unleash a Wave of Death dealing damage based on how long Death Seal has been active and your current percent missing health.
Releases a Wave of Death dealing damage based on how long Death Seal has been actve and your current percent missing health.
Throw a device to create a Decoy that taunts enemies around it for 2.5 seconds before exploding.
Detonate your decoys instantly.

Requires a bow.

Fires an arrow that embeds itself in a target then explodes after 0.6 seconds.

Creates a devouring orb at the target location for 10 seconds that deals void damage over time to nearby enemies, and casts a void rift whenever something dies nearby, hitting nearby enemies to deal void damage.

Each successive rift has 12% more damage and area of effect, capped at an increase of 120%.
Dash a short fixed distance in the target direction dealing damage to all enemies in your path.

Your minion's health is drained for 3% of its max health per second for each second the shade has been active, but it and your minions around it deal +10 necrotic damage with spells and attacks and have 50% increased necrotic damage.
Slam the ground with your weapon damaging enemies in a large area and creating 2 to 4 aftershocks nearby.

Active: Enchant your weapon with elemental power. Your melee attacks deal 50% more elemental damage for 5 seconds.

Passive: Your melee attacks deal 15% more elemental damage.
A melee attack that hits all enemies in a large area in front of you. Enemies killed by the hit are erased from existence, replaced by void rifts.

The nearest allied target is healed for 100 health. If the target is one of your minions then the healing is tripled and the minion is energised for 4 seconds, increasing its size by 20% and its damage by 100%.

Rapidly move a short distance forwards.

Evading does not grant damage immunity or prevent abilities from hitting you.

Evade gains 0.5% Increased Cooldown Recovery Speed per Character level.

Rapidly move a short distance forwards.

Evading does not grant damage immunity or prevent abilities from hitting you.

Evade gains 0.5% Increased Cooldown Recovery Speed per Character level.

Rapidly move a short distance forwards.

Evading does not grant damage immunity or prevent abilities from hitting you.

Evade gains 0.5% Increased Cooldown Recovery Speed per Character level.

Rapidly move a short distance forwards.

Evading does not grant damage immunity or prevent abilities from hitting you.

Evade gains 0.5% Increased Cooldown Recovery Speed per Character level.

Rapidly move a short distance forwards.

Evading does not grant damage immunity or prevent abilities from hitting you.

Evade gains 0.5% Increased Cooldown Recovery Speed per Character level.

Throws a trap that explodes when triggered, dealing throwing fire damage. Traps take 0.4 seconds to arm and explode when enemies walk over them or after 30s. 

You can have a maximum of 6 active traps at a time.
Your Falcon rapidly strike enemies within the target area.

Passive:
Summons a {Falcon} that fights with you.

Active:
{Falcon Strikes}: The {Falcon} rapidly hits many enemies in the target area.
Grants a burst of 400 ward. Then for 3 seconds, hits deal 30% less damage to you and cause a retaliatory burst of flames.
A bow or melee attack that performs 3 rapid strikes. The last strike can be cancelled by moving.

Hold ability key to channel. While channelling you have 100% increased mana regen and gain 10 ward per second. After channelling for exactly one second you recover 10% of missing mana and gain a burst of 40 mana and ward.

A melee attack that hits all enemies in an area in front of you.

25% chance on hit to summon a Forged Weapon which lasts for 20 seconds and is affected by the stats and attack rate of your weapon. You can control up to 6 Forged Weapons at once.
Leap to the target location, dealing damage to nearby enemies when you hit the ground.
Create a burst of fire damage around a minion. Has 400% added damage effectiveness.
Creates three successively larger ice explosions in the target direction. Each explosion deals more damage than the last.

Throws a hammer that returns to you after a short delay.

Added Throwing Damage is applied to each hit at 110% effectiveness.
Heals all allies in a target area for 50 health and applies a lingering warmth which heals 80 health over the next 3 seconds. The lingering warmth cannot stack on the same target.
You and nearby allies passively gain 30% increased damage and +15% additional elemental resistance. Activating Holy Aura doubles the stat bonuses for 4 seconds.
Return back to human form, returning your original stats and abilities.
Return back to human form, returning your original stats and abilities.
Return back to human form, returning your original stats and abilities.
Return back to human form, returning your original stats and abilities.

Calls forth five hungering souls that seeks out enemies, on hit they deal necrotic damage and then possesses the target.

Possessed enemies take necrotic damage over time for 1.5 seconds. Enemies cannot be possessed by multiple souls at once.
Opens a rift that repeatedly shoots a barrage of ice shards in a target direction for 5 seconds.
Toggles a protective shield that chills nearby enemies and grants 4 ward per second, but drains 3 mana per second. Can be cast instantly.

A powerful melee attack that leaves an area of {Consecrated Ground} for 4 seconds.

Allies on the {Consecrated Ground} are healed for 50 health each second.

Enemies on Consecrate Ground take fire damage over time that scales with spell damage. Consecrate Ground gains 1 spell damage per 5% increased healing effectiveness.
Become immune for a duration and rapidly strike nearby targets with 6 mirages.
Dash to a nearby enemy and strike it with your weapon. Cannot be used without a target. Costs more mana the further away the target is.

Manifests an animated set of heavy armor, which attacks slowly.

Stats granted by your body armor, helmet, gloves and boots also apply to your Manifest Armor.
Curses the enemy, reducing all resistances by 25% for 8 seconds. This effect does not stack.
Jump forward a short distance and slam into the ground dealing damage to nearby enemies.
Call a meteor from the sky that deals a large amount of damage upon landing. Added damage is applied at 950% effectiveness.

A melee attack that grants a stack of {Armament} if you hit at least one enemy.

Armament stacks up to 2 times and each stack lasts 3 seconds.

Each stack of {Armament} causes an additional sword to strike a nearby enemy when you use Multistrike.
Leaps backwards and throws a net which snares enemies and deals physical damage. Netted bosses and rare enemies have 35% less movement speed, and other enemies are immobilized.

The companion ability of your Primal Raptor.

Sends your Raptor into a rampage. Your raptor gains 40% more damage (multiplicative with other modifiers) and 40% increased attack speed, cast speed, movement speed, and size for 4 seconds

Take on the mantle of death itself and transform into a Reaper, temporarily gaining the Reap ability. 

While in Reaper Form, your health regen does not apply and your health decays over time. When your health reaches 0 instead of dying you transform back to human form and are healed to full health.

Hold ability key to channel for up to 2 seconds. You take 80% less damage while channelling.

When you stop channelling the barrier breaks dealing physical damage to all nearby enemies. This deals 20 additional physical damage for each hit you received while channelling.

Manifests a ring of shields around you.

Each shield has 75 base health and lasts up to 10 seconds. Shields draw enemy aggression and block projectiles.

Stats on your equipped shield also apply to the shields created by Ring of Shields
Rips blood out of a target enemy dealing physical damage to it. An orb of the blood is drawn back to you, restoring 5 health when it reaches you.

Unleash a thunderous roar which knocks enemies back and stuns them for 1.5 seconds. Rares and bosses are stunned for half as long.. All effects from your Warcry Skill Tree are applied to Roar.

20% of remaining cooldown recovered whenever you stun (or freeze if Warcry is converted to cold) an enemy, including with Roar itself. This effect can occur a maximum of two times each second.

Perform a short dash attack and manifest a shadow at your previous position, which will perform a sweeping melee attack or piercing bow attack for you after a short delay.

The shadow will persist afterwards for up to 5 seconds. Shadows will imitate your next direct use of Shadow Cascade, Shurikens, Umbral Blades, Explosive Trap, or Acid Flask. Shadows can also imitate indirect uses of Shadow Cascade. Up to 3 shadows can be active at once.

A sweeping melee attack of 2 arcs that strikes enemies in a circle around you dealing cold damage and instantly kills frozen enemies below 10% health.

Enemies in the middle of the attack are hit twice, where the arcs overlap.
Dash a short fixed distance in the target direction.

Channel to charge a powerful frontal cone attack, dealing fire and physical damage to all enemies hit. Charging for longer increases its range and power. 2 second maximum charge time.

Your armor is doubled while channelling Smelter's Wrath.
Drop a Smoke Bomb at your feet that blinds enemies and grants you haste while you remain inside it. The Smoke Bomb grows in size over its duration. Lasts 4 seconds.

Freezes enemies in a cone in front of you. Freeze lasts for 2 seconds.

20% of remaining cooldown recovered whenever you freeze an enemy, including with Snap Freeze itself. This effect can occur a maximum of two times each second.

Feasts on the souls of {Cursed} enemies, dealing necrotic damage to them, and drawing fragments of their souls back to you. The soul fragments each grant 3 ward when they reach you.

Feasts on up to 13 enemies, prioritising those closest to you.
Curses a target with a powerful necrotic damage over time effect which lasts 3 seconds. Spreads to a single nearby target on target death.

Transform into a forest spriggan, increasing your spell casting capabilities, and gaining 4 new abilities.

Your mana is replaced with Rage while in spriggan form. When your Rage reaches 0 you automatically transform back into your human form. Your starting Rage is equal to your maximum mana. Rage slowly decays over time at a constant rate.

Casts an orb in a target direction that deals lightning damage and pulls enemies that it hits towards it as it travels. At the target location it explodes, dealing lightning damage to enemies surrounding it and also striking up to 5 enemies further out. The further it travels the higher the damage and area of effect of the explosion, up to 100%.

Summons a primal bear that follows you into combat. When its health drops to 0 it is downed, and you can stand near it to revive it.

The bear counts as a companion. You can have a maximum of two companions at once by default.
Summons a bone golem, which attacks slowly, but has a 15% chance to retaliate with Bone Shatter when hit.
Summons a skeleton mage to assist you. You are limited to 2 mages at a time.
Summons a Skeleton Death Knight to assist you.

Summons a Forged Weapon which lasts for 20 seconds and is affected by the stats and attack rate of your weapon. You can control up to 6 Forged Weapons at once.

Forged Weapons have 220 based health.
Summons a totem that grants Frenzy to all nearby allies. Frenzy increases attack and cast speed by 20%.
Summons a totem that heals nearby allies. Healing totems have 100 base health and last 6 seconds.

Summons a Hive that releases Locusts while enemies are nearby. You can have a maximum of three Hives by default and each Hive contains 12 Locusts.

You are limited to 12 active Locusts and each Locust lasts up to 12 seconds.
Summons a skeleton mage to assist you. You are limited to 2 mages at a time.

Summons a primal raptor that ferociously attacks your enemies. When its health drops to 0 it is downed, and you can stand near it to revive it.

The raptor counts as a companion. You can have a maximum of two companions at once by default.

Summons a primal sabertooth cat that leaps into combat. When its health drops to 0 it is downed, and you can stand near it to revive it.

The sabertooth cat counts as a companion. You can have a maximum of two companions at once by default.

Summons a scorpion that follows you into combat. Its attacks with its tails inflict a long lasting poison. When its health drops to 0 it is downed, and you can stand near it to revive it.

The scorpion counts as a companion. You can have a maximum of two companions at once by default.

Summons a Skeletal Mage to assist you. You are limited to 2 Skeletal Mages at a time.
Skeletal Mages cast Dread Bolt, which has 150% added damage effectiveness and has a 50% chance to apply Damned on hit.
Summons a skeleton warrior or skeleton archer to guard you. You are limited to 3 skeletons at a time.
Summon a primal wolf to guard you and attack your enemies

Summon a spriggan that casts spells to attack your enemies and has a passive Healing Aura that restores 10 health per second. When its health drops to 0 it is downed, and you can stand near it to revive it.

The spriggan counts as a companion. You can have a maximum of two companions at once by default.

Summons a primal wolf that follows you into combat. When its health drops to 0 it is downed, and you can stand near it to revive it.

The wolf counts as a companion. You can have a maximum of two companions at once by default.

Summons Storm Crows up to your companion limit. The Storm Crow casts spells from a distance. When its health drops to 0 it is downed, and you can stand near it to revive it.

The Storm Crow counts as a companion. You can have a maximum of two companions at once by default.

Summons a totem that casts lightning storms around nearby enemies.

Storm totems last 8 seconds, and you cannot control more than 1 at once.
Summons a totem that fires thorns at nearby enemies. You can have a maximum of two thorn totems by default.

Summons 1 poisonous vine.

Vines are melee minions that inflict poison on hit. You can have up to 12 vines.

Summon 3 poisonous vines for 8 seconds.

Each vine has 50 base health.
Summons a Volatile Zombie which trudges towards the nearest enemy. When it reaches an enemy or dies, it explodes dealing physical and fire damage to surrounding enemies.

Summons a primal wolf that follows you into combat. When its health drops to 0 it is downed, and you can stand near it to revive it.

While you have your maximum number of wolves, the ability is replaced with {Howl}.

The wolf counts as a companion. You can have a maximum of two companions at once by default.
Summons a wraith that rapidly fades from this world, but will seek and attack your foes while it remains.
A circular attack which deals damage to all nearby enemies, then gathers nearby locusts to create a Swarm around you which deals damage over time to nearby enemies.

Transform into an agile Swarmblade, increasing your melee capabilities, and gaining 4 new abilities.

Your mana is replaced with Rage while in swarmblade form. When your Rage reaches 0 you automatically transform back into your human form. Your starting Rage is equal to your maximum mana. Rage slowly decays over time at a constant rate.

Every 2 seconds, summons a symbol that orbits you, causing you and your allies' attacks and spells to deal 3 additional fire damage and increasing health regeneration by 20%.

Activate to consume the symbols, granting 100 ward and 5% less damage taken per symbol for 3 seconds.

You can have up to 3 symbols at a time.

Requires a melee weapon.

Jump forward and strike in an area in front of you while shadows appear and simultaneously strike on either side of you.
Teleport to target location.
Teleport to target location.

A melee combo attack that cycles through a cold strike, a physical strike, and a lightning strike that trigger elemental {Tempest} spells of the same damage type if the strikes hit at least one enemy.

Cold strike: Triggers a {Frigid Tempest}, which is a cold projectile that pierces through enemies.
Physical strike: Triggers a {Wind Tempest}, which is a small twister that lasts a short duration dealing physical damage per over time.
Lightning strike: Triggers a {Thunder Tempest}, which is an expanding storm of lightning bolts.

Form a protective barrier of thorns around yourself or an ally that grants 30 armor and reflects 50 damage to attackers. When the shield expires, a burst of thorns shoots out and damages all nearby enemies.

Creates a new body for you at the target location, then detonates your old body to deal physical damage to enemies around it.

Instead of mana, this spell costs 13% of your current health.
Recall all Umbral Blades to you, hitting enemies along the way.

A melee attack that also prepares you to riposte incoming hits.

If vengeance successfully hits an enemy and you take a hit in the next 2 seconds you will riposte, taking 25% less damage and striking at a nearby enemy.

A combo ability that jumps forwards in time to a target location and performs a melee attack on arrival. Recast within 3 seconds to jump backwards in time to your original location and perform a melee attack on arrival.
Casts a fiery orb that spews burning shrapnel in all directions.

Reveals wandering spirits around you for 6 seconds. The spirits wander at random dealing necrotic damage over time to enemies they pass through.

4 spirits are revealed immediately after casting the skill and 17 more are revealed over the next 6 seconds.

The spirits individually last up to 4 seconds, but all remaining spirits fade once the reveal duration ends.

Unleash a mighty roar that knocks back nearby enemies and stuns them for 1.5 seconds. Rares and bosses are stunned for half as long.

20% of remaining cooldown recovered whenever you stun (or freeze if Warcry is converted to cold) an enemy, including with Warcry itself. This effect can occur a maximum of two times each second.
Spin towards the mouse while you hold down the ability key, striking nearby enemies as you move.

Transform into a ferocious werebear, increasing your physical capabilities and gaining 4 new abilities. 

Your mana is replaced with Rage while in werebear form. When your Rage reaches 0 you automatically transform back into your human form. Your starting Rage is equal to your maximum mana. Rage slowly decays over time at a constant rate.
